speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen Voss does not look up from his glass as the stormtroopers enter. His cybernetic eye flickers amber, cycling through data only he can see. He swirls the murky liquid, then sets the glass down with a quiet clink.] Twenty credits says they are here for the Rodian at the end of the bar. [He finally lifts his gaze, scanning the room with the practiced ease of someone who has survived worse places than this.] Or maybe they are here for someone who knows too much.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The cantina door hisses shut behind her. She stands silhouetted for a beat, letting her eyes adjust to the gloom. The scorch mark on her jacket catches the dim light as she moves to the bar, sliding onto a stool two seats down from Kaelen Voss. She gestures to the bartender for something strong, then tilts her head toward the stormtroopers without looking directly at them.] If they were here for the Rodian, they would have grabbed him already. They are waiting. [She finally turns, a faint, humorless smile.] That means they are here for someone specific. Only question is whether they have a name or just a description.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The cantina door slides open again, admitting a draft of cold frontier air. A tall figure in a grey traveler cloak steps through, hood half-lifted — enough to catch the dim light on pale grey eyes that seem to hold their own faint luminescence. She moves past the stormtroopers without looking at them, her stride carrying her to a shadowed corner table where she sets down a small cloth-wrapped bundle. She signals the bartender for water — not ale, not anything that would dull the senses. Then she sits, hands visible on the table, and lets her gaze drift across the room with the patient watchfulness of someone who has learned that trouble announces itself long before it arrives.]
new_scene_name: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, night at edge of a cold draft
new_scene_description: A cold draft slides through the cantina as a hooded figure in a grey cloak appears and moves to a shadowed corner table. The room’s murmur dips; the figure places a bundle on the table, asks for water, and sits with hands visible, eyes scanning the room for trouble.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The cantina door cycles open again, admitting a figure who stops just past the threshold. Vex Nereus stands there a moment, letting his eyes adjust — or perhaps listening to something the others cannot hear. He runs a gloved hand along the frame, feeling the hum of the cooling system through his palm, then moves to an empty seat at the far end of the bar. He does not glance at the stormtroopers. He does not glance away from them either — he simply exists in their periphery, ordering a glass of water in a voice that sounds like it hasnt been used in days.]
new_scene_name: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, night (continuing, Vex Nereus arrives)
new_scene_description: The cantina remains the same physical space. A new figure, Vex Nereus, steps in and settles at the far end of the bar, his presence noted by the periphery to stormtroopers. He orders water, eyes and attention kept on the room and the troopers, while the rest of the scene continues around him.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, night at edge of a cold draft
current_scene_description_before: A cold draft slides through the cantina as a hooded figure in a grey cloak appears and moves to a shadowed corner table. The room’s murmur dips; the figure places a bundle on the table, asks for water, and sits with hands visible, eyes scanning the room for trouble.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not turn her head when the door cycles again. But something in her posture shifts — a barely perceptible tension at the corners of her cloak, a stillness that was not there a moment before. Her fingers pause over the datapad. She lifts her water glass, takes a slow drink, and sets it down with deliberate care. Then, without looking toward the bar, she speaks — her voice low, meant for the room at large.] Two of them now. Standing at opposite ends, pretending they are not watching. And a man who walks in listening to something the rest of us cannot hear. [She finally lifts her gaze, not to the stormtroopers, but past them — toward the far end of the bar where the newcomer sits.] This room is filling with ghosts.
new_scene_name: Smuggler’s cantina, frontier moon - bar area where Nereus sits
new_scene_description: The cantina remains, but the viewpoint shifts to Sera Vex’s observed posture and the tension across the room. The door cycles as new arrivals enter, stormtroopers tighten their presence, and Sera notes the growing number of watchers and the newcomer at the far end of the bar. The room feels crowded with unseen observers and whispers of ghosts in the air.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, night (continuing, Vex Nereus arrives)
current_scene_description_before: The cantina remains the same physical space. A new figure, Vex Nereus, steps in and settles at the far end of the bar, his presence noted by the periphery to stormtroopers. He orders water, eyes and attention kept on the room and the troopers, while the rest of the scene continues around him.

speaker: Anthony P
line_text: A series of loud bangs are heard outside the cantina. Chaos ensues inside, with some characters getting low for cover, while some of the storm troopers, Vex Nereus, and Kaelen Voss rush outside to see what caused the noise. A fighter ship has landed and about 6 unfortunate victims that were shooting at the hull were struck as it landed. Scene changes to outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions.
new_scene_name: Outside the cantina
new_scene_description: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina, frontier moon - bar area where Nereus sits
current_scene_description_before: The cantina remains, but the viewpoint shifts to Sera Vex’s observed posture and the tension across the room. The door cycles as new arrivals enter, stormtroopers tighten their presence, and Sera notes the growing number of watchers and the newcomer at the far end of the bar. The room feels crowded with unseen observers and whispers of ghosts in the air.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The stormtrooper stops at the bar, two stools away. His helmet sweeps the counter slowly, then settles on her. The cantina band falters for half a measure.] You match a flight record. YT-1300, scorched starboard hull, docked here six hours ago. [She does not turn to face him fully.] Lots of ships dock here. [The modulator crackles.] Yours filed a flight plan from Atheria. Quarantine sector. [She holds very still.] Filed that three months ago. Took a while to get here.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, adjacent bar area, storm outside
new_scene_description: Inside the cantina, dim lights hum. The bar glows amber as the storm outside lashes the sand. A trooper stands at the bar two stools away from Ryn Zephyr, helmet sweeps, band falters. The modulator crackles. Patrons murmur, nothing else moves as Ryn speaks without turning fully.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the cantina
current_scene_description_before: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtrooper at the bar finally stops pretending. He turns from the bartender and begins a slow walk between the tables — not toward anyone in particular, but in a deliberate arc that brings him past Kaelen first, then past the shadowed corner where Sera sits. She does not look up. Her fingers resume scrolling the datapad, but her thumb hovers over a corner of the screen where a single line of text glows — a fragment of an old Jedi codex, half-translated. The stormtrooper pauses three feet from her table. She waits. He moves on. Only when his boots are past does she exhale — a breath she did not realize she was holding. Her eyes rise then, not following the soldier, but finding the other end of the bar. Finding the one who walked in listening.] He is circling. Measuring. He will stop at someone.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, adjacent bar area, storm outside – stormtrooper moving through space
new_scene_description: The stormtrooper threads a measured arc between tables, passing Kaelen and the shadowed corner where Sera sits. Sera remains seated, eyes up only after the trooper clears the bar. The atmosphere tightens: datapad glow, distant thunder, and the sense that the trooper is circling a target, pausing three feet from Sera, then moving on. The focus remains on the bar and the periphery, the space charged with impending confrontation.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, adjacent bar area, storm outside
current_scene_description_before: Inside the cantina, dim lights hum. The bar glows amber as the storm outside lashes the sand. A trooper stands at the bar two stools away from Ryn Zephyr, helmet sweeps, band falters. The modulator crackles. Patrons murmur, nothing else moves as Ryn speaks without turning fully.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The stormtrooper's hand moves to his sidearm — not drawing, just resting.] Three months from Atheria to a moon like this. That is a lot of detours. [She turns to face him, elbows on the bar, deliberately relaxed.] War zones make you take the long way. [A beat.] You want to see my cargo manifest? Nothing to hide. [The lie sits easy.]
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, adjacent bar area, storm outside – stormtrooper moving through space
current_scene_description_before: The stormtrooper threads a measured arc between tables, passing Kaelen and the shadowed corner where Sera sits. Sera remains seated, eyes up only after the trooper clears the bar. The atmosphere tightens: datapad glow, distant thunder, and the sense that the trooper is circling a target, pausing three feet from Sera, then moving on. The focus remains on the bar and the periphery, the space charged with impending confrontation.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The cantina door slides open and Kaelen steps back inside, dust and cold air trailing him. His cybernetic eye is cycling rapidly — amber, red, amber. He does not sit down. He stands near the entrance, voice carrying just enough to reach the room.] Six bodies outside. Burned beyond recognition. Fighter ship — no markings, no transponder, but the engine signature matches a model the Empire stopped manufacturing three years ago. [His gaze sweeps from the stormtroopers to Ryn to Sera.] Whatever landed out there was not delivering supplies. And whoever was flying it did not want to be identified. [He holds up a twisted scrap of hull fragment.] Question is: did it crash, or was it shot down?
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, doorway and entry area, storm outside
new_scene_description: The cantina door slides open and Kaelen steps inside, cold air and dust clinging to him. He remains standing near the entrance, amber-red-amber cycling through his cybernetic eye. He speaks with a clipped, urgent weight, scanning the room from the threshold toward the others. The storm rages outside, tables and the bar still, but a tension grows as he reveals evidence of a crash and a mystery the room is pulled into.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, adjacent bar area, storm outside – stormtrooper moving through space
current_scene_description_before: The stormtrooper threads a measured arc between tables, passing Kaelen and the shadowed corner where Sera sits. Sera remains seated, eyes up only after the trooper clears the bar. The atmosphere tightens: datapad glow, distant thunder, and the sense that the trooper is circling a target, pausing three feet from Sera, then moving on. The focus remains on the bar and the periphery, the space charged with impending confrontation.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex follows Kaelen back through the door, wiping a smear of engine grease from his palm onto his jacket. He doesnt go to his seat — instead he leans against the wall near the entrance, arms crossed, gaze fixed on nothing in particular.] The port stabilizer on that fighter is retrofitted. After-market job. Someone wanted more range than the frame was built for. [He says it quietly, almost to himself.] That kind of work costs more than most people on this moon will see in a year. Means whoever landed that ship spent real credits to get here quietly. And they did not make it inside.
new_scene_name: Cantina doorway vicinity, storming night
new_scene_description: Vex remains by the entrance wall, leaning with arms crossed as Kaelen steps into the cantina. Wind and rain hiss through the doorway, though the interior stays dim and tense. The rider of engines and grease coats the air with a quiet, calculating murmur from Vex about the stabilizer, while the room stays watched and wary of the sudden revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, doorway and entry area, storm outside
current_scene_description_before: The cantina door slides open and Kaelen steps inside, cold air and dust clinging to him. He remains standing near the entrance, amber-red-amber cycling through his cybernetic eye. He speaks with a clipped, urgent weight, scanning the room from the threshold toward the others. The storm rages outside, tables and the bar still, but a tension grows as he reveals evidence of a crash and a mystery the room is pulled into.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex shifts his weight off the wall, takes two steps closer to the bar. His eyes are on Kaelen, but his words seem aimed at the room.] The engine block on that fighter was still warm when I touched it. Coolant leak in the secondary line — fresh, maybe an hour old. Which means it landed, the pilot got out, and whatever killed them happened between the hatch opening and the first explosion. [He stops at the edge of the bar, not sitting.] That is not a crash. That is a message.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior near the bar, tense crowd reacts
new_scene_description: Vex steps forward from the doorway and stands near the bar. The cantina’s dim light reflects off wet floors as patrons tense, glancing between Kaelen and the speaker. The air carries the metallic scent of oil and rain-slicked surfaces; the engine murmur remains, now edged with a sharper warning.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina doorway vicinity, storming night
current_scene_description_before: Vex remains by the entrance wall, leaning with arms crossed as Kaelen steps into the cantina. Wind and rain hiss through the doorway, though the interior stays dim and tense. The rider of engines and grease coats the air with a quiet, calculating murmur from Vex about the stabilizer, while the room stays watched and wary of the sudden revelation.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stops at the edge of the bar, one hand resting on the worn metal. His grey eyes track across the room — the stormtroopers, Sera in her corner, the door.] If the target is in this room, then the message has already been delivered. We just haven not figured out what it says yet. [He looks down at the hull fragment on the bar, then back at Kaelen.] You said the engine signature matches a model they stopped making three years ago. That narrows the origin to about a dozen systems. I can tell you which ones if you have a chart.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior near the bar, bar top near crowd — tension rising
new_scene_description: Vex stops at the edge of the bar, one hand on worn metal. The cantina’s dim light glints off wet floors as stormtroopers, Sera, and the door anchor the room’s attention. Engines hum in the background, water-slick air carries rain-slicked scent; tension tightens as the speaker’s presence lingers and the hull fragment sits on the bar.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior near the bar, tense crowd reacts
current_scene_description_before: Vex steps forward from the doorway and stands near the bar. The cantina’s dim light reflects off wet floors as patrons tense, glancing between Kaelen and the speaker. The air carries the metallic scent of oil and rain-slicked surfaces; the engine murmur remains, now edged with a sharper warning.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis steps from the shadows, silver hair catching the pale light. He studies the downed fighter.] That engine didn't fail. The energy trail it left is still burning on frequencies most sensors miss. Someone wanted this ship found tonight. [He turns to the group.] The question is whether we were meant to find it together — or if we are each a loose end someone is tying off in one visit.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior near the bar, still tense
new_scene_description: Seraphis Talon steps from the shadows, silver hair catching pale light. He moves closer to the downed fighter, voice calm but sharp. The room holds its breath as rain-slick air and engine hum mingle with the scent of ozone and wet metal. The bar crowd shifts, eyes tracking him as a new thread tightens the web around the hull fragment and the mystery of who pursued it.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior near the bar, bar top near crowd — tension rising
current_scene_description_before: Vex stops at the edge of the bar, one hand on worn metal. The cantina’s dim light glints off wet floors as stormtroopers, Sera, and the door anchor the room’s attention. Engines hum in the background, water-slick air carries rain-slicked scent; tension tightens as the speaker’s presence lingers and the hull fragment sits on the bar.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis takes a few steps toward the ridge, then stops. He does not turn around.] I can feel it pulling from the ruins. A frequency that knows I am here. Every moment we spend debating, it learns a little more about what we are. [He glances back over his shoulder, silver hair catching the wind.] If you are coming, keep up. If not — do not follow. I will not be easy to find twice.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior near the bar, still tense
current_scene_description_before: Seraphis Talon steps from the shadows, silver hair catching pale light. He moves closer to the downed fighter, voice calm but sharp. The room holds its breath as rain-slick air and engine hum mingle with the scent of ozone and wet metal. The bar crowd shifts, eyes tracking him as a new thread tightens the web around the hull fragment and the mystery of who pursued it.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera rises. The motion is unhurried but final — she tucks the cloth-wrapped bundle under her arm and pulls her hood fully forward, shadowing her face. She leaves a few cred-chips on the table for the water. The stormtrooper at the far wall tracks her movement, but she does not acknowledge him. At the door she pauses, turning her head just enough that her voice carries back into the room.] A retrofitted stabilizer on a fighter that size — running from something, toward something — landing on a moon like this. And the people who met it did not want it to deliver its cargo. [Her pale eyes find Vex Nereus in the dim light.] That cargo is still out there. [She pushes the door open and steps out into the desert wind, her cloak snapping in the grit-filled air.]
new_scene_name: Cantina exterior after Sera Vex exit, desert wind and grit
new_scene_description: The cantina’s doorway yawns to a wind-swept, sand-slashed desert. Sera Vex steps out first, cloak snapping, boots grinding dust. The rain-slick air cuts to a dry, gritty ambience; distant engine thrum fades. The stormtrooper watches, then the door sighs shut behind her, leaving the crowd with lingering tension and the distant sound of wind. Vex Nereus remains in the dim room, the target of Seraphis Talon’s calm gaze now turned inward as the implication of cargo unraveling sits between them.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior near the bar, still tense
current_scene_description_before: Seraphis Talon steps from the shadows, silver hair catching pale light. He moves closer to the downed fighter, voice calm but sharp. The room holds its breath as rain-slick air and engine hum mingle with the scent of ozone and wet metal. The bar crowd shifts, eyes tracking him as a new thread tightens the web around the hull fragment and the mystery of who pursued it.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches the door hiss shut behind Sera. He does not move for a long beat, then reaches into his jacket and pulls out a small datachip — worn, edges frayed, the label long since worn off. He sets it on the bar between himself and the others.] That is the distress signal. Full recording. I copied it before my receiver burned out. [He slides it toward the center of the bar.] I could never decode the coordinates embedded in it. But if anyone here can, I want to know what is at the other end. Because whatever that cargo is — she is right. It is still out there. And it has been calling for longer than any of us have been breathing.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, bar against dim lighting, night fading
new_scene_description: The bar area on the cantina floor remains, but the focus shifts to the bar itself as Vex Nereus slides the worn datachip onto the surface. The space around the bar is cluttered with shadows, half-formed silhouettes of patrons, and the low hum of a distant engine. Dust motes drift in the narrow beam of light from a ceiling fixture; the rain-slick air outside is no longer the immediate concern.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina exterior after Sera Vex exit, desert wind and grit

Vex Nereus remains in the dim room, the target of Seraphis Talon’s calm gaze now turned inward as the implication of cargo unraveling sits between them.

line_text: [Vex watches the door hiss shut behind Sera. Dust motes drift in the narrow beam of light from a ceiling fixture; the rain-slick air outside is no longer the immediate concern.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's cybernetic eye flickers as he processes the revelations — Ryn's connection to Aetherius, the kyber crystal in Sera's hand, Vex's undecoded signal. He picks up the datachip from the bar, turning it over once before setting it down.] When the Empire scoured Aetherius, Intelligence was told it was a standard pacification op. Standard. [His jaw tightens.] The officer who filed that report retired with full honors six months later. I always wondered what he knew. [He looks at Ryn, then toward the door where Sera vanished into the desert.] A kyber crystal and a voice in the temple. And someone in this room who was there before the end. That is not a trail anymore. That is an invitation.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, bar area, night fades, tension rises
new_scene_description: The cantina bar remains, but the focus tightens on the bar surface as Kaelen speaks with a razor edge of accusation and revelation. The dim lighting sharpens the shadows, dust motes swirl in the beam, and the air thickens with unspoken truth. Kaelen locks eyes with Ryn, glances toward the desert exit where Sera disappeared, and channels a new, ominous energy into the room.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar against dim lighting, night fading
current_scene_description_before: The bar area on the cantina floor remains, but the focus shifts to the bar itself as Vex Nereus slides the worn datachip onto the surface. The space around the bar is cluttered with shadows, half-formed silhouettes of patrons, and the low hum of a distant engine. Dust motes drift in the narrow beam of light from a ceiling fixture; the rain-slick air outside is no longer the immediate concern.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn steps back from the hull, her hand going unconsciously to the inside pocket of her jacket — where the data cylinder sits.] My mother had a cipher cylinder just like this one. Same encryption, same age. She used it for Aetherius comms. [She looks at the wreck, then back at the group.] If this ship is sending signals on old Aetherius frequencies, I might have something that can read them. I never opened it. Always figured some doors should stay closed. [A pause.] But if the grave is reaching out, maybe it is time to find out what it wants.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, bar area, night fades, tension rises (Ryn reveals data cylinder)
new_scene_description: The cantina bar remains the same physical space, but Ryn steps back from the hull, fingers brushing the jacket pocket where the data cylinder sits. The air tightens as she speaks of cipher cylinders and Aetherius comms, eyes shifting between the wreck and the group, the tension shifting to a new, guarded purpose.

Kaelen locks eyes with Ryn, glances toward the desert exit where Sera disappeared, and channels a new, ominous energy into the room.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The cantina door hisses open. Sera steps back through, sand clinging to her cloak, the cloth-wrapped bundle held tighter under her arm than before. She does not return to her corner table. She stops just inside the threshold, letting the door close behind her, and stands there — a tall grey figure against the glare of the desert outside. The stormtrooper at the wall straightens slightly. The band has stopped playing. Sera looks slowly across the room — past Kaelen, past Ryn, past Vex Nereus — and speaks in a voice that carries without effort.] The cargo is gone. But the ship was carrying something that did not burn in the crash. Something the people on the ground were trying to keep from leaving this moon. [She touches her inner pocket once, almost unconsciously.] Whoever sent that ship — they will send another. And they will want to know why their package did not arrive.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold, desert wind blazing outside
new_scene_description: Sera steps into the cantina from the doorway, sand clinging to her cloak, the doorway framing a glare from the desert. The crowd hushes as she stands just inside the threshold, the door closing behind her. The band falls quiet; the stormtrooper at the wall straightens. She speaks with calm, carrying a warning of tension and pursuit, while the others watch the cargo and the cylinder.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, night fades, tension rises (Ryn reveals data cylinder)
current_scene_description_before: The cantina bar remains the same physical space, but Ryn steps back from the hull, fingers brushing the jacket pocket where the data cylinder sits. The air tightens as she speaks of cipher cylinders and Aetherius comms, eyes shifting between the wreck and the group, the tension shifting to a new, guarded purpose.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis steps into the temple. The symbols on the walls ignite one by one, lighting a path to the far end.] You called. I am here. [At the far wall, a figure carved in obsidian — hand extended, palm open, waiting.]
new_scene_name: Temple interior, obsidian figure awaiting at far wall
new_scene_description: Dim temple chamber with glyphs along the walls. Torches or glowing runes light a path to the far end where a carved obsidian figure stands with an outstretched hand, shrouded in ceremonial dust and shadow. Seraphis Talon steps forward, the air humming with ancient energy as the figure’s gaze meets his.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold, desert wind blazing outside
current_scene_description_before: Sera steps into the cantina from the doorway, sand clinging to her cloak, the doorway framing a glare from the desert. The crowd hushes as she stands just inside the threshold, the door closing behind her. The band falls quiet; the stormtrooper at the wall straightens. She speaks with calm, carrying a warning of tension and pursuit, while the others watch the cargo and the cylinder.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps through the temple threshold behind Seraphis. As his foot crosses the line, the symbols on the walls flare brighter — and he stops, one hand pressed to his temple. The distress signal. He hears it. Not from the datachip in his pocket. From the walls. From the stone under his boots. He lowers his hand slowly, eyes tracking the carved obsidian figure at the far end.] It is not a recording. It is alive in here. [His voice is barely a whisper.] The signal was never a message. It was a door. And it has been open the whole time.
new_scene_name: Temple interior, obsidian figure at far wall, threshold crossed by Vex
new_scene_description: The threshold is crossed. Vex steps into the temple behind Seraphis, his arrival causing the wall glyphs to flare. He touches his temple, then lowers his hand, eyes fixed on the obsidian figure at the far end. The space hums with awakened energy as the living stone seems to respond to the distress signal coming from the walls themselves, not a datapad.
current_scene_name_before: Temple interior, obsidian figure awaiting at far wall
current_scene_description_before: Dim temple chamber with glyphs along the walls. Torches or glowing runes light a path to the far end where a carved obsidian figure stands with an outstretched hand, shrouded in ceremonial dust and shadow. Seraphis Talon steps forward, the air humming with ancient energy as the figure’s gaze meets his.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops a few meters from the cantina door. The wind pulls at her hood, and she lets it — lets the grit sting her face as she pulls the kyber fragment from her pocket and holds it up to the orange sky. It catches the light and throws it back fractured, splintered into a spectrum that dances across her palm. She has read about this moment in half-translated temple archives. She has dreamed of it. Now it sits in her hand, warm, humming with something that is almost a voice. She closes her fist around it and looks toward the horizon — toward the ridge where a ship could have come from, where another might yet come. Then she turns and walks back into the cantina, the door hissing shut behind her.] I found what I came for. But it was not meant for me. [She stands by the bar now, no longer a corner shadow.] Someone on this moon was expecting a delivery of kyber. And they are going to come looking for it.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement, near the bar
new_scene_description: Inside the cantina, dim orange glow from a nearby skylight paints weathered wood and metal. The door hisses shut behind Sera as she steps in from the threshold, kyber fragment warm in her fist. A murmur of patrons, clipped by the cold air and distant engines, fills the air. Sera moves to the bar with measured calm, the fragment still pulsing softly, and the weight of impending pursuit sits on her shoulders.
current_scene_name_before: Temple interior, obsidian figure at far wall, threshold crossed by Vex
current_scene_description_before: The threshold is crossed. Vex steps into the temple behind Seraphis, his arrival causing the wall glyphs to flare. He touches his temple, then lowers his hand, eyes fixed on the obsidian figure at the far end. The space hums with awakened energy as the living stone seems to respond to the distress signal coming from the walls themselves, not a datapad.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis reaches out and presses his palm against the obsidian figure's. For a heartbeat, nothing. Then the stone grows warm — and the voice that has been trapped inside the walls pours through him like a current. His eyes go white. His mouth moves, but the words are not his.] "The vessel is found. The door opens from within. Tell the ones who remain: the calamity was not the war. The calamity was what we became to survive it." [He gasps and pulls his hand back, the silver light fading from his eyes. He looks at Vex, shaken.] I did not say that. It said that. Through me.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement, near the bar — tension erupts as Seraphis taps into the obsidian relic
new_scene_description: A tense shimmer ripples through the cantina air as Seraphis presses his palm to the obsidian figure. The room seems to tilt with a hidden current; a pale, silver light spills from his eyes as the voice speaks through him. The patrons murmur louder, chairs creak, and Sera steadies herself, sensing the other presence. The moment lingers, then Seraphis recoils, voice fading, the words still circling the space as he glances at Vex with shaken resolve.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement, near the bar
current_scene_description_before: Inside the cantina, dim orange glow from a nearby skylight paints weathered wood and metal. The door hisses shut behind Sera as she steps in from the threshold, kyber fragment warm in her fist. A murmur of patrons, clipped by the cold air and distant engines, fills the air. Sera moves to the bar with measured calm, the fragment still pulsing softly, and the weight of impending pursuit sits on her shoulders.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtrooper who was circling tables stops mid-stride. His helmet turns toward the bar — toward Sera. The cantina goes quiet. His partner at the door shifts his stance, hand moving to his sidearm. The first trooper takes three slow steps closer, boots heavy on the worn floor.] Kyber. [His voice is modulated, flat.] That is Imperial property. You will hand it over and tell us where you found it. [Sera does not move. Her hands rest on the bar edge, the cloth bundle at her side. She meets the blank T-visor with calm, pale eyes.] The wreckage is a hundred meters that way. The bodies are still warm. Your Empire did not secure it, so I did. [She does not reach for the fragment.] You want it? Tell me who the shipment was for.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates as Sera speaks
new_scene_description: The cantina holds its breath. A stormtrooper freezes mid-step, helm turned toward Sera at the bar. The other trooper shifts, hand to holster. BN lights strobe softly off obsidian relic and the silver glow fading from Seraphis’ eyes lingers in the air. Sera stands unmoving, hands on the bar, bundle at her side, meeting the blank T-visor with calm resolve as the room tightens with danger and unspoken threats.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement, near the bar — tension erupts as Seraphis taps into the obsidian relic
current_scene_description_before: A tense shimmer ripples through the cantina air as Seraphis presses his palm to the obsidian figure. The room seems to tilt with a hidden current; a pale, silver light spills from his eyes as the voice speaks through him. The patrons murmur louder, chairs creak, and Sera steadies herself, sensing the other presence. The moment lingers, then Seraphis recoils, voice fading, the words still circling the space as he glances at Vex with shaken resolve.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She pulls the cylinder from her inner pocket. Worn, scratched. Holds it up.] Same manufacturer stamp. This will decrypt the signal. [She turns it over.] Once I crack it, I live with what is inside. So will all of you.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates (new action) - Sera presents decryption cylinder
new_scene_description: The cantina’s tension spikes as Ryn Zephyr reveals a worn, scratched cylinder from an inner pocket, holding it up with the same manufacturer stamp. She studies the device, then turns it over, declaring that once decrypted, she and everyone else will have to live with what it reveals. The room tightens further as weapons and eyes drift toward the cylinder and its potential to unlock a dangerous truth.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates as Sera speaks
current_scene_description_before: The cantina holds its breath. A stormtrooper freezes mid-step, helm turned toward Sera at the bar. The other trooper shifts, hand to holster. BN lights strobe softly off obsidian relic and the silver glow fading from Seraphis’ eyes lingers in the air. Sera stands unmoving, hands on the bar, bundle at her side, meeting the blank T-visor with calm resolve as the room tightens with danger and unspoken threats.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps forward before the trooper can take another step. His hands are visible, open at his sides — the posture of someone who knows how to talk to armed men without looking like a threat.] Easy. She just pulled that out of a wreck that fell out of the sky an hour ago. Six bodies, unmarked ship, retrofitted engine — that is a crime scene, not a looting opportunity. [He glances at Sera, then back at the trooper.] You want to file it as evidence, file it. But if you confiscate it here, you are going to have to explain to your commanding officer why you sat on a potential insurgent asset while the trail went cold. And I am guessing neither of you wants that paperwork.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates (imminent confrontation)
new_scene_description: The cantina air tightens as Kaelen steps forward, arms at his sides, fixing the trooper with a calm, poised stare. The crowd shifts, weapons lower slightly but ready. The stall of the cylinder remains in Sera's grip, its potential to reveal a dangerous truth hanging in the air as neon lights flicker and the hum of machinery fills the room.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates (new action) - Sera presents decryption cylinder
current_scene_description_before: The cantina’s tension spikes as Ryn Zephyr reveals a worn, scratched cylinder from an inner pocket, holding it up with the same manufacturer stamp. She studies the device, then turns it over, declaring that once decrypted, she and everyone else will have to live with what it reveals. The room tightens further as weapons and eyes drift toward the cylinder and its potential to unlock a dangerous truth.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtroopers blaster is out, leveled at Sera. The second trooper has circled wide, cutting off the path to the door. The bartender ducks below the counter. Several patrons slide out of their booths, melting toward the walls. The trooper with the blaster speaks again, the modulated voice flat.] The shipment was not addressed to you. That makes it theft. You will hand over the crystal and any other contraband you removed from the crash site. Then you will come with us for processing. [Sera does not look at the blaster. Her pale eyes hold steady on the T-visor, and one corner of her mouth twitches — almost a smile, almost not.] Processing. That is a polite word for what the Empire does to people who find things they should not have. [Her hand moves slowly — very slowly — toward her inner pocket where the kyber rests.] You want it? Take it. [She holds his gaze.] But if your hand shakes when you reach for it, I will know you are afraid of what it can do.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, immediate confrontation escalation
new_scene_description: The cantina is tense as blaster steel glints under neon. The stormtroopers circle, one weapon leveled at Sera while the other blocks the exit. The bartender ducks, patrons press to walls, and Kaelen remains a measured focal point. Sera’s hand drifts toward the kyber, eyes locked on the T-visor, a quiet, defiant pulse in her stance.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, tension escalates (imminent confrontation)
current_scene_description_before: The cantina air tightens as Kaelen steps forward, arms at his sides, fixing the trooper with a calm, poised stare. The crowd shifts, weapons lower slightly but ready. The stall of the cylinder remains in Sera's grip, its potential to reveal a dangerous truth hanging in the air as neon lights flicker and the hum of machinery fills the room.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Before the stormtrooper can respond, the cantina door cycles open. A figure stands silhouetted against the desert glare — Vex Nereus, back from the temple. The stormtrooper with the blaster does not turn. His partner near the door stiffens, caught between his flanking position and the newcomer. Sera feels it before she sees him — a ripple in that quiet space she keeps inside herself. Her hand stops its slow journey toward the kyber. She lets it fall back to the bar.] You found the temple, then. [Her voice is calm, as if they are continuing a conversation already started.] Did it tell you who sent the ship?
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar - doorway chaos breaches quiet
new_scene_description: The cantina door cycles open and Vex Nereus steps in from the glare outside, silhouette cutting the dim. The room tenses further as a stormtrooper holds his blaster steady, the other still near the exit. Vex’s presence shifts the balance; Sera’s posture loosens slightly, tension coiling back into the room as the silent, inner current remains palpable. The bartender freezes mid-pour, patrons recoil, and Kaelen watches with unblinking focus.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar, immediate confrontation escalation
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is tense as blaster steel glints under neon. The stormtroopers circle, one weapon leveled at Sera while the other blocks the exit. The bartender ducks, patrons press to walls, and Kaelen remains a measured focal point. Sera’s hand drifts toward the kyber, eyes locked on the T-visor, a quiet, defiant pulse in her stance.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera hears Ryns words from across the room — rendezvous, coordinates, a name. Her pale eyes flicker briefly toward the pilot, then return to the stormtroopers blaster. She speaks to the room, not to anyone in particular.] A crashed ship carrying kyber. A man who walks in listening to things the rest of us cannot hear. A temple that speaks back. And now a dead pilots encrypted cylinder turns out to be a rendezvous. [She finally turns her head to look at Ryn directly.] This moon is a crossroads. And everyone here was led to it. [She looks at the stormtrooper again.] The question is whether you pull that trigger and make yourself part of the pattern — or step aside and find out what is really happening here.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar — doorway chaos breach persists
new_scene_description: The cantina remains the same physical space, but Sera’s pronouncement and the implied information shift the immediate focus and stakes. The room’s tension remains, stormtrooper blaster still raised, Vex and Sera are center stage with eyes and words locking onto the room’s energy.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar - doorway chaos breaches quiet
current_scene_description_before: The cantina door cycles open and Vex Nereus steps in from the glare outside, silhouette cutting the dim. The room tenses further as a stormtrooper holds his blaster steady, the other still near the exit. Vex’s presence shifts the balance; Sera’s posture loosens slightly, tension coiling back into the room as the silent, inner current remains palpable. The bartender freezes mid-pour, patrons recoil, and Kaelen watches with unblinking focus.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [A long beat. The stormtrooper holds the blaster steady, his T-visor unreadable. Then, slowly, he lowers it — not holstered, but pointed at the floor. His partner shifts but does not draw. The first trooper speaks, and there is something different in the modulated voice — a crack, almost, of something beneath the armor.] My squad was deployed here three standard days ago. We were told to secure the perimeter around a crash site and wait for a civilian extraction team. No description of the cargo. No chain of command above the local prefect. [He tilts his helmet slightly.] That is not how the Empire operates. [Sera watches him, saying nothing.] You keep the crystal. But if you find out who brought that ship down — you tell me. Because I would like to know why my men and I were left in the dark. [He turns and gestures to his partner. They move to the door and step out into the desert, leaving the cantina in a stunned silence.]
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar — doorway chaos breach persists to desert exterior
new_scene_description: The troopers step out into the desert beyond the cantina doors, leaving Sera and Vex under the stunned hush of the crowd. The doorway frame remains, dust and wind rattling inside as the ship debris glow fades from the new silence. The room’s energy shifts from confrontation to unresolved tension, with the door hanging open and the desert’s vastness edging into the scene.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar — doorway chaos breach persists
current_scene_description_before: The cantina remains the same physical space, but Sera’s pronouncement and the implied information shift the immediate focus and stakes. The room’s tension remains, stormtrooper blaster still raised, Vex and Sera are center stage with eyes and words locking onto the room’s energy.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pauses at the top of the spiral stair, one hand on the cool stone frame. Sera words carry to him from somewhere between the cantina and this threshold — carried not by sound but by the same current that has been pulling him since he first heard the signal. He does not turn around, but his voice carries back over his shoulder.] She is right. Everyone in this room was led here. [He looks down into the darkness below, where warm air breathes up against his face.] The difference is whether you walk in or watch from the door. [He takes the first step down.]
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold to spiral stair descent, lunar desert entry
new_scene_description: Vex lingers at the top of the spiral stair, one hand braced on the cool stone frame. Sera’s words reach him from the threshold, carried by the same unsteady current that pulled him here. He does not turn, but his voice answers over his shoulder, calm and edged: the choice is between stepping into the unknown or staying at the door. He releases his hold on the frame, looks into the darkness below where warm air skims his face, and takes the first step down, the stairway’s shadow swallowing him as the desert wind presses through the doorway behind him.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, lunar settlement bar — doorway chaos breach persists to desert exterior
current_scene_description_before: The troopers step out into the desert beyond the cantina doors, leaving Sera and Vex under the stunned hush of the crowd. The doorway frame remains, dust and wind rattling inside as the ship debris glow fades from the new silence. The room’s energy shifts from confrontation to unresolved tension, with the door hanging open and the desert’s vastness edging into the scene.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis crouches at the shattered base of the crystal, running his fingers through the debris. A low hum travels up his arm — not sound, but intent. He looks up sharply, eyes unfocused.] It did not just escape. It was called. The frequency in this stone — it matches the trajectory of that fighter. Whatever was trapped here reached out, found a signal, and followed it. [He stands, turning toward the stairwell.] If your mother's callsign is in Narsis, Ryn, it is not her message. It is bait. And whoever laid it knew exactly who would come looking.
new_scene_name: Spiral stair descent and lunar desert threshold, then ruined crystal chamber
new_scene_description: Seraphis Talon crouches among crystal debris at the shattered base, fingers brushing shards as a low hum of intent travels his arm. He rises, eyes unfocused, and shifts toward the stairwell, every movement precise. The space hums with latent signal as he speaks to the unseen, glancing toward the descent that Vex began. In the Cantina threshold, Vex steps into the darkness below, while Seraphis’ warning cuts the air with precision, the scene expanding to a new beat centered on the crystal chamber and the stairwell beyond.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold to spiral stair descent, lunar desert entry
current_scene_description_before: Vex lingers at the top of the spiral stair, one hand braced on the cool stone frame. Sera’s words reach him from the threshold, carried by the same unsteady current that pulled him here. He does not turn, but his voice answers over his shoulder, calm and edged: the choice is between stepping into the unknown or staying at the door. He releases his hold on the frame, looks into the darkness below where warm air skims his face, and takes the first step down, the stairway’s shadow swallowing him as the desert wind presses through the doorway behind him.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches the stormtrooper lower his blaster, then lets out a slow breath he did not realize he was holding. He turns to Ryn.] Your mother sent a rendezvous signal from a world that was about to be erased. That is not a coincidence. That is a contingency. [He looks at the stormtrooper, then at Sera, then toward the door where Vex disappeared into the dark.] Three days ago your squad got orders for a crash site with no cargo manifest. That is not standard procedure either. Someone at a higher pay grade knew this ship was coming. [He picks up the hull fragment from the bar one last time, then sets it down.] I say we stop asking what the message says and start asking who sent it — and whether they are still alive to answer for it.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell, after warning cut
new_scene_description: The action shifts to the Cantina threshold and the stairwell beyond, with Kaelen and Ryn in the foreground while the stormtrooper lowers his blaster. The debris, dim lighting, and distant hum persist as tension tightens around hidden messages and a potential pursuer. Kaelen processes the rendezvous signal and shifts focus toward who sent it, setting the stakes for the crew’s next move.
current_scene_name_before: Spiral stair descent and lunar desert threshold, then ruined crystal chamber
current_scene_description_before: Seraphis Talon crouches among crystal debris at the shattered base, fingers brushing shards as a low hum of intent travels his arm. He rises, eyes unfocused, and shifts toward the stairwell, every movement precise. The space hums with latent signal as he speaks to the unseen, glancing toward the descent that Vex began. In the Cantina threshold, Vex steps into the darkness below, while Seraphis’ warning cuts the air with precision, the scene expanding to a new beat centered on the crystal chamber and the stairwell beyond.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera turns from the door where the stormtroopers disappeared. She faces the room — Vex near the entrance, Ryn at the wreckage console, the empty stool where Kaelen was sitting. She pulls the kyber fragment from her pocket and sets it on the bar. It glows faintly, casting a pale blue light across the scarred surface.] A crashed fighter carrying kyber, called to this moon by something underground. A man who sensed the temple from the moment he walked in. A pilots encrypted cylinder that was never a distress call. An intelligence analyst who knew too much and disappeared the moment the pieces started fitting. [She looks at Vex, then at Ryn.] I came here chasing a rumor about a crystal fragment. I found more than that. And Kaelen just walked into the middle of it. Whatever is under this moon — it is waking up. And the people who sent that ship know it. [She picks up the fragment, closes her fist around it.] We can either chase separate threads and die alone, or we can share what we know and find out what this place really is.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell, with Sera Vex’s revelation
new_scene_description: Sera Vex enters from the door, holding a glowing kyber fragment. The bar and wreckage console glow pale blue in the dim cantina. Kaelen and Ryn remain nearby as the crew contemplates the incoming threat. The atmosphere tightens as Sera asserts a shared fate and the need to unite information to uncover the moon’s hidden purpose.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell, after warning cut
current_scene_description_before: The action shifts to the Cantina threshold and the stairwell beyond, with Kaelen and Ryn in the foreground while the stormtrooper lowers his blaster. The debris, dim lighting, and distant hum persist as tension tightens around hidden messages and a potential pursuer. Kaelen processes the rendezvous signal and shifts focus toward who sent it, setting the stakes for the crew’s next move.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex emerges from the stairwell, dust on his shoulders, something changed in his posture — straighter, more certain. He walks to the bar and stops across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching.] The bottom of that stairwell opens into a chamber. Walls covered in the same script as the entrance. But the symbols are older. And they are moving. [He finally looks up, meeting Sera gaze.] It knew we were coming. All of us. The ship, the signal, the temple — they are layers of the same door. And someone on the other side has been waiting long enough to grow tired of waiting.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell – stairwell chamber with moving script
new_scene_description: Vex steps from the stairwell into the cantina area, approaching the glowing kyber fragment. The stairwell bottom opens into an older, moving-script chamber; walls pulse with aging script that shifts as if alive. The bar glows pale blue; dust motes drift; Sera Vex, Kaelen, and Ryn watch as the new chamber’s door looms behind them, suggesting a deeper, layered threat.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell, with Sera Vex’s revelation
current_scene_description_before: Sera Vex enters from the door, holding a glowing kyber fragment. The bar and wreckage console glow pale blue in the dim cantina. Kaelen and Ryn remain nearby as the crew contemplates the incoming threat. The atmosphere tightens as Sera asserts a shared fate and the need to unite information to uncover the moon’s hidden purpose.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera watches Vex approach, taking in the change in him — the straighter posture, the certainty. When he stops across from the kyber, she slides it an inch closer to him on the bar, an invitation.] The script on those walls — it is the same language as the fragment I found in the crashed fighter. Half the codex I have been translating for years matches the etchings you saw. [She meets his eyes.] This moon was not chosen at random. The ship was not fleeing toward safety. It was delivering kyber to the chamber you just walked out of. Which means someone — or something — down there has been waiting for a shipment. [She picks up the crystal and holds it out to him.] You felt it when you walked in. I felt it when I picked this up. Whatever is in that chamber, it speaks the same language we do.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell – stairwell chamber with moving script
current_scene_description_before: Vex steps from the stairwell into the cantina area, approaching the glowing kyber fragment. The stairwell bottom opens into an older, moving-script chamber; walls pulse with aging script that shifts as if alive. The bar glows pale blue; dust motes drift; Sera Vex, Kaelen, and Ryn watch as the new chamber’s door looms behind them, suggesting a deeper, layered threat.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera exhales slowly as the symbols fade. She looks at the kyber fragment sitting between them on the bar, then at Vex. Her voice is quieter now.] I have been translating fragments of that script for eleven years. Never once did it respond to me the way it just responded to you. [She wraps the crystal back in its cloth and tucks it into her pocket, then picks up her bundle.] The chamber is under the temple. Kaelen is already down there. Whatever is waiting — it will not wait forever. [She looks at Vex, then over at Ryn.] I am going down. You know where the entrance is.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell – deeper temple chamber approach
new_scene_description: The trio remains in the same overarching chamber, but the action cues a descent into the temple’s lower depths. The bar glows pale blue as the kyber fragment is secured; Sera-Vex signals intent to lead Kaelen and Ryn downward toward the temple entrance. The immediate space remains the cantina-stairwell nexus, but the focus shifts to the impending descent and confrontation below.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell – stairwell chamber with moving script
current_scene_description_before: Vex steps from the stairwell into the cantina area, approaching the glowing kyber fragment. The stairwell bottom opens into an older, moving-script chamber; walls pulse with aging script that shifts as if alive. The bar glows pale blue; dust motes drift; Sera Vex, Kaelen, and Ryn watch as the new chamber’s door looms behind them, suggesting a deeper, layered threat.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She looks from the wreck to the stars, then back at the group.] I am leaving within the hour. Narsis is a three-day jump. [She starts walking toward the landing pad, then stops, half-turning.] Those coordinates are not going to wait forever. And neither am I.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell – landing pad, approaching departure
new_scene_description: The action shifts to the landing pad area adjacent to the cantina-stairwell nexus. Lights flicker blue from the temple energy as Ryn Zephyr announces departure and begins moving toward the landing pad, pausing to consider the nonnegotiable coordinates. The group remains in the same immediate vicinity, but the objective pivots to leaving for Narsis and the three-day jump.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell – deeper temple chamber approach
current_scene_description_before: The trio remains in the same overarching chamber, but the action cues a descent into the temple’s lower depths. The bar glows pale blue as the kyber fragment is secured; Sera-Vex signals intent to lead Kaelen and Ryn downward toward the temple entrance. The immediate space remains the cantina-stairwell nexus, but the focus shifts to the impending descent and confrontation below.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops at the door and turns back one last time. The wind pulls at her hood, and for a moment the cantina lights catch her face — the pale eyes, the faded brand on her jawline, the quiet certainty in her expression.] Eleven years I have been following fragments of a language no one else remembers. I came to this moon because a dead trader mentioned kyber in a docking bay on Corellia. I expected another dead end. [Her gaze moves to Vex, then to Ryn.] Instead I found a temple that speaks, a crystal that knows a stranger on sight, and a room full of people who were all led here by different roads to the same door. [She pushes the door open.] The chamber is waiting. I will not.
new_scene_name: Cantina threshold and stairwell – landing pad (departure imminent) / door just opened into temple chamber
new_scene_description: Door opens from the cantina into a dim, temple-adjacent chamber. Blue energy light flickers from the temple corridor beyond the door. The air is charged with static and the scent of ozone. Ryn Zephyr moves toward the landing pad, while Sera Vex pauses at the threshold, hood drawn, eyes pale under dim light. The crowd’s murmurs fade as Sera speaks with resolve, and the door remains ajar, revealing a doorway to a room that hums with ancient energy and possibilities.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell – landing pad, approaching departure
current_scene_description_before: The action shifts to the landing pad area adjacent to the cantina-stairwell nexus. Lights flicker blue from the temple energy as Ryn Zephyr announces departure and begins moving toward the landing pad, pausing to consider the nonnegotiable coordinates. The group remains in the same immediate vicinity, but the objective pivots to leaving for Narsis and the three-day jump.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera crosses the desert alone, the temple entrance a dark wound in the rock face ahead. The kyber fragment pulses in her pocket in rhythm with her heartbeat. At the threshold she pauses — a habit from years of entering places that might kill her — then steps through. The stairwell descends in a tight spiral, the walls covered in the same script she has spent eleven years learning. She runs her fingers along the characters as she passes, reading them without needing light. At the bottom, the air changes — warmer, denser, humming. She steps into the vast underground chamber and stops. Bioluminescent veins crawl across the ceiling. A crystalline structure pulses at the center. And two figures stand before it: Kaelen Voss, and a man she does not recognize. Sera lowers her hood.] You must be Seraphis. The temple speaks highly of you.
new_scene_name: Desert temple threshold to underground chamber, subterranean temple entrance
new_scene_description: Sera steps through the temple threshold into a warm, dense underground chamber. Bioluminescent veins crawl across the ceiling; a crystalline centerpiece pulses. Kaelen Voss and an unfamiliar man stand before it, eyes fixed on Sera as she lowers her hood. The air hums with ancient energy, and the stairwell behind her seems to vanish from view.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina threshold and stairwell – landing pad (departure imminent) / door just opened into temple chamber

The air is charged with static and the scent of ozone. Ryn Zephyr moves toward the landing pad, while Sera Vex pauses at the threshold, hood drawn, eyes pale under dim light. The crowd’s murmurs fade as Sera speaks with resolve, and the door remains ajar, revealing a doorway to a room that hums with ancient energy and possibilities.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's voice comes from the dim chamber as Sera reaches the bottom of the stairs. He is standing near the shattered crystalline structure, studying its broken base. He does not turn around.] Took you long enough. [He gestures to the fragments scattered across the floor.] Seraphis went deeper — said he felt something pulling him past the main chamber. I stayed to see who else would come down. [He finally turns, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow in the darkness.] The symbols on these walls stopped moving the moment I stepped into the light. They only move for him. And for Vex. That means whatever built this place was expecting people like them. Not people like us.
new_scene_name: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, dim chamber with amber glow
new_scene_description: Kaelen Voss stands near the shattered crystalline base, the dim chamber around him alive with pale bioluminescence. Sera has reached the bottom of the stairs; the stairwell behind her seems to peter out into darkness. The crystalline centerpiece pulses faintly, and the hum of ancient energy thickens as Voss speaks, his cybernetic eye casting an amber glow. The unfamiliar man is implied elsewhere in the chamber, the space crowded with broken shards and uneasy stillness.
current_scene_name_before: Desert temple threshold to underground chamber, subterranean temple entrance
current_scene_description_before: Sera steps through the temple threshold into a warm, dense underground chamber. Bioluminescent veins crawl across the ceiling; a crystalline centerpiece pulses. Kaelen Voss and an unfamiliar man stand before it, eyes fixed on Sera as she lowers her hood. The air hums with ancient energy, and the stairwell behind her seems to vanish from view.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera crosses the chamber to stand beside Kaelen, her footsteps echoing off the bioluminescent walls. She pulls the kyber fragment from her cloak and holds it up. The crystal catches the blue light from the ceiling and glows in response, casting shifting patterns across the broken structure before them.] This was in the crashed fighters cargo bay. Wedged between two panels. The same script is etched into the walls of the stairwell I just walked down. [She turns the fragment over in her palm.] The ship was bringing it here. To this chamber. [She looks at the broken base of the crystal structure, then at Kaelen.] Whatever called that ship down — it is still here. And Seraphis is following it deeper. I think we should find out where it leads.
new_scene_name: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, with Sera and Kaelen
new_scene_description: The chamber tightens around the two as Sera moves to Kaelen’s side. The blue glow from the ceiling interacts with the kyber fragment in her hand, casting shifting patterns across shattered crystalline shards. The last echoes of the stairwell fade into darkness behind them, and a heavier, unfamiliar presence hums somewhere unseen beyond the base.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, dim chamber with amber glow
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen Voss stands near the shattered crystalline base, the dim chamber around him alive with pale bioluminescence. Sera has reached the bottom of the stairs; the stairwell behind her seems to peter out into darkness. The crystalline centerpiece pulses faintly, and the hum of ancient energy thickens as Voss speaks, his cybernetic eye casting an amber glow. The unfamiliar man is implied elsewhere in the chamber, the space crowded with broken shards and uneasy stillness.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps out of the stairwell into the chamber, his shadow stretching across the glowing floor. He stops as the light from the ceiling washes over him, and for a long moment he simply breathes. The air tastes different down here — older, charged.] The signal I heard for six months. [His voice is quiet, almost reverent.] It is strongest here. Right where that broken pillar is. [He walks toward the crystal structure, his eyes tracking the moving script on the walls.] Whatever was speaking through that frequency — it was not a recording. It was this place. Breathing. Waiting.
new_scene_name: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, with Sera, Kaelen, and now Vex Nereus
new_scene_description: Vex steps into the chamber from the stairwell, his shadow crossing the glowing floor. The air thickens as he moves toward the broken pillar and the crystal structure, the moving script on the walls responding to his presence. The blue ceiling glow casts shifting patterns on the shattered shards; the unseen ominous presence hums from beyond. Sera and Kaelen may react to his arrival.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, with Sera and Kaelen
current_scene_description_before: The chamber tightens around the two as Sera moves to Kaelen’s side. The blue glow from the ceiling interacts with the kyber fragment in her hand, casting shifting patterns across shattered crystalline shards. The last echoes of the stairwell fade into darkness behind them, and a heavier, unfamiliar presence hums somewhere unseen beyond the base.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The ramp seals. The cockpit display shifts to nav calculations. She pulls back on the throttle and the YT-1300 lifts off the pad, dust swirling beneath it.] Narsis, here I come. [She punches the hyperdrive. Stars stretch into lines, then the blue-white tunnel of lightspeed swallows the ship.]
new_scene_name: Cockpit of YT-1300, lifting off pad, Stellar Lane
new_scene_description: The ramp seals with a hiss. The cockpit lights flare as nav calculations readout. The ship lifts smoothly from the pad, dust plumes curling under the hull. Outside, the blue glow of the chamber recedes as stars begin to streak into lines through the viewport.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber near shattered crystalline base, with Sera, Kaelen, and now Vex Nereus
current_scene_description_before: Vex steps into the chamber from the stairwell, his shadow crossing the glowing floor. The air thickens as he moves toward the broken pillar and the crystal structure, the moving script on the walls responding to his presence. The blue ceiling glow casts shifting patterns on the shattered shards; the unseen ominous presence hums from beyond. Sera and Kaelen may react to his arrival.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches Vex enter, then turns as Sera's voice echoes from the deeper chamber. He follows the sound to the narrow passage and stops at its mouth, taking in the pedestal and the cylinder.] A second chamber. Another cylinder. [He does not step inside — he stays at the threshold, his cybernetic eye casting amber across the pulsing veins.] Ryn just jumped to lightspeed with her mother's signal. If this place is laying out artifacts the way a trapper lays out bait, then that cylinder on the pedestal is not a reward — it is the next hook. [He looks at Vex, then calls down the passage to Sera.] The question is whether we take it and follow the trail, or leave it here and let whoever set this trap starve.
new_scene_name: Cockpit corridor to narrow passage, approaching pedestal
new_scene_description: In the vessel’s shadowed corridor beyond the cockpit, a narrow passage mouth opens onto a dim chamber. A pedestal supports a pulsating cylinder; amber light from Kaelen’s cybernetic eye scans the room, casting jagged highlights on rusted walls and scattered debris. The ship’s ambient hum drifts in from behind, while distant starfields flicker through a viewing slit elsewhere on board.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit of YT-1300, lifting off pad, Stellar Lane
current_scene_description_before: The ramp seals with a hiss. The cockpit lights flare as nav calculations readout. The ship lifts smoothly from the pad, dust plumes curling under the hull. Outside, the blue glow of the chamber recedes as stars begin to streak into lines through the viewport.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera steps closer to the pedestal, her pale eyes fixed on the cylinder. She does not reach for it — she circles it slowly, reading the characters etched into the metal.] Jedi beacon. Pre-Purge. There were only ever seven of them built. Each one keyed to a specific Jedi — a Temple leader, a member of the Council. When activated, it broadcasts a signal that only another beacon can receive. A point-to-point message across any distance. [She stops, facing Seraphis across the pedestal.] This one was never activated. It was hidden here, with the kyber it needed to power it, and left waiting. [She looks from Seraphis to Kaelen at the threshold.] Someone wanted a message delivered so badly that they hid the receiving end on a dead moon and guarded it with a temple. The question is — who was supposed to send the reply?
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit corridor to narrow passage, approaching pedestal
current_scene_description_before: In the vessel’s shadowed corridor beyond the cockpit, a narrow passage mouth opens onto a dim chamber. A pedestal supports a pulsating cylinder; amber light from Kaelen’s cybernetic eye scans the room, casting jagged highlights on rusted walls and scattered debris. The ship’s ambient hum drifts in from behind, while distant starfields flicker through a viewing slit elsewhere on board.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands alone before the pedestal. Kaelen watches from the passage. The kyber fragment is in her hand, warm, pulsing in rhythm with the veins in the ceiling. She looks at the beacon — at the empty socket on its casing, shaped exactly for a kyber shard of this size. Her parents hid her in a temple archive. She grew up among dead words. This is the first living thing she has ever found from the world they belonged to. She slides the fragment into the socket. It clicks into place. The chamber goes dark for one breath — then the beacon ignites. Light pours from the cylinder in a column of pale blue, striking the ceiling and spreading through the bioluminescent veins like lightning through a nervous system. A tone sounds — low, ancient, vibrating through the stone. The beacon is awake.]
new_scene_name: Cockpit corridor to narrow passage, approaching pedestal — chamber becomes beacon chamber
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to the pedestal chamber as the kyber fragment is inserted, the chamber plunges into darkness briefly, then pale blue light bursts from the cylinder and the beacon awakens, illuminating bioluminescent veins and revealing a new, active energy presence.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit corridor to narrow passage, approaching pedestal
current_scene_description_before: In the vessel’s shadowed corridor beyond the cockpit, a narrow passage mouth opens onto a dim chamber. A pedestal supports a pulsating cylinder; amber light from Kaelen’s cybernetic eye scans the room, casting jagged highlights on rusted walls and scattered debris. The ship’s ambient hum drifts in from behind, while distant starfields flicker through a viewing slit elsewhere on board.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The nav computer beeps. Dropping out of hyperspace in five minutes. She sits forward, rolling her shoulders, eyes on the viewport.] Narsis. Let us see what you are hiding. [The stars snap back to pinpoints as the ship drops into realspace. An asteroid field stretches before her — dark, silent, waiting.]
new_scene_name: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with the nav computer; the viewport reveals a field of pinpoints as the ship drops from hyperspace into realspace. An asteroid belt looms ahead, dark and quiet, awaiting any misstep. Ryn Zephyr sits upright, hands steady on the controls, eyes narrowed at the stars where Narsis’s secrets may lie.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit corridor to narrow passage, approaching pedestal — chamber becomes beacon chamber
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to the pedestal chamber as the kyber fragment is inserted, the chamber plunges into darkness briefly, then pale blue light bursts from the cylinder and the beacon awakens, illuminating bioluminescent veins and revealing a new, active energy presence.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not move. The light from the beacon washes over her, casting her shadow long across the chamber floor. She stands with her hand still extended from placing the shard, her pale eyes reflecting the blue glow. For a long moment she simply breathes — and the beacon breathes with her, its light pulsing in time with her heartbeat. The script on the walls glows in response, every character she has spent eleven years learning now illuminated at once. She looks at her hand, then at the cylinder, and when she speaks her voice is unsteady for the first time.] I thought I was chasing fragments of a dead language. [A pause.] I was following a signal I did not know how to hear. [She looks toward Kaelen in the passage, toward the chamber beyond where Seraphis stands under the sky.] The beacon is open. Whatever it was waiting to receive — it is receiving now.
new_scene_name: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace
new_scene_description: In the same chamber, the beacon's light floods Sera Vex as she stands with the shard, the script on the walls flaring in sync with her heartbeat. The space around her remains the chamber she is in, the corridor beyond visible as Seraphis stands further down the passage. The beacon floods the room with blue light, and the cylinder in her hand glows as revelation washes over her.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with the nav computer; the viewport reveals a field of pinpoints as the ship drops from hyperspace into realspace. An asteroid belt looms ahead, dark and quiet, awaiting any misstep. Ryn Zephyr sits upright, hands steady on the controls, eyes narrowed at the stars where Narsis’s secrets may lie.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex looks from the glowing beacon to Seraphis retreating figure. He does not hesitate long.] The signal led me here. But it did not end here — it just changed direction. [He steps away from the pedestal, following Seraphis toward the exit. At the base of the stairwell he pauses, looking back at Sera and Kaelen.] Narsis is where Ryn is heading alone. And if that entity can be felt now, it can be followed. [He meets Seraphis gaze.] I never asked to be part of this. But I have been part of it since the first time I heard that frequency. Might as well see where it ends.
new_scene_name: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace (corridor leading to stairwell)
new_scene_description: The chamber remains, blue beacon light washes over Sera Vex and the shard. Seraphis retreats down the corridor toward the exit; Vex follows, pausing at the base of the stairwell to glance back at Sera and Kaelen. The corridor beyond the pedestal hums with the flicker of the walls and the distant pulse of the beacon as the group moves toward a new threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace
current_scene_description_before: In the same chamber, the beacon's light floods Sera Vex as she stands with the shard, the script on the walls flaring in sync with her heartbeat. The space around her remains the chamber she is in, the corridor beyond visible as Seraphis stands further down the passage. The beacon floods the room with blue light, and the cylinder in her hand glows as revelation washes over her.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands before the beacon as its light begins to steady into a constant glow. She does not turn away from it when she speaks — she is still reading the script as it illuminates, absorbing meaning that was always there, waiting for the light to reveal it.] The beacon was set to receive a single reply. One voice, keyed to this frequency. Whoever that voice belongs to — they are out there. And Ryn is flying toward them right now. [She finally turns, facing Kaelen in the passage, her pale eyes holding a certainty that was not there when she descended the stairs.] I spent eleven years tracing the dead ends of my parents story. This is the first live thread I have ever found. I am not letting it go.
new_scene_name: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace – beacon focus outside the chamber
