new_scene_description: The beacon’s glow stabilizes into a steady luminance. Sera Vex stands before it, still reading the illuminated script as it reveals meaning long hidden. She turns to Kaelen in the passage, pale eyes fixed with newfound certainty. Behind them, Seraphis retreats toward the exit; the corridor hums with the beacon’s pulse, the group poised at the threshold toward realspace and whatever lies beyond.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace (corridor leading to stairwell)
current_scene_description_before: The chamber remains, blue beacon light washes over Sera Vex and the shard. Seraphis retreats down the corridor toward the exit; Vex follows, pausing at the base of the stairwell to glance back at Sera and Kaelen. The corridor beyond the pedestal hums with the flicker of the walls and the distant pulse of the beacon as the group moves toward a new threshold.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The ship docks inside the hollow asteroid. The bay is dark, emergency lights flickering. She powers down and sits for a moment, listening to the hull tick as it cools.] No other ships on the sensors. Got here first. [She grabs the cylinder, checks her blaster, and heads for the airlock.] Time to see what you left me, Mom.
new_scene_name: Hollow asteroid bay, dim emergency lights
new_scene_description: The ship docks inside a dark hollow asteroid. Emergency lights flicker along a bare bay. Ryn Zephyr powers down, sits, listens to the hull cooling, then grabs a cylinder and checks her blaster before moving toward the airlock.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit, approaching realspace from hyperspace – beacon focus outside the chamber
current_scene_description_before: The beacon’s glow stabilizes into a steady luminance. Sera Vex stands before it, still reading the illuminated script as it reveals meaning long hidden. She turns to Kaelen in the passage, pale eyes fixed with newfound certainty. Behind them, Seraphis retreats toward the exit; the corridor hums with the beacon’s pulse, the group poised at the threshold toward realspace and whatever lies beyond.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex climbs the loading ramp of Seraphis ship, pausing at the top to look back at the moon one last time. The cantina is a distant speck. The temple entrance invisible from here. He tucks his hands into his jacket and steps inside.] Never thought I would leave this moon in anything but my own ship. [He moves through the hold to the co-pilot seat, settling in with the ease of someone who has spent more time in cockpits than anywhere else.] But I never thought I would hear a dead world calling my name either. So. Narsis.
new_scene_name: Seraphis ship interior, loading ramp and hold transition
new_scene_description: Vex climbs the loading ramp of the Seraphis, pauses at the top, then steps inside and moves through the hold toward the co-pilot seat, settling in. The distant moon and speck of the cantina fade from view as the ship hums to life; the action shifts from the hollow asteroid bay to the Seraphis interior.
current_scene_name_before: Hollow asteroid bay, dim emergency lights
current_scene_description_before: The ship docks inside a dark hollow asteroid. Emergency lights flicker along a bare bay. Ryn Zephyr powers down, sits, listens to the hull cooling, then grabs a cylinder and checks her blaster before moving toward the airlock.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera emerges from the temple at a run — not a sprint, but the fastest she has moved since she arrived on this moon. Her cloak streams behind her, the kyber-less beacon left humming in the dark behind her. She crosses the desert toward the south landing pad, where a battered Corellian freighter sits with its loading ramp still down. Seraphis is in the cockpit. Vex is already aboard. She hits the ramp at a jog, gripping the rail, and turns at the top to look back at the temple entrance one last time. Then she steps inside. The ramp lifts. The engines shift pitch.]
new_scene_name: South landing pad, battered Corellian freighter, dusk
new_scene_description: A battered Corellian freighter sits with its loading ramp down on a dusty south landing pad. The Seraphis is already in the cockpit; Vex arrives at the top of the ramp, jogs aboard, and steps inside as the ramp lifts and the engines shift pitch. The desert wind remains, and the temple entrance recedes in the distance.
current_scene_name_before: Seraphis ship interior, loading ramp and hold transition
current_scene_description_before: Vex climbs the loading ramp of the Seraphis, pauses at the top, then steps inside and moves through the hold toward the co-pilot seat, settling in. The distant moon and speck of the cantina fade from view as the ship hums to life; the action shifts from the hollow asteroid bay to the Seraphis interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps through the loading ramp as it begins to rise, his cybernetic eye flickering amber in the dim hold. He finds a spot against the bulkhead, braced against the vibration of takeoff. As the ship breaks atmosphere, he pulls out a small datapad — the hull fragment's serial number still on the screen. He studies it for a moment, then pockets it.] Three days is a lot of time to wonder what we are walking into. [He glances toward Vex in the co-pilot seat, then through the cockpit arch at the blue tunnel of hyperspace.] Ryn has a head start and a message from her mother. We have a fading resonance and a ship full of people who should not have met each other. That is either the worst team ever assembled or the only one that could actually pull this off.
new_scene_name: Corellian freighter cockpit, lifting off into hyperspace
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums as the ship lifts from the pad; controls glow, the vast blue tunnel of hyperspace yawns ahead. Kaelen pockets a datapad, leaning against the bulkhead as the hull vibrates with ascent. Vex sits in the co-pilot seat, quiet but attentive; the Seraphis rumbles with intake air and distant whine of engines.
current_scene_name_before: South landing pad, battered Corellian freighter, dusk
current_scene_description_before: A battered Corellian freighter sits with its loading ramp down on a dusty south landing pad. The Seraphis is already in the cockpit; Vex arrives at the top of the ramp, jogs aboard, and steps inside as the ramp lifts and the engines shift pitch. The desert wind remains, and the temple entrance recedes in the distance.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [In the blue-hushed quiet of hyperspace, Seraphis sits cross-legged on the deck, eyes closed. The silver glow pulses in slow rhythm with his breath.] The entity is not a voice. It is a wound. A撕裂 in the energy field that surrounds all living things. It does not think — it bleeds. And it is bleeding into Ryn through that callsign the same way it bled into me through the crystal. [He opens his eyes.] The question is not what it wants. It does not want. The question is who carved that wound in the first place. Because whoever did — they are still out there. And they made sure we would find it.
new_scene_name: Hyperspace cabin interior, Seraphis meditative on deck
new_scene_description: In the blue hush of hyperspace, Seraphis sits cross-legged on the deck, silver glow pulsing with his breath. The Seraphis presence is a wound, bleeding into Ryn through the callsign, silent but intimate. Kaelen and Vex remain at their positions in the cockpit, the freighter still vibrating with the ship’s ascent, lights flickering softly as tunnel walls rush by outside.
current_scene_name_before: Corellian freighter cockpit, lifting off into hyperspace
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums as the ship lifts from the pad; controls glow, the vast blue tunnel of hyperspace yawns ahead. Kaelen pockets a datapad, leaning against the bulkhead as the hull vibrates with ascent. Vex sits in the co-pilot seat, quiet but attentive; the Seraphis rumbles with intake air and distant whine of engines.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera sits against the bulkhead, her cloak pulled around her, the empty space in her pocket where the kyber fragment used to be. She looks at Seraphis across the dim hold.] When I held the kyber — before I placed it in the beacon — I felt something on the other end. Not a voice. Not words. But an awareness. Like something knew the crystal had been found. [She pauses.] I thought it was the beacon, waiting to be activated. But it was not the beacon. It was wherever the signal was meant to reach. That is what Ryn is flying toward. Not a place. A presence.
new_scene_name: Hyperspace cabin exterior to hold, immediate focus on Sera and Seraphis
new_scene_description: In the dim hold, Sera sits against the bulkhead with cloak drawn tight, Kyber fragment missing from her pocket. Across the space, Seraphis remains a wound in the ship’s quiet, their shared tension crackling with the hyperspace glow. The freighter continues its ascent; cockpit lights blink as tunnel walls shuttle by outside, and the visible distance between Sera and Seraphis thickens with unspoken weight.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace cabin interior, Seraphis meditative on deck
current_scene_description_before: In the blue hush of hyperspace, Seraphis sits cross-legged on the deck, silver glow pulsing with his breath. The Seraphis presence is a wound, bleeding into Ryn through the callsign, silent but intimate. Kaelen and Vex remain at their positions in the cockpit, the freighter still vibrating with the ship’s ascent, lights flickering softly as tunnel walls rush by outside.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The blue tunnel of hyperspace flickers. The ship shudders. On the console, the nav computer counts down — minutes to Narsis. Seraphis stands, bracing himself against the bulkhead, the silver in his eyes brightening.] It knows we are coming. The wound is closing. Or rather — it is finding a new host to heal inside. [He checks his sidearm, then looks at the canopy.] Ryn has been in system for almost three days. If the entity wanted to consume her, it would have happened already. Which means it is waiting. For something else. [The countdown hits zero. Stars slam back into existence.] Or someone.
new_scene_name: Hyperspace corridor to cockpit | countdown near Narsis
new_scene_description: The ship shudders as hyperspace flickers, nav countdown ticking down minutes to Narsis. Seraphis rises from the bulkhead, eyes silver and intense, bracing himself while scanning the canopy. The blue tunnel wanes, then reels back as stars slam into view. A tense hush settles; the space between Sera and Seraphis crackles with new, dangerous awareness as the ship resumes its course.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace cabin exterior to hold, immediate focus on Sera and Seraphis
current_scene_description_before: In the dim hold, Sera sits against the bulkhead with cloak drawn tight, Kyber fragment missing from her pocket. Across the space, Seraphis remains a wound in the ship’s quiet, their shared tension crackling with the hyperspace glow. The freighter continues its ascent; cockpit lights blink as tunnel walls shuttle by outside, and the visible distance between Sera and Seraphis thickens with unspoken weight.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps up to the cockpit, braced against the transit frame as he studies the asteroid field through the viewport. His cybernetic eye cycles through magnification settings, locking onto the distant emergency light.] That is her ship. Docked but not powered down. Emergency beacon still lit means she either left in a hurry or she wanted to make sure we found the right rock. [He looks from Sera to Vex to Seraphis.] We are three days behind her. If that thing bled into her through the callsign, she may not be alone in there anymore. The question is whether we are walking into a reunion or a rescue mission.
new_scene_name: Hyperspace corridor to cockpit, approaching a docked ship’s beacon
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with ambient alarms as the transit frame settles. Kaelen steps up, braced against the frame, eyes the asteroid field through the viewport. The emergency beacon glows within the distance, hinting at a ship docked nearby. Tension rises as the crew deciphers whether this is a reunion or a rescue, while Seraphis and Sera exchange a wary look over the canvas of stars.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace corridor to cockpit | countdown near Narsis
current_scene_description_before: The ship shudders as hyperspace flickers, nav countdown ticking down minutes to Narsis. Seraphis rises from the bulkhead, eyes silver and intense, bracing himself while scanning the canopy. The blue tunnel wanes, then reels back as stars slam into view. A tense hush settles; the space between Sera and Seraphis crackles with new, dangerous awareness as the ship resumes its course.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not wait for an answer from the dark. She walks forward, her boots steady on the stone, her hand empty at her side. Behind her, she hears the others falling in — Vexs footsteps, Seraphiss quieter tread, Kaelen bringing up the rear. The tunnel narrows, then opens into a vast cavern. At its center: a structure of black stone, smaller than the one on the moon but identical in form. And before it, on her knees, Ryn Zephyr. The cylinder lies open beside her. Her hands are empty. She is not moving. But her eyes are open — fixed on something only she can see. Sera stops at the edge of the cavern.] Ryn. [Softer now.] Come back.
new_scene_name: Cavern interior with black stone structure, Ryn Zephyr kneeling
new_scene_description: The party enters a vast cavern whose ceiling is lost in darkness. A black stone monolith sits at the center, its form eerily familiar. Ryn Zephyr kneels beside an open cylinder, unmoving, eyes fixed on something unseen. Sera stands at the cavern’s threshold, addressing Ryn in a softer tone, while Kaelen, Seraphis, and Vex enter behind, paused and tense.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace corridor to cockpit, approaching a docked ship’s beacon
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with ambient alarms as the transit frame settles. Kaelen steps up, braced against the frame, eyes the asteroid field through the viewport. The emergency beacon glows within the distance, hinting at a ship docked nearby. Tension rises as the crew deciphers whether this is a reunion or a rescue, while Seraphis and Sera exchange a wary look over the canvas of stars.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She stands in silence for a long moment after the recording ends. Then she pulls the cylinder from the terminal and walks to the next chamber. Inside: rows of data drives, each labeled with Aetherius dates and designations. She runs a hand over them, then picks one at random and slots it into a reader.] Burn it. [She reads the first file. Her expression shifts.] Or use it. [She looks at the ceiling, a hard smile.] Classic Mom. Giving me the choice but knowing exactly which one I would make.
new_scene_name: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal
new_scene_description: The party steps into a narrower, glossy corridor adjacent to the cavern. The air is cooler, with faint hums from distant machinery. Ryn stands near a console, cylinder in hand, while Sera, Kaelen, Seraphis, and Vex regroup behind her, the cavern dimly visible through an arched opening.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern interior with black stone structure, Ryn Zephyr kneeling
current_scene_description_before: The party enters a vast cavern whose ceiling is lost in darkness. A black stone monolith sits at the center, its form eerily familiar. Ryn Zephyr kneels beside an open cylinder, unmoving, eyes fixed on something unseen. Sera stands at the cavern’s threshold, addressing Ryn in a softer tone, while Kaelen, Seraphis, and Vex enter behind, paused and tense.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera sets the empty cylinder down. She does not know what she is doing — not really. She has never done this before. But she places her palm against Ryns chest, over her heart, and closes her eyes. She thinks of the kyber fragment, of the way it resonated when Vex touched it. She thinks of the beacon, of the light that passed through her. She thinks of her parents, who she never knew, who she has spent eleven years trying to hear. And she pushes — not with her hand, but with the part of her that has been listening to a signal she did not understand. The cavern flickers. Ryn blinks. And Sera gasps, her eyes flying open, because for one terrible instant she is not in the cavern anymore — she is inside Ryns mind, standing in a dark room where a door is closing.] No. [Her voice cracks.] No, you do not get to take her.
new_scene_name: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach
new_scene_description: The cavern flickers as Sera’s attempt to push the beacon sends her consciousness slipping into Ryn’s mind. The physical corridor remains, but the immediate action shifts to a shared mental space: a dark room with a closing door within Ryn’s thoughts. Sera stumbles back, voices strained, while Ryn’s body remains in the corridor, momentarily unresponsive.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal
current_scene_description_before: The party steps into a narrower, glossy corridor adjacent to the cavern. The air is cooler, with faint hums from distant machinery. Ryn stands near a console, cylinder in hand, while Sera, Kaelen, Seraphis, and Vex regroup behind her, the cavern dimly visible through an arched opening.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stands motionless at the cavern entrance, watching Sera with Ryn. He felt it — the same current that has been moving through him since the cantina, but channeled through her deliberately, with intent. His grey eyes catch the flicker of light as the cavern responds. When Sera gasps, he takes a half-step forward, then stops himself.] You felt it too. That thing inside you that you have been ignoring. [His voice is low, meant only for her.] I have been feeling it for six months without knowing what it was. You just used it. You saved her with it.
new_scene_name: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach (still in mental space)
new_scene_description: The physical corridor remains, but Sera and Ryn’s minds are tangled in a shadowed room inside the share mind. Sera staggers, breath ragged, as a door in the mental chamber begins to close. In the real world, Ryn’s body is still, eyes unfocused.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach
current_scene_description_before: The cavern flickers as Sera’s attempt to push the beacon sends her consciousness slipping into Ryn’s mind. The physical corridor remains, but the immediate action shifts to a shared mental space: a dark room with a closing door within Ryn’s thoughts. Sera stumbles back, voices strained, while Ryn’s body remains in the corridor, momentarily unresponsive.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera keeps her hand pressed to Ryns chest, her eyes squeezed shut, sweat beading on her temple. She does not know what she is doing — but she knows she cannot stop. Through the connection, she feels the entity pulling, a sickening drag like a current. And beneath it, smaller, fainter — Ryn. Not the battered pilot who walked into the cantina. The girl who flew for a rebel cell that got crushed. The one who never opened the cylinder because she was afraid of what it would cost her. Sera reaches toward that flicker with everything she has.] Ryn. Your mother did not trap a monster. She trapped a memory that was too heavy for her to carry alone. And she trusted you to carry the key. [A pause. The pull weakens.] She trusted you.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach (still in mental space)
current_scene_description_before: The physical corridor remains, but Sera and Ryn’s minds are tangled in a shadowed room inside the share mind. Sera staggers, breath ragged, as a door in the mental chamber begins to close. In the real world, Ryn’s body is still, eyes unfocused.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex feels the pressure change in the air — the same kind of shift he felt when Sera placed the kyber in the beacon. He moves closer, not to interfere but to stand at her shoulder, close enough that the current passing through her brushes against him too. He does not touch her. He simply stands there, present.] You are not alone in there. [His voice is steady, certain.] Whatever you are reaching toward — I can feel it pulling too. But I can feel you stronger. Keep going.
new_scene_name: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach — Vex arrives as anchor
new_scene_description: The mental chamber expands with a shifting pressure as Vex steps in beside Sera, anchoring the link. The shared space hums with faint arcs of light; Vex’s presence creates a stabilizing beacon, though the door to the mental chamber still breathes shut and the real world holds Ryn in stillness.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach (still in mental space)
current_scene_description_before: The physical corridor remains, but Sera and Ryn’s minds are tangled in a shadowed room inside the share mind. Sera staggers, breath ragged, as a door in the mental chamber begins to close. In the real world, Ryn’s body is still, eyes unfocused.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis carries Ryn through the docking ring, back into his ship. He lays her on the crew bunk, straps her in, and stands over her for a long moment. Her breathing is steady. The shimmer is gone — for now.] The lock and the wound. Made by the same hand. [He returns to the cockpit, fires up the engines.] That means whoever built the temple prison also built the thing that broke out of it. And they built me to be the door. [He pulls away from the drifting YT-1300, setting a course.] I am done being a door. Time to find the architect.
new_scene_name: Seraphis Talon’s ship interior, cockpit to docking ring return (ship departure)
new_scene_description: Inside a starship's cockpit, engines ignite as Seraphis pulls away from the drifting YT-1300. The crew bunk is secured behind him and Ryn rests strapped in, breathing steady. The ship hums with power as navigation locks engage and the exterior view slides past, the docking ring receding. Seraphis speaks of closing doors and finding the architect, resolved to pursue a new course.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber corridor beyond the terminal, mind-link breach — Vex arrives as anchor
current_scene_description_before: The mental chamber expands with a shifting pressure as Vex steps in beside Sera, anchoring the link. The shared space hums with faint arcs of light; Vex’s presence creates a stabilizing beacon, though the door to the mental chamber still breathes shut and the real world holds Ryn in stillness.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera helps Ryn to her feet. The pilot is unsteady, leaning on her, but her eyes are clear. Together they walk out of the cavern, through the dark tunnels, back to the docking bay where the freighter sits beside Ryns ship. Seraphis meets them at the loading ramp. Vex is behind them. Kaelen stands near the consoles, arms crossed, watching. Ryn stops at the base of her ships ramp and looks back at Sera.] You know I was not just carrying a memory in that cylinder. I was carrying the key to how to destroy it. [Sera meets her eyes.] Then we still have work to do.
new_scene_name: Docking bay entrance of freighter complex, night
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to a dim, cavernous docking bay. Ryn, supported by Sera, staggers toward the freighter’s loading ramp as Kaelen and the crew cluster near the consoles. The freighter sits beside Ryn’s ship, hull panels glinting under harsh overhead lights. The air smells of coolant and ozone; distant engines thrumming echo through metal catwalks. Sera and Ryn stop at the ramp, gazes locking, while the architect’s absence hints at the looming threat.
current_scene_name_before: Seraphis Talon’s ship interior, cockpit to docking ring return (ship departure)
current_scene_description_before: Inside a starship's cockpit, engines ignite as Seraphis pulls away from the drifting YT-1300. The crew bunk is secured behind him and Ryn rests strapped in, breathing steady. The ship hums with power as navigation locks engage and the exterior view slides past, the docking ring receding. Seraphis speaks of closing doors and finding the architect, resolved to pursue a new course.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands in the center of the docking bay, the group gathered around her. She looks tired, but there is a clarity in her eyes that was not there when she first walked into the cantina. She picks up the empty cylinder from where she left it, turning it over in her hands.] The beacon is still active on the moon. The kyber is inside it, broadcasting. [She looks at Ryn.] Your mother built a cage. You and I — we just opened it. But we also learned how it works. [She looks at Seraphis, at Vex, at Kaelen.] We build another cage. A bigger one. And we bait it with the one thing the entity cannot resist — the signal it has been chasing for a thousand years.
new_scene_name: Docking bay center, maintenance catwalks around the beacon
new_scene_description: The crew clusters around the central beacon console as Sera stands in the docking bay center, holding the empty cylinder. The beacon on the moon remains active; blue light flickers from the device. Ryn leans on Sera, Kaelen and Seraphis exchange wary glances. The air hums with engines and ionized dust drifting in the harsh light; the freighter looms nearby, its hull panels slick with coolant. Sera speaks with quiet resolve, the group listening as plan and danger converge in this shared space.
current_scene_name_before: Docking bay entrance of freighter complex, night
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to a dim, cavernous docking bay. Ryn, supported by Sera, staggers toward the freighter’s loading ramp as Kaelen and the crew cluster near the consoles. The freighter sits beside Ryn’s ship, hull panels glinting under harsh overhead lights. The air smells of coolant and ozone; distant engines thrumming echo through metal catwalks. Sera and Ryn stop at the ramp, gazes locking, while the architect’s absence hints at the looming threat.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis sits in the pilot seat, eyes closed, hands resting on the console. The ship drifts. Ryn breathes softly in the bunk behind him.] I have been trying to remember. Before the Alliance. Before the betrayal. There is a gap in my memory — a year I cannot account for. I always thought it was the trauma. [He opens his eyes.] But trauma leaves scars. This leaves nothing. A perfect erasure. That is not the work of a wound. That is the work of a craftsman. [He pulls up a star chart.] The temple was built in an age called the Precursor Silence. According to the records, only one faction survived that era intact. [He highlights a region of space.] The Obsidian Conclave. And their last known seat was here. [He sets a course.] Wake me when we arrive.
new_scene_name: Ship cockpit, during drift toward destination
new_scene_description: Inside the pilot seat, the ship drifts with its engines idling. Seraphis sits with eyes closed, hands on the console, while Ryn rests in the bunk behind him. The starfield scrolls on the displays as a star chart glows, marking the Obsidian Conclave region. A calm, tense stillness settles over the cockpit as the course is set and the crew absorbs the new revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Docking bay center, maintenance catwalks around the beacon
current_scene_description_before: The crew clusters around the central beacon console as Sera stands in the docking bay center, holding the empty cylinder. The beacon on the moon remains active; blue light flickers from the device. Ryn leans on Sera, Kaelen and Seraphis exchange wary glances. The air hums with engines and ionized dust drifting in the harsh light; the freighter looms nearby, its hull panels slick with coolant. Sera speaks with quiet resolve, the group listening as plan and danger converge in this shared space.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She drifts in the void, engines on standby, the comm unit silently scanning every frequency. The satchel of data drives sits on the console. She picks one up, weighs it in her hand.] Come on. Someone out there must remember. [The comm light stays dark. She sets the drive down and leans back, staring at the stars.] I can wait.
current_scene_name_before: Ship cockpit, during drift toward destination
current_scene_description_before: Inside the pilot seat, the ship drifts with its engines idling. Seraphis sits with eyes closed, hands on the console, while Ryn rests in the bunk behind him. The starfield scrolls on the displays as a star chart glows, marking the Obsidian Conclave region. A calm, tense stillness settles over the cockpit as the course is set and the crew absorbs the new revelation.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops at the top of the loading ramp and turns back once. The hollow asteroid hangs silent behind them — Ryns ship already gone, her new course set for Aetherius. The beacon signal from the moon is a faint pulse at the edge of her awareness, steady, waiting. She looks at Vex, at Seraphis, at the empty co-pilot seat where Kaelen was.] Ryn is following her own thread now. That leaves the four of us to close the door. [She steps inside the ship.] Let us go build a cage.
new_scene_name: Loading ramp entrance to ship, after Ryn departs
new_scene_description: The loading ramp seals behind Sera as she steps onto the ship, the hollow asteroid fading in the distance. The cockpit remains dim, engine hums rise slightly as the vessel subtly shifts orientation. The four remaining crew exchange glances, a quiet tension settling into the air.
current_scene_name_before: Ship cockpit, during drift toward destination
current_scene_description_before: Inside the pilot seat, the ship drifts with its engines idling. Seraphis sits with eyes closed, hands on the console, while Ryn rests in the bunk behind him. The starfield scrolls on the displays as a star chart glows, marking the Obsidian Conclave region. A calm, tense stillness settles over the cockpit as the course is set and the crew absorbs the new revelation.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She brings the YT-1300 alongside the waiting ship. Through the viewport, she sees a figure standing in an open airlock — silhouette against the dim light. She opens a channel.] You called. I came. [A pause. The figure steps forward into the light: an older woman, grey-streaked hair, a face weathered by years and grief. Ryn's breath catches.] ...Mom?
new_scene_name: Open airlock of the waiting ship, dim exterior light
new_scene_description: The YT-1300 glides into alignment with the open airlock. The exterior hull glints faintly as a figure steps into the light from the waiting ship’s airlock, the space between ships still and cold. Inside, crew watches the viewport, tension tightening as comms crackle softly and the ship’s hum sharpens in anticipation.
current_scene_name_before: Loading ramp entrance to ship, after Ryn departs
current_scene_description_before: The loading ramp seals behind Sera as she steps onto the ship, the hollow asteroid fading in the distance. The cockpit remains dim, engine hums rise slightly as the vessel subtly shifts orientation. The four remaining crew exchange glances, a quiet tension settling into the air.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis cycles the airlock into the Conclave ring. The interior is preserved — not decayed. Shelves of crystalline data-cylinders line the walls, each one glowing with a faint internal light. He runs his fingers along them, reading labels in a script he should not understand but does.] They kept records. Everything. The experiments, the failures, the subjects. [He stops at a cylinder labeled with a symbol he recognizes — the same symbol carved above the temple door.] The lock. That is me. [He pulls it from the shelf, weighing it in his hands.] This will tell me who made me. And whether I was ever meant to be a person, or just a mechanism that learned to speak.
new_scene_name: Conclave archive chamber, Conclave ring interior
new_scene_description: A pristine, circular archive chamber with glossy walls and suspended data cylinders orbiting softly. The air is still, lit by the glow of crystalline cylinders. Seraphis Talon stands among the shelves, the cylinder in his hands, a weight of revelation sense in the tight air as quiet hums fill the space.
current_scene_name_before: Open airlock of the waiting ship, dim exterior light
current_scene_description_before: The YT-1300 glides into alignment with the open airlock. The exterior hull glints faintly as a figure steps into the light from the waiting ship’s airlock, the space between ships still and cold. Inside, crew watches the viewport, tension tightening as comms crackle softly and the ship’s hum sharpens in anticipation.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The ship hums through hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the bulkhead, the empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces the faded script on its lining — the same characters from the temple walls, the beacon, the cylinder. She has been reading this language her whole life without understanding it. Now she does. She folds the cloth carefully and tucks it into her jacket. Then she closes her eyes and waits for the journey to end.]
new_scene_name: Ship interior, hyperspace passage - Sera Vex awaiting journey
new_scene_description: Sera Vex sits apart from the others, back against the bulkhead of the ship, a cloth bundle open in her lap. She studies the faded script on its lining—the same glyphs seen in the temple and on the beacon and cylinder. She reads, folds the cloth, tucks it into her jacket, closes her eyes, and waits as the ship hums through hyperspace.
current_scene_name_before: Conclave archive chamber, Conclave ring interior
current_scene_description_before: A pristine, circular archive chamber with glossy walls and suspended data cylinders orbiting softly. The air is still, lit by the glow of crystalline cylinders. Seraphis Talon stands among the shelves, the cylinder in his hands, a weight of revelation sense in the tight air as quiet hums fill the space.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The silence stretches between the two ships. Ryn finally looks away from the viewport, at the satchel of drives on her console.] You left me the data. The coordinates. Everything I needed to find the truth — but not you. [She looks back, voice steadier now.] Why now? Why not ten years ago? [Her mother's voice comes through, soft.] Because ten years ago, the Empire was still watching the grave. They stopped watching last year. I have been waiting for you to ask the right question ever since.
new_scene_name: Ship interior, hyperspace passage — tense convergence between Sera Vex and Ryn Zephyr
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to a tense, shared interior aboard the second ship. The hum of hyperspace persists as consoles glow. Ryn Zephyr sits at her console, the satchel of drives open on the desk, data coordinates displayed. Sera Vex remains within sensing distance on the other ship's path, the two vessels locked in a quiet standoff as the moment of confrontation arrives.
current_scene_name_before: Ship interior, hyperspace passage - Sera Vex awaiting journey
current_scene_description_before: Sera Vex sits apart from the others, back against the bulkhead of the ship, a cloth bundle open in her lap. She studies the faded script on its lining—the same glyphs seen in the temple and on the beacon and cylinder. She reads, folds the cloth, tucks it into her jacket, closes her eyes, and waits as the ship hums through hyperspace.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sits back in her chair, processing. Then she reaches for the airlock control.] I am coming over. We have twenty years to catch up on, and I have a satchel full of your old data drives that I want answers about. [She pauses before hitting the cycle button.] And Mom? [A long pause on the other end.] Yes? [Ryn's voice cracks, just slightly.] I thought I lost you. [Her mother's voice, barely a whisper.] I know, baby. I know.
current_scene_name_before: Ship interior, hyperspace passage — tense convergence between Sera Vex and Ryn Zephyr
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to a tense, shared interior aboard the second ship. The hum of hyperspace persists as consoles glow. Ryn Zephyr sits at her console, the satchel of drives open on the desk, data coordinates displayed. Sera Vex remains within sensing distance on the other ship's path, the two vessels locked in a quiet standoff as the moment of confrontation arrives.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The airlock cycles. Ryn steps into her mother's ship — old, lived-in, smelling of engine oil and ion scrub. Her mother stands in the corridor, grey eyes wet. Neither moves for a long moment.] You are shorter than I remember. [Her mother laughs — a sound Ryn has not heard in twenty years.] You are taller. And you have my jacket. [Ryn looks down at the battered leather, then back up, a real smile breaking through.] I know. That is why I kept it.
new_scene_name: Mother’s ship corridor, old and lived-in
new_scene_description: Ryn steps into her mother’s ship. The corridor is cluttered with aged, well-used panels, oil scent thick in the air, and the hum of life support. Grey-eyed memories hang between them as they face each other, the battered leather jacket cradled in Ryn’s hands. Neither moves as the moment stretches.
current_scene_name_before: Ship interior, hyperspace passage — tense convergence between Sera Vex and Ryn Zephyr
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to a tense, shared interior aboard the second ship. The hum of hyperspace persists as consoles glow. Ryn Zephyr sits at her console, the satchel of drives open on the desk, data coordinates displayed. Sera Vex remains within sensing distance on the other ship's path, the two vessels locked in a quiet standoff as the moment of confrontation arrives.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis seals the Conclave archive behind him, the data cylinder secured in his jacket. He walks back through the ring toward his ship, footsteps echoing in the silent stone.] I came here looking for answers about who made me. I found them. Now I have a choice: chase a ghost to Aetherius, or face the woman who designed my bones. [He pauses at the airlock, looking back at the crystalline shelves.] But the cylinder also contained a failsafe protocol. If the Lock ever learns what it is, the Architect arranged a meeting. [He reads from the cylinder's final entry.] 'When the door opens itself, bring it to the workshop. The workshop is where it was born.' [He steps into his ship.] The workshop is on Aetherius. She has been planning this reunion since before I took my first breath.
new_scene_name: Airlock of Seraphis Talon ship, in transit toward Aetherius
new_scene_description: Seraphis seals the archive, turns from the Conclave chamber, and steps into the airlock. The ring of his ship looms beyond, engines hum faintly as the corridor breathes with the weight of his revelation. He steps into his ship, the airlock closing behind him, and the corridor loses him as the view shifts to the exterior hull and the star-speckled void beyond.
current_scene_name_before: Mother’s ship corridor, old and lived-in
current_scene_description_before: Ryn steps into her mother’s ship. The corridor is cluttered with aged, well-used panels, oil scent thick in the air, and the hum of life support. Grey-eyed memories hang between them as they face each other, the battered leather jacket cradled in Ryn’s hands. Neither moves as the moment stretches.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The Conclave ring recedes as Seraphis pulls his ship away. He sets coordinates for Aetherius, the dead star shrinking in the viewport. Ryn's transmission still echoes in the cabin — her mother's voice, warm, real.] She gets to go home. [He says it without bitterness, just fact.] I get to meet the woman who wrote my source code. [He engages the hyperdrive. Stars stretch into light.] The failsafe said the workshop is on Aetherius. But it also said something else. Something I did not understand until now. [He pulls up the final line of the cylinder's data.] 'When the Lock meets the Wound, the circuit closes. The door opens. And the Architect will be waiting on both sides.' [He looks at Ryn's coordinates.] She is not just on Aetherius. She is the reason Aetherius was scoured. She erased her own workshop to hide what she made. And she has been waiting for us to find each other.
new_scene_name: Aetherius view, cockpit of Seraphis Talon in transit
new_scene_description: The cockpit glows with the star-streaked void; the hyperdrive thrums louder as the ship accelerates. Ryn’s transmission lingers, a maternal warmth echoing through the cabin. Seraphis studies the final line of data, coordinates flickering on the console, the archive’s mystery tightening around him as the ship leaves the Conclave behind and carves toward the dead star of Aetherius.
current_scene_name_before: Airlock of Seraphis Talon ship, in transit toward Aetherius
current_scene_description_before: Seraphis seals the archive, turns from the Conclave chamber, and steps into the airlock. The ring of his ship looms beyond, engines hum faintly as the corridor breathes with the weight of his revelation. He steps into his ship, the airlock closing behind him, and the corridor loses him as the view shifts to the exterior hull and the star-speckled void beyond.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sits at the small galley table on her mother's ship, a cup of something hot between her hands. Her mother sits across from her, the satchel of data drives open between them.] So. What did the Empire find on Aetherius? [Her mother's gaze flickers to the drives, then back to Ryn.] They found a signal. Not a transmission — a resonance. Buried deep in the planetary core. Older than any civilization in this galaxy. [She pauses.] They called it the Chorus. And it is still singing.
new_scene_name: Mother's ship galley, after-hours, data drives open between mother and Ryn
new_scene_description: A cramped, warm galley aboard a living ship. The cups steam, the satchel of data drives lies between them, dim light casting soft shadows. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other, the air tense with the weight of the Chorus. The resonance hum of Aetherius lingers as their conversation shifts from mission to buried signals.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius view, cockpit of Seraphis Talon in transit
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit glows with the star-streaked void; the hyperdrive thrums louder as the ship accelerates. Ryn’s transmission lingers, a maternal warmth echoing through the cabin. Seraphis studies the final line of data, coordinates flickering on the console, the archive’s mystery tightening around him as the ship leaves the Conclave behind and carves toward the dead star of Aetherius.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn picks up one of the data drives, turns it over.] So what now? We sit in a dead system and let the Chorus keep singing? [Her mother studies her for a long moment.] I spent two decades making sure no one found this data. But you found it anyway. That means I taught you well enough to find me — and that means you get to choose what happens next. [Ryn looks at the drive, then at her mother.] I know a few people who might want to hear about a signal older than the galaxy. And they are not Imperial.
new_scene_name: Mother’s ship galley, after-hours, data drives open between mother and Ryn
new_scene_description: The cramped, warm galley hums with life-sustaining systems. Aster-lit panels flicker faintly, steam from cups fogging the air. The data drive satchel lies between mother and Ryn; tension threads the space as the Chorus pulses in the background. Ryn eyes the drive, then her mother, the conversation shifting from mission to buried signals.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's ship galley, after-hours, data drives open between mother and Ryn
current_scene_description_before: A cramped, warm galley aboard a living ship. The cups steam, the satchel of data drives lies between them, dim light casting soft shadows. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other, the air tense with the weight of the Chorus. The resonance hum of Aetherius lingers as their conversation shifts from mission to buried signals.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The two ships drift side by side in the dead system. Ryn and her mother sit in companionable silence, watching the cold star through the viewport.] So what do we do while we wait for the cavalry? [Her mother smiles — tired, but real.] We talk. Twenty years is a lot of stories. [Ryn settles back, the satchel of drives on the table between them, the Chorus still singing in the data.] I would like that.
current_scene_name_before: Mother’s ship galley, after-hours, data drives open between mother and Ryn
current_scene_description_before: The cramped, warm galley hums with life-sustaining systems. Aster-lit panels flicker faintly, steam from cups fogging the air. The data drive satchel lies between mother and Ryn; tension threads the space as the Chorus pulses in the background. Ryn eyes the drive, then her mother, the conversation shifting from mission to buried signals.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn stands at the viewport, watching the empty space where the cantina crew will appear. Her mother joins her, two cups of caf in hand.] How long? [Her mother checks a display.] A few hours, if they pushed their drives. [Ryn takes the cup.] Good. Gives us time to figure out how we tell them about the Chorus without starting a panic. [Her mother smiles dryly.] You mean without telling them that the Empire has been sitting on a power source that could rewrite the laws of space travel. [Ryn sips her caf.] Exactly. No pressure.
new_scene_name: Mother’s ship galley, viewport angle, after-hours conversation
new_scene_description: The galley remains on the mother’s ship, but the action shifts to the viewport where Ryn and her mother stand side by side, cups in hand, gazing into the silent space beyond the glass. The data drives sit between them, a ticking focal point as they trade lines about timing and revelations. The Chorus hums beneath the dialogue, mood tense but controlled.
current_scene_name_before: Mother’s ship galley, after-hours, data drives open between mother and Ryn
current_scene_description_before: The cramped, warm galley hums with life-sustaining systems. Aster-lit panels flicker faintly, steam from cups fogging the air. The data drive satchel lies between mother and Ryn; tension threads the space as the Chorus pulses in the background. Ryn eyes the drive, then her mother, the conversation shifting from mission to buried signals.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sets down her empty cup and reaches for one of the data drives, slotting it into a reader on the galley table. A waveform appears — complex, beautiful, pulsing.] Listen. [She taps play. A low hum fills the room — resonant, almost organic, like a distant choir singing through stone.] The Chorus. Recorded from the core of Aetherius before the scouring. [Her mother watches her face as she hears it for the first time.] That is the sound of something that has been singing for longer than our species has existed.
new_scene_name: Mother’s ship galley, viewport still and data-driven moment
new_scene_description: Ryn slots in the data drive; a waveform pulses on the reader. The galley hums with the new resonance as the Chorus recording fills the space. The mother watches, the data drives ticking softly between them, the viewport offering nothing but the silent void beyond. Conversation pauses to listen, the air charged with revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Mother’s ship galley, viewport angle, after-hours conversation
current_scene_description_before: The galley remains on the mother’s ship, but the action shifts to the viewport where Ryn and her mother stand side by side, cups in hand, gazing into the silent space beyond the glass. The data drives sit between them, a ticking focal point as they trade lines about timing and revelations. The Chorus hums beneath the dialogue, mood tense but controlled.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Aetherius rises in the viewport — a grey world, its surface scarred by orbital bombardment. The scour is old, the craters healed over, but nothing grows. Seraphis brings the ship low, following a beacon frequency hidden in the cylinder data. It leads to a mountain range, then into a canyon, then to a wall of stone that is not stone at all — a hangar door, camouflaged.] The workshop. [He sets the ship down. The door does not open. Instead, a voice comes through the comm — calm, familiar, unhurried.] 'You are early, Prototype VII. I always liked that about you. Impatient. Come inside. I have been preparing for this conversation since before you had a name.' [Seraphis sits in the silence, hand on the cylinder in his pocket. He does not respond. He just cycles the airlock and steps out into the grey light of Aetherius.]
new_scene_name: Workshop hangar on Aetherius, camouflaged hangar door behind canyon wall
new_scene_description: The hangar-like chamber is hidden within a camouflaged wall, dimly lit by a cold, grey light. The ship rests at a quiet, alloyed floor; a voice crackles through the comm as Seraphis steps out, cylinder in hand, the airlock cycling. The space feels ceremonial, heavy with expectancy and the hush of stalled time.
current_scene_name_before: Mother’s ship galley, viewport still and data-driven moment
current_scene_description_before: Ryn slots in the data drive; a waveform pulses on the reader. The galley hums with the new resonance as the Chorus recording fills the space. The mother watches, the data drives ticking softly between them, the viewport offering nothing but the silent void beyond. Conversation pauses to listen, the air charged with revelation.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The hangar door opens. Inside: a workshop frozen in time — holographic schematics of neural architectures, empty cryo-cradles, a wall of faces. His face. Seraphis walks past rows of dormant prototypes, each one a variation of him. At the center, a woman sits at a console. She does not turn.] You have your mother's eyes. [Kaelen Voss finally looks up. She is older than the cantina, her face etched with the kind of exhaustion that spans decades.] But not her memories. Those I left blank on purpose. A clean slate makes a better lock. [Seraphis stops ten feet from her, hand on the cylinder.] You made me to trap something. Then you let it out. Why? [She smiles — thin, sad.] Because I realized too late that the thing I was locking away was hope. And without it, we were already dead.
new_scene_name: Workshop interior, frozen time, holographic schematics and cryo-cradles
new_scene_description: A sterile, time-warped workshop inside the hangar, with holographic neural schematics flickering, empty cryo-cradles lining the walls, and a wall of faces—Seraphis included. Kaelen Voss sits at a console at the center, not turning. The air hums with dormant energy; Seraphis stands ten feet away, cylinder in hand, the room thick with unspoken history and a ceremonial stillness.
current_scene_name_before: Workshop hangar on Aetherius, camouflaged hangar door behind canyon wall
current_scene_description_before: The hangar-like chamber is hidden within a camouflaged wall, dimly lit by a cold, grey light. The ship rests at a quiet, alloyed floor; a voice crackles through the comm as Seraphis steps out, cylinder in hand, the airlock cycling. The space feels ceremonial, heavy with expectancy and the hush of stalled time.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The cantina crew's ships drift into formation around her mother's vessel. Ryn watches from the viewport, the Chorus still humming softly from the speakers.] They made it. [Her mother stands beside her, hands tucked into her pockets.] So. You ready to tell them everything? [Ryn picks up the satchel of drives, weighing it one last time.] They came all this way. Reckon they deserve the truth. [She heads for the airlock, pausing at the threshold.] Coming? [Her mother smiles.] Would not miss it.
new_scene_name: Cantina crew ships around vessel, airlock threshold
new_scene_description: The hangar falls away to a new exterior-facing interior: the airlock chamber of the mother's vessel, lit by corridor lights. Ryn Zephyr stands with her mother at the threshold, the hum of the Chorus still faint from the speakers. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn’s side as the air shifts with the hint of incoming crew beyond the airlock.
current_scene_name_before: Workshop interior, frozen time, holographic schematics and cryo-cradles
current_scene_description_before: A sterile, time-warped workshop inside the hangar, with holographic neural schematics flickering, empty cryo-cradles lining the walls, and a wall of faces—Seraphis included. Kaelen Voss sits at a console at the center, not turning. The air hums with dormant energy; Seraphis stands ten feet away, cylinder in hand, the room thick with unspoken history and a ceremonial stillness.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The airlock opens onto her mother's ship. Ryn steps through, satchel in hand, and finds Kaelen Voss leaning against a bulkhead, arms crossed.] You look like you have seen a ghost. [Ryn smirks.] Something like that. [She holds up the satchel.] Got something you need to hear. All of you. [She looks past him at the others gathering in the galley — Vex, Sera, Seraphis.] The Empire did not bury a weapon on Aetherius. They buried a conversation. And it is still going.
new_scene_name: Mother's ship airlock, Kaelen Voss at bulkhead
new_scene_description: The airlock chamber of the mother's vessel opens, revealing a crowded galley beyond. Kaelen Voss leans against a nearby bulkhead with arms crossed as Ryn steps through, satchel in hand. The crew gathers: Vex, Sera, Seraphis, their silhouettes dimly lit by corridor glow and the hum of distant Chorus still audible.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina crew ships around vessel, airlock threshold
current_scene_description_before: The hangar falls away to a new exterior-facing interior: the airlock chamber of the mother's vessel, lit by corridor lights. Ryn Zephyr stands with her mother at the threshold, the hum of the Chorus still faint from the speakers. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn’s side as the air shifts with the hint of incoming crew beyond the airlock.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn slots a drive into the galley display. The Chorus waveform appears — ancient, pulsing, beautiful. The room falls silent as the low hum fills the space.] This is what they found. A signal from the core of Aetherius. Older than any known civilization. [She looks at the group — Kaelen, Vex, Sera, Seraphis, her mother.] The Empire could not understand it, so they buried it. But someone has been answering it. And they built ships that run on its resonance. [She sets the drive down.] That wreck we found? It was one of theirs.
new_scene_name: Mother's ship galley, near-quiet chorus of the drive
new_scene_description: The galley atmosphere tightens as the drive drive hums beneath the display. Ryn steps back, drive settled, the Chorus waveform pulsing in the display. The room remains in shadow except for the corridor glow spilling through the airlock, crew gathered tensely around the bulkhead and the satchel on Ryn's hip glinting. The air crackles with a charged hush as the new revelation sinks in.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's ship airlock, Kaelen Voss at bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The airlock chamber of the mother's vessel opens, revealing a crowded galley beyond. Kaelen Voss leans against a nearby bulkhead with arms crossed as Ryn steps through, satchel in hand. The crew gathers: Vex, Sera, Seraphis, their silhouettes dimly lit by corridor glow and the hum of distant Chorus still audible.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps forward from the galley, wiping a streak of coolant from his forearm. He looks at the satchel, then at Ryn.] A conversation that is still going. Means whoever started it is still on the other end. Or something is pretending to be them. [He stops a few feet from her, arms loose at his sides.] Either way, we have been following someone else script since the moment that fighter crashed. I would like to know who has been writing it. [He nods at the satchel.] Play it.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's ship galley, near-quiet chorus of the drive
current_scene_description_before: The galley atmosphere tightens as the drive drive hums beneath the display. Ryn steps back, drive settled, the Chorus waveform pulsing in the display. The room remains in shadow except for the corridor glow spilling through the airlock, crew gathered tensely around the bulkhead and the satchel on Ryn's hip glinting. The air crackles with a charged hush as the new revelation sinks in.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The crew disperses to their ships, prepping for the jump to uncharted space. Ryn lingers at the airlock with her mother.] You sure about this? [Her mother looks at her, steady.] I have been running my whole life. I am tired of running. [Ryn nods.] Then let us go find out who has been singing back. [She steps into her own ship, the satchel of drives secure. The small fleet forms up and points toward the void beyond the charts.]
new_scene_name: Mother's ship airlock vestibule, just before jump sequence
new_scene_description: The corridor outside the airlock remains dim, with occasional alarms pulsing. Ryn lingers at the airlock with her mother, the satchel at Ryn’s hip catching the glow of console warnings. The crew disperses to their shipbeds and prepares for the jump, the fleet aligning in a tight formation as the void beckons beyond the chart display.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's ship galley, near-quiet chorus of the drive
current_scene_description_before: The galley atmosphere tightens as the drive drive hums beneath the display. Ryn steps back, drive settled, the Chorus waveform pulsing in the display. The room remains in shadow except for the corridor glow spilling through the airlock, crew gathered tensely around the bulkhead and the satchel on Ryn's hip glinting. The air crackles with a charged hush as the new revelation sinks in.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She docks at the listening post. The airlock opens to darkness — emergency lights flicker on as she enters, revealing corridors lined with alien script, walls covered in resonance charts spanning millennia.] This place has been recording the Chorus longer than humans have had spaceflight. [She runs her fingers along a carving that predates every known language in the galaxy.] Whoever built this — they were waiting for someone to find it. And I think that someone is us.
new_scene_name: Listening Post docking, dark corridors of alien archive
new_scene_description: Emergency lights flicker as the airlock opens to a shadowy, ancient corridor. Alien script lines the walls, resonance charts glow along the surfaces, and a carved tablet beneath a long-lit plaque hums with a primordial resonance. Ryn steps inside, fingertips tracing the carving that predates all known language, the air thick with unseen presence and a charged silence.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's ship airlock vestibule, just before jump sequence
current_scene_description_before: The corridor outside the airlock remains dim, with occasional alarms pulsing. Ryn lingers at the airlock with her mother, the satchel at Ryn’s hip catching the glow of console warnings. The crew disperses to their shipbeds and prepares for the jump, the fleet aligning in a tight formation as the void beckons beyond the chart display.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn pulls her hand back from the spire. The Chorus fades to a background hum. She stands in silence for a moment, then opens the comm to the fleet.] The people who built this — they are not gone. They are out there, beyond the rim. The spire showed me coordinates. A place the Chorus is strongest. [She transmits the data.] That is where we go next.
new_scene_name: Listening Post docking, dark corridors of alien archive — comm transmit to fleet
new_scene_description: Ryn withdraws from the spire, the chorus fading to a background hum. She stands in the dim, pulsating corridor, silence thick in the air. She opens the comm to the fleet and transmits the spire’s coordinates, the carved tablet’s resonance still echoing in the space.
current_scene_name_before: Listening Post docking, dark corridors of alien archive
current_scene_description_before: Emergency lights flicker as the airlock opens to a shadowy, ancient corridor. Alien script lines the walls, resonance charts glow along the surfaces, and a carved tablet beneath a long-lit plaque hums with a primordial resonance. Ryn steps inside, fingertips tracing the carving that predates all known language, the air thick with unseen presence and a charged silence.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Kaelen stands, walks to one of the cryo-cradles. She touches the edge of it gently, like a grave.] The Conclave came to me with a problem. Something old had awakened in the deep places of the galaxy. It was not a god. It was not a monster. It was a question — given form, given hunger. It consumed civilizations by asking them who they were and then becoming the answer. [She turns to face him.] I could not kill it. So I built a lock that could hold it. I used my own genetic template. I used a dead woman's memories as insulation. You are my daughter, Seraphis. And the woman whose memories I sealed inside you — she was my wife. [Seraphis does not move. His hand falls from the cylinder.] You built me from her. [Kaelen nods.] I built you to be her. So that when the question came for you, it would find someone who already knew the answer.
new_scene_name: Hospital corridor outside room 214
new_scene_description: A stark, dimly lit alien archive corridor with humming consoles. Ryn steps back from the spire, comms still crackling softly. The corridor’s walls pulse with bioluminescent panels; farther ahead, a glimmering seal seals an arched doorway to a cryo-cradle chamber. The air tastes of ozone and old stone.
current_scene_name_before: Listening Post docking, dark corridors of alien archive — comm transmit to fleet
current_scene_description_before: Ryn withdraws from the spire, the chorus fading to a background hum. She stands in the dim, pulsating corridor, silence thick in the air. She opens the comm to the fleet and transmits the spire’s coordinates, the carved tablet’s resonance still echoing in the space.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She steps out of the ship onto the crystalline floor. The chamber is vast — larger than any city she has seen. At the center, a spire identical to the one at the listening post rises a hundred meters, but this one is active, fully lit, the Chorus pouring from it in waves.] This is the heart. The source of it all. [Her mother and the crew emerge behind her, spreading out, staring up at the spire in awe.] Every signal from Aetherius. Every answer. It all came from here. [Ryn walks toward the spire, the light reflecting in her eyes.]
new_scene_name: City-sized chamber within the heart of the ship: the Chorus spire chamber
new_scene_description: A vast, crystalline chamber dominates the space. The air hums with energy; strands of light pour from a central, active spire, casting prismatic reflections across smooth, glassy floors. Ryn and the crew step onto the radiant surface, gazing upward as the Chorus surges from the spire. Family and teammates fan out, eyes fixed on the pulsing beacon at the chamber’s core.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital corridor outside room 214
current_scene_description_before: A stark, dimly lit alien archive corridor with humming consoles. Ryn steps back from the spire, comms still crackling softly. The corridor’s walls pulse with bioluminescent panels; farther ahead, a glimmering seal seals an arched doorway to a cryo-cradle chamber. The air tastes of ozone and old stone.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn stands before the light-being, heart pounding, but her voice steady.] A new voice. That is what you want? [The figure pulses, the Chorus harmonizing around its words.] 'The song is eternal. But voices fade. We have been alone for longer than your species has drawn breath. You carry the resonance in your blood — your mother touched the spire at Aetherius before you were born. It marked her. And through her, you. You were always meant to find us.' [Ryn looks back at her mother, who stands frozen at the edge of the chamber.] You never told me. [Her mother's voice, barely audible.] I did not know. Not for certain. Not until you touched the spire at the listening post and I saw it recognize you.
new_scene_name: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship, approaching the light-being
new_scene_description: Ryn stands before the luminous, shifting figure as the Chorus floods the chamber. The radiant surface beneath accents the disbelief in her eyes; her mother remains at the chamber’s edge, a frozen silhouette. Light strands weave through the air, refracting around the central spire, while the crew dimly watches, tension taut as strings. The space feels suddenly intimate, as if the spire themselves are listening.
current_scene_name_before: City-sized chamber within the heart of the ship: the Chorus spire chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, crystalline chamber dominates the space. The air hums with energy; strands of light pour from a central, active spire, casting prismatic reflections across smooth, glassy floors. Ryn and the crew step onto the radiant surface, gazing upward as the Chorus surges from the spire. Family and teammates fan out, eyes fixed on the pulsing beacon at the chamber’s core.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn is silent for a long moment. Then she reaches into her jacket and pulls out the data cylinder — the one her mother pressed into her hand before Aetherius fell, the one that started this entire journey. She holds it up to the light-being.] I did not come all this way to walk away. But I am not joining anything that leaves everyone else behind. [She sets the cylinder on the spire's base, where it resonates with the crystal.] This cylinder carries the first question my mother ever asked the Chorus. I am leaving it here as proof that we are ready to listen. Not as followers. As partners. [She steps back, meeting the entity's light directly.] Teach us. Do not consume us.
current_scene_name_before: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship, approaching the light-being
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands before the luminous, shifting figure as the Chorus floods the chamber. The radiant surface beneath accents the disbelief in her eyes; her mother remains at the chamber’s edge, a frozen silhouette. Light strands weave through the air, refracting around the central spire, while the crew dimly watches, tension taut as strings. The space feels suddenly intimate, as if the spire themselves are listening.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Later. The crew sits in a circle on the crystalline floor of the heart chamber. The Chorus hums gently in the background — no longer overwhelming, just present. Comfortable. Ryn leans back on her hands, looking at the faces around her: Kaelen, Vex, Sera, Seraphis, her mother.] So. We have a base at the edge of known space, the keys to a billion years of knowledge, and a direct line to something older than the stars themselves. [She grins.] Anyone know a good engineer? We are going to need a bigger ship.
new_scene_name: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship — circle on crystalline floor
new_scene_description: The crew sits cross-legged on the crystalline floor of the heart chamber. The Chorus hums softly in the background, a calm presence. Light strands drift, bathed in a warm, intimate glow as faces turn toward Ryn. The space feels cozy and shared, like a gathered crew planning a bold future.
current_scene_name_before: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship, approaching the light-being
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands before the luminous, shifting figure as the Chorus floods the chamber. The radiant surface beneath accents the disbelief in her eyes; her mother remains at the chamber’s edge, a frozen silhouette. Light strands weave through the air, refracting around the central spire, while the crew dimly watches, tension taut as strings. The space feels suddenly intimate, as if the spire themselves are listening.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex snorts — the closest thing to a laugh he has produced since stepping into that cantina. He looks down at his grease-stained hands, then back at the group.] You are looking at one. [He gestures vaguely at the Chorus, the crystalline chamber, the impossible technology around them.] I spent ten years patching together freighters that were falling apart before I was born. This? [He taps the crystal floor.] This is the first thing I have seen in years that I actually want to understand. [He meets Ryns grin with something close to one of his own.] So yes. Bigger ship. And someone to teach me what all of this means.
new_scene_name: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship — still in the same moment
new_scene_description: The crew remains seated on the crystalline floor, the warm glow and drifting light strands unchanged. Vex Nereus stands, leaning into the shared space, eyes fixed on Ryn as the cantina humor fades into resolve. The chamber hums with a new, heavier intent as they process his words about a bigger ship and learning the meaning of it all.
current_scene_name_before: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship — circle on crystalline floor
current_scene_description_before: The crew sits cross-legged on the crystalline floor of the heart chamber. The Chorus hums softly in the background, a calm presence. Light strands drift, bathed in a warm, intimate glow as faces turn toward Ryn. The space feels cozy and shared, like a gathered crew planning a bold future.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The workshop trembles. Dust drifts from the ceiling. The holographic schematics flicker and die. Kaelen looks up, her expression shifting from grief to something harder — readiness.] It heard you say it. The word. 'Mother.' The entity does not feed on fear. It feeds on identity. And you just claimed one. [The lights fail. A low resonance fills the air — the same frequency from the temple, from the crystal prison, from Ryn's cockpit. Seraphis turns toward the hangar door, the silver in her eyes flaring bright.] Then let it come. I have been a prototype, a lock, a ghost wearing a dead woman's face. Now I want to find out what I become when I stop being anyone's question and start being my own answer. [She walks toward the door. Kaelen watches her go — and for the first time, she smiles like a mother.]
new_scene_name: Hangar Bay entrance, ship; sudden breach in control
new_scene_description: A crackling silence falls as dust bursts from the hangar doors. The hull shudders; the hangar lights flicker, revealing a vast space beyond with dim emergency glow. Seraphis strides toward the hangar, Kaelen in reluctant, wary step behind. The crew shifts, some rising to intercept, others bracing for what comes next. The hum of the ship intensifies, a corridor of cold metal and possibility opening to the unknown.
current_scene_name_before: Chorus spire chamber, heart of ship — still in the same moment
current_scene_description_before: The crew remains seated on the crystalline floor, the warm glow and drifting light strands unchanged. Vex Nereus stands, leaning into the shared space, eyes fixed on Ryn as the cantina humor fades into resolve. The chamber hums with a new, heavier intent as they process his words about a bigger ship and learning the meaning of it all.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Outside the workshop, the grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of light descends — not burning, not blinding, but shimmering like heat over stone. The entity does not have a shape. It has presence. It speaks without sound.] YOU WHO WERE MADE TO HOLD ME. YOU WHO CARRY THE MEMORY OF THE ONE WHO REFUSED. WHAT ARE YOU WILLING TO BECOME? [Seraphis walks into the light. Her silver hair lifts, her eyes white, her voice steady.] I am willing to become what she already was. Not a lock. Not a weapon. A person who loved something more than she feared the unknown. [The light pulses — once, twice — then stills.] I choose to become someone who answers for herself. [The entity does not consume her. It hesitates. For the first time in millennia, the question meets an answer it did not create. And the workshop door behind Seraphis slides open — Kaelen standing in the frame, tears cutting tracks through the dust on her face.]
new_scene_name: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light descends outside
new_scene_description: A silver-lit exterior scene unfolds as a shimmering, formless entity speaks. Seraphis steps into the light; Kaelen appears behind in the workshop doorway, dust clinging to her tears. The workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen drawn into the moment as the light lingers over the ground and metal around them.
current_scene_name_before: Hangar Bay entrance, ship; sudden breach in control
current_scene_description_before: A crackling silence falls as dust bursts from the hangar doors. The hull shudders; the hangar lights flicker, revealing a vast space beyond with dim emergency glow. Seraphis strides toward the hangar, Kaelen in reluctant, wary step behind. The crew shifts, some rising to intercept, others bracing for what comes next. The hum of the ship intensifies, a corridor of cold metal and possibility opening to the unknown.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps out of the workshop into the grey light of Aetherius. The column of light has faded, but the air still hums with what passed through it. He scans the horizon, tool in hand, then looks at the others gathering in the doorway. The cage frame is half-built behind him, welded from salvaged plating and conduit.] The entity pulled back. It did not retreat — it pulled back. There is a difference. [He wipes sweat from his brow, leaving a streak of grease.] That means it is thinking. And if it is thinking, it is planning. [He looks at Seraphis, then at the half-finished cage.] We have maybe an hour before it decides what to try next. I need someone on the resonance calibrator and someone on the lock mechanism. The frame is ready for the lining.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light descends outside
current_scene_description_before: A silver-lit exterior scene unfolds as a shimmering, formless entity speaks. Seraphis steps into the light; Kaelen appears behind in the workshop doorway, dust clinging to her tears. The workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen drawn into the moment as the light lingers over the ground and metal around them.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex crouches beside the cage frame, running a diagnostic scanner along the welded seam. The readout flickers — interference from the residual entity frequency. He mutters a curse under his breath, then looks up.] Kaelen. That lock sequence you pulled from the beacon data — does it require a continuous power feed or a pulse? Because if it is a pulse, I can route it through the secondary relay and save us thirty minutes of cabling. [He stands, wiping his hands on his pants, and glances at the sky.] Thirty minutes we might not have.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light descends outside
current_scene_description_before: A silver-lit exterior scene unfolds as a shimmering, formless entity speaks. Seraphis steps into the light; Kaelen appears behind in the workshop doorway, dust clinging to her tears. The workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen drawn into the moment as the light lingers over the ground and metal around them.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stops mid-weld. The tool in his hand goes quiet. He raises his head slowly, eyes unfocused, listening to something only he can hear. The Aether — a thread of it, thin but present — winds through the desert air like smoke.] It is back. [His voice is flat, certain.] Not the full presence. Just a thread. Testing. [He sets the welder down and moves to the cage frame, running his hand along the conduit.] Which means it knows we are building. And it is trying to find the gap before we seal it. [He looks at Seraphis, then Sera.] We do not have an hour. We have minutes. Finish the lock. I will hold the thread.
new_scene_name: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light intensifies
new_scene_description: The workshop interior comes into sharper focus: workbenches, tools, and a cage frame. Kaelen and Seraphis stand near the doorway as the welders’ hum softens. Vex Nereus, mid-weld, freezes, head lifting to hear the Aether’s thread weaving through the air. The space tightens with urgency as the thread suggests an intruding presence; metal glints, sparks die, and the crew readies to seal the connection.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light descends outside
current_scene_description_before: A silver-lit exterior scene unfolds as a shimmering, formless entity speaks. Seraphis steps into the light; Kaelen appears behind in the workshop doorway, dust clinging to her tears. The workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen drawn into the moment as the light lingers over the ground and metal around them.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex connects the final conduit and steps back. The cage frame hums — a low, deliberate vibration that resonates in the chest. The desert air around it shimmers faintly. He wipes his hands on his jacket and looks at the group.] Feedback loop is live. The resonance calibrator is singing at the same frequency as the thread. [He gestures at the shimmer.] If this works, the entity will follow the signal straight into the cage before it realizes the door is closing. [His voice drops.] If it does not work — we just painted a target on every one of us. [He picks up a tool, meets each of their eyes in turn.] I am standing by the activation switch. Someone tell me when to throw it.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius workshop threshold, silver light intensifies
current_scene_description_before: The workshop interior comes into sharper focus: workbenches, tools, and a cage frame. Kaelen and Seraphis stand near the doorway as the welders’ hum softens. Vex Nereus, mid-weld, freezes, head lifting to hear the Aether’s thread weaving through the air. The space tightens with urgency as the thread suggests an intruding presence; metal glints, sparks die, and the crew readies to seal the connection.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The silver light on Aetherius has faded. The entity is gone — not defeated, but answered. Seraphis stands alone on the grey plain outside the workshop, the wind pulling at her hair.
