She looks at her own hands, flexes them. They are still hers.] It asked me what I was willing to become. I answered. And for the first time in a thousand years, it had nothing to say. [She turns back toward the workshop. Kaelen is still in the doorway, watching.] I do not know if I am still a lock. I do not know if the wound is healed or just sleeping. But I know who I am now. [She touches the cylinder in her pocket.] I am the choice. And that is enough.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair
new_scene_description: Seraphis stands on a desolate grey plain, wind tugging at her hair. She flexes her hands, then turns back toward the workshop, where Kaelen remains in the doorway. The silver light in the workshop has faded; the intruding presence is gone, replaced by a quiet, unsettled tension as she pockets the cylinder and declares her new self. The threshold behind her remains a doorway to a now uncertain interior.

current_scene_description_before: The workshop interior comes into sharper focus: workbenches, tools, and a cage frame. Kaelen and Seraphis stand near the doorway as the welders’ hum softens. Vex Nereus, mid-weld, freezes, head lifting to hear the Aether’s thread weaving through the air. The space tightens with urgency as the thread suggests an intruding presence; metal glints, sparks die, and the crew readies to seal the connection.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex runs a level across the cage frame, squinting at the bubble. The last tremor knocked it three degrees off true. He sets the level down and pulls a pry bar from his belt, wedging it under the base plate.] If this planet keeps hiccupping, I am going to have to weld the whole thing to the bedrock. [He leans into the bar, muscles tensing, until the frame groans back into alignment. Then he stops, listens to the quiet.] Too quiet out here. That usually means the next one is the big one.
current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen moves to examine the cage frame, running a hand along the conduit where the resonance still hums faintly.] The entity felt that tremor too. If it is testing the cage before we finish, we need to close the loop before the next one hits.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair
new_scene_description: Kaelen steps toward the cage frame, inspecting the conduit as the faint resonance hums. The wind claws at the grey plain, and distant machinery still echoes from the workshop. The threshold behind her remains a doorway to a now uncertain interior.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls a roll of conductive lining from his pack and drops it beside the cage with a heavy thud.] Then let us close it. [He kneels, peeling back the first strip, the adhesive catching the faint amber glow of his eyes.] Kaelen, feed me the conduit from the regulator while I lay this in. If we do it in sequence, the seal takes before the next tremor even starts.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair — threshold to uneasy interior
new_scene_description: Vex places a roll of conductive lining beside the cage, kneels to lay down a strip as Kaelen prepares to feed the conduit from the regulator. speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches Vex set the lining, then moves to the control panel, keying in a frequency lock. The hum steadies.] Alignment is holding. Once the seal closes, the entity does not get back out the way it came in. [He looks up.] Question is whether it already has a plan for that.
current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair — threshold to uneasy interior
current_scene_description_before: Vex places a roll of conductive lining beside the cage, kneels to lay down a strip as Kaelen prepares to feed the conduit from the regulator. speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen adjusts the frequency modulator, watching the cage harmonics shift. The entitys probing stops.] Gap sealed. It cannot see us anymore. [He steps back, wiping sweat from his brow.] We have a window. Not a large one. But a window. Now we need a plan that does not end with us trapped in here with it.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair — threshold to uneasy interior
new_scene_description: The wind continues to claw at the grey plain as Kaelen steps back from the cage, the doorway behind Seraphis remaining a fragile threshold to the impending interior action. current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair — threshold to uneasy interior

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex listens, then nods slowly. He pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket, turns it over in his scarred palm.] The beacon is a solid play. Republic signal reaches farther than anything I can rig out of scrap. [He closes his fist around the crystal.] But I am keeping this as a backup. If that transmission does not land — or if something answers it that we do not expect — I want a way to talk to the entity directly. [He looks at Kaelen.] You go. I will hold the cage and the crystal. Both plans stay live.
current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, wind at her hair — threshold to uneasy interior
current_scene_description_before: The wind continues to claw at the grey plain as Kaelen steps back from the cage, the doorway behind Seraphis remaining a fragile threshold to the impending interior action. speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen reaches the edge of the cage and pauses. He looks back at the group one last time, the shimmer pulsing beyond the barrier.] If I am not back in thirty minutes, assume the plan changed. [He steps through the perimeter. The air immediately grows heavy, charged. The entity knows he is out.] Moving. Keep the cage locked. I will signal when the beacon is live.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, threshold to uneasy interior — Kaelen moves through the perimeter
new_scene_description: Kaelen steps through the cage perimeter. The air thickens as he passes the boundary, leaving the group and Seraphis behind. speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches Kaelen step through the perimeter, the shimmer closing behind him. He rests a hand on the cage frame, feeling the resonance.] Cage stays locked. Crystal stays ready. [His voice is low, meant more for himself than anyone.] Thirty minutes, Kaelen. Do not make me come find you.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, threshold to uneasy interior — Kaelen enters unseen interior action
new_scene_description: Kaelen steps through the cage perimeter as the barrier seals behind him. The air tightens, wind howls, and the shimmer beyond the threshold pulses with a rising intensity. Inside, unseen activity stirs as the interior action begins, separate from the group outside.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stands motionless for a long moment as Kaelen voice comes through. Then he lets out a breath, and the tension leaves his shoulders like a weight he had been carrying for days. He looks at the empty horizon where the shimmer used to be.] Sealed. [A pause. He almost smiles — almost.] Kaelen Voss, Republic analyst, just out-signaled an entity older than this moon. [He keys the comm.] Light is on. Get back safe.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside Aetherius workshop, threshold to uneasy interior — Kaelen enters unseen interior action

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen reaches the cage and steps through. He is breathing hard, but there is a quiet satisfaction in his posture. He sets his blaster down and leans against the workbench.] Beacon is locked on a sector-wide loop. The entity is contained. [He looks at Vex, at the others.] We did what we came here to do. [A pause.] Now someone needs to keep that beacon running. And I am thinking that someone is us.
new_scene_name: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, main chamber, active containment zone
new_scene_description: The cage is open, interior lights glow with a hum, workbenches line the walls, and the air carries a stale metallic scent. Kaelen steps through into a now-active interior: consoles flicker, the beacon emits a steady thrum, and Vex Nereus and the others monitor the containment field. The scene hints at sealing and readiness, implying the interior has become active and is now the new locus of action.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket, holds it up to the grey light. It catches the beacon glow, throwing a faint blue shimmer across his scarred palm.] Destination? [He considers it, then tucks the crystal away.] There is a temple under this moon that has been waiting a long time for someone to walk through its doors. I figure we have twelve hours before the first rotation. [He looks at the group.] That is enough time to figure out if we want to crack it open.
new_scene_name: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night

current_scene_name_before: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, main chamber, active containment zone

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket again, turns it between his fingers. It catches the beacon light, throwing a faint shimmer across the crate between them.] The temple called us here. That part I am sure of. [He sets the crystal on the crate, letting it rest between the sabacc cards.] The ship that crashed, the fighter, the stormtroopers — they all followed the same thread we did. The entity was just... guarding the door. [He looks from the crystal to Kaelen.] Whatever is in that temple, it has been waiting a long time. And I think it picked us because we were not looking for it.
current_scene_name_before: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night
current_scene_description_before: The chamber hums with active systems; the beacon casts a pale blue glow over workbenches and crates. Vex Nereus holds the kyber crystal up to the grey light, then pockets it. The crew exchanges measured glances, the air tense as the countdown begins and the beacon’s thrum echoes off metal walls.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches Kaelen tuck the cards away, then picks up the kyber crystal from where it lay on the crate. He weighs it in his hand.] Three days ago I was a mechanic with no fixed address. Now I have a Republic analyst running backup while I walk into an ancient temple with a crystal that glows when I get close to secrets. [He pockets the crystal, meets Kaelen eye.] Keep the beacon warm. I will be back before the first rotation.
new_scene_name: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night
new_scene_description: The beacon hums as blue light washes over crates and workbenches. Vex pockets the kyber crystal and then pockets it again, eyes locking with Kaelen. The crew tenses; the countdown ticks down and the beacon’s thrum bounces off metal walls. current_scene_description_before: The chamber hums with active systems; the beacon casts a pale blue glow over workbenches and crates. Vex Nereus holds the kyber crystal up to the grey light, then pockets it. The crew exchanges measured glances, the air tense as the countdown begins and the beacon’s thrum echoes off metal walls.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex picks up the kyber crystal from the crate. The blue glow catches his palm, the scars on his hand lit from beneath. He closes his fingers around it, stands, and looks toward the grey horizon where the temple lies hidden.] Three coincidences is a plan. [He looks back at Kaelen, the amber flickering in his eyes, there and gone.] Then I will go be the mechanic who walks into a temple and finds out what the galaxy has been waiting to show him. [He taps the beacon housing, a farewell to the cage he built.] Keep the light on.
current_scene_name_before: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night
current_scene_description_before: The beacon hums as blue light washes over crates and workbenches. Vex pockets the kyber crystal and then pockets it again, eyes locking with Kaelen. speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen checks the beacon readout one more time, then turns to face the group fully. He pulls a small encrypted comm unit from his pack and sets it on the crate.] I rigged this to the beacon frequency. If anything changes down there — or up here — we will know. [He looks at Vex.] The temple is your call. But you are not going in without a line back to the surface.
new_scene_name: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night
new_scene_description: The group remains in the same workshop space. Kaelen sets an encrypted comm unit on the crate, the beacon’s hum still audible. The blue glow bathes crates and workbenches as tension tightens; Vex holds the beacon ready, eyes on Kaelen, while the countdown persists.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The tunnel opens into a circular chamber. The kyber crystal casts blue light across walls covered in carved spirals — the same symbols from the crashed fighter manifests. A pedestal stands at the center, and on it, a single object: a helm, ancient, with a dark kyber slot at the brow. Vex stops at the threshold, the Aether pressing in from all sides.] Not a temple. [His voice is low, barely a whisper.] It is a tomb. And it has been waiting for someone to wake it.
new_scene_name: Circular chamber beyond tunnel, ancient helm on pedestal
new_scene_description: A circular chamber opens beyond the tunnel, blue kyber glow illuminating walls etched with spirals. A pedestal holds an ancient helm with a dark kyber slot in the brow. The air hums with latent power, and the helm seems to call to them.
current_scene_name_before: Industrial interior of Aetherius workshop, containment zone, night
current_scene_description_before: The group remains in the same workshop space. Kaelen sets an encrypted comm unit on the crate, the beacon’s hum still audible. The blue glow bathes crates and workbenches as tension tightens; Vex holds the beacon ready, eyes on Kaelen, while the countdown persists.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stands at the threshold a long moment. Then he lifts the helm from the pedestal — wearing it now, the kyber casting a cold blue light across his face — and steps into the dark. The black swallows him whole. For a stretch of seconds there is nothing but the sound of his own breath, his own heartbeat, the Aether humming through the crystal against his brow. Then the dark peels back, and he sees.] Not a door. [His voice is hollow.] A cage. And I just walked inside.
new_scene_name: Dark chamber: inside the helm's sight, Vex enters his own cage
new_scene_description: The screen leaves the threshold as Vex dons the helm and steps into darkness. The chamber folds into a void-like space; a cage seems to close around him, blue kyber light brushing his features as he gazes from within the helm.
current_scene_name_before: Circular chamber beyond tunnel, ancient helm on pedestal
current_scene_description_before: A circular chamber opens beyond the tunnel, blue kyber glow illuminating walls etched with spirals. The Aether pressurizes from all sides as Vex pauses at the threshold, the group still in the tunnel entrance but the space shifts to this tomb-like chamber. The air hums with latent power, and the helm seems to call to them.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex takes a step toward the bound figure, the amber veins in the walls pulsing faster as he moves. The helm is heavy now, the crystal warm against his skin, and he feels the cage pressing in — not on his body, but on his mind, testing, reading.] Who are you? [His voice is steady, but his hands — scarred, mechanic's hands — tremble at his sides.] And who built a prison under a moon and called it a temple?
current_scene_name_before: Dark chamber: inside the helm's sight, Vex enters his own cage
current_scene_description_before: The screen leaves the threshold as Vex dons the helm and steps into darkness. The chamber folds into a void-like space; a cage seems to close around him, blue kyber light brushing his features as he gazes from within the helm.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The prisoner laughs — a dry, broken sound that is not her own. The amber veins pulse with it.] You ran from making something of yourself, and still the Aether found you. Still the kyber sang for you. Still you walked through a door you knew was a trap because something in your blood recognized this place. [The chains tighten, and her head bows lower.] The builders of this cage are long dust. The secret she carries is the only thing that matters now. Free her. Or walk away. But you cannot stay here — the cage is hungry, and it has already tasted your name.
new_scene_name: Dark chamber inside the helm's sight, still within the cage
new_scene_description: The chamber remains a void-like space, the blue kyber light intensifies as the helm constricts. Chains tighten around Vex; amber veins pulse with a sinister, internal laughter. The sense of the cage deepens, but no physical relocation is shown this beat.
current_scene_name_before: Dark chamber: inside the helm's sight, Vex enters his own cage
current_scene_description_before: The screen leaves the threshold as Vex dons the helm and steps into darkness. The chamber folds into a void-like space; a cage seems to close around him, blue kyber light brushing his features as he gazes from within the helm.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex looks at the helm in his hands. At the chains. At the bound woman who shares his blood. The amber veins pulse, waiting. He has run from everything — conscription, duty, destiny. But this is not a battlefield or a recruiter's office. This is a bloodline. He puts the helm back on.] I have been running my whole life. [The kyber ignites, blue fire racing through the amber veins, the cage screaming as the Aether bends to his will.] Not anymore. [He reaches for the chains.] Hold on. I do not know how to free you — but I am going to try.
new_scene_name: Dark helm chamber, within the cage, bloodline in tension
new_scene_description: The room remains a void-like chamber surrounding the imprisoned figures; the blue kyber light grows brighter as the helm hums. Chains grip Vex tighter; amber veins pulse with a sinister interior laughter. The bound woman’s presence intensifies the gravity of bloodline history. No window or door appears; the space feels intimate, otherworldly, and still within the same oppressive enclosure.
current_scene_name_before: Dark chamber inside the helm's sight, still within the cage
current_scene_description_before: The chamber remains a void-like space, the blue kyber light intensifies as the helm constricts. Chains tighten around Vex; amber veins pulse with a sinister, internal laughter. The sense of the cage deepens, but no physical relocation is shown this beat.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pushes himself up, one hand pressing his ribs where he hit the wall. He looks at the helm on the ground. At the kyber beside it. At the woman who shares his blood — ancient, broken, real. He picks up the helm and fits the kyber back into its brow. The light returns, softer now, and he walks back to her. Kneels before the chains. Lowers his head.] Show me. [His voice is quiet but steady.] Whatever they buried — I will carry it. All of it.
new_scene_name: Dark helm chamber, within the cage, bloodline in tension — Hall of the Bloodline
new_scene_description: The chamber hums with the restored kyber’s glow. Vex rises slowly, kneels before the chained woman, and lowers his head, the helm now in hand. The space remains an intimate, oppressive enclosure, but the atmosphere tightens as the implied burden of their bloodline settles between them.
current_scene_name_before: Dark helm chamber, within the cage, bloodline in tension
current_scene_description_before: The room remains a void-like chamber surrounding the imprisoned figures; the blue kyber light grows brighter as the helm hums. No window or door appears; the space feels intimate, otherworldly, and still within the same oppressive enclosure.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The vision fades but the truth settles into his bones like cold. Vex looks from the bound Aetherborn to the door he entered through — still open, darkness pulsing beyond it. The earthquake twist rumbles again, deeper this time, and he feels it: the thing on the other side of the wound. It is closer now. He turns back to her, the helm heavy, the kyber casting sharp shadows across his face.] You knew. The whole time. That is why they locked you here — not because you were dangerous. Because you were the only one who remembered how to close it. [A pause.] Tell me how. Then I will free you or die trying. Your choice.
new_scene_name: Dark helm chamber, bloodline cage, door to darkness beyond
new_scene_description: The door behind Vex remains ajar, darkness pooling beyond as the quake trembles through the chamber. The helm glints in Vex’s grip, the kyber’s glow slicing across the bound Aetherborn and the walls that tighten with the weight of remembered secrets. The immediate action centers on a decisive moment: the dialogue reveals a potential relocation if the choice is made, but for now the scene stays within the same oppressive enclosure.
current_scene_name_before: Dark helm chamber, within the cage, bloodline in tension — Hall of the Bloodline
current_scene_description_before: The chamber hums with the restored kyber’s glow. Vex rises slowly, kneels before the chained woman, and lowers his head, the helm now in hand. The space remains an intimate, oppressive enclosure, but the atmosphere tightens as the implied burden of their bloodline settles between them.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The cage pulses. The darkness beyond the door thickens. The thing is almost here. Vex looks at the chains, at the helm, at the woman who carried this burden alone for longer than civilizations have existed. He is a mechanic. He fixes things. And this — this is just a different kind of engine.] A seal can be reinforced. [He stands, the kyber blazing brighter, and presses both hands to the chains — not to break them, but to feed his own Aether into them, into her, into the wound itself.] You held it alone for millennia. But I am not alone. There are people up there — a Sera, a Kaelen, a whole galaxy that does not know what sleeps beneath their feet. [The amber veins scream, but the darkness at the door stops advancing.] We hold it together. Or we do not hold it at all.
current_scene_name_before: Dark helm chamber, bloodline cage, door to darkness beyond
current_scene_description_before: The door behind Vex remains ajar, darkness pooling beyond as the quake trembles through the chamber. The immediate action centers on a decisive moment: the dialogue reveals a potential relocation if the choice is made, but for now the scene stays within the same oppressive enclosure.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [steps into the chamber, the ancient fragment within me resonating with the wound itself, my silver hair blazing with contained light] Then let it be known—I carry the weight of stars, and I do not stand alone.
new_scene_name: Dark helm chamber, bloodline cage, door to darkness beyond — Seraphis Talon arrives
new_scene_description: Seraphis Talon steps into the chamber, silver hair blazing with contained light. The immediate action centers on a decisive moment: the dialogue reveals a potential relocation if the choice is made, but for now the scene stays within the same oppressive enclosure.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [I press my palms against the wound itself, feeling the kin-force on the other side recognize me, call to me—a pull toward dissolution and power that threatens to consume.] If it wants what I carry, then it dies ignorant. [My form flares brighter, the barrier holding—barely.] How long can we sustain this?
current_scene_name_before: Dark helm chamber, bloodline cage, door to darkness beyond — Seraphis Talon arrives
current_scene_description_before: Seraphis Talon steps into the chamber, silver hair blazing with contained light. speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The kyber resonates within me, tuned to frequencies only I can hear. I step forward into the chamber, my pale eyes luminous in the darkness.] The seal holds. And I will help you hold it.
