The sacred forge crackles as blue flame licks the air; Pyros looks up from the bronze tripod, the crowd around them tense. Selene speaks with gravity, holding the bone fragment, the bearing of revelations clear. The ground tremors continue, the air thick with foreboding as the line between mortal toil and divine omens narrows toward an unspoken collision.
current_scene_name_before: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau near Olympus gates
current_scene_description_before: The stage shifts to a moment of action at the sacred forge and the upper grounds. Pyros kneels beside a bronze tripod, fingers tracing a new hairline crack; the sacred flame sputters and steadies amid tremors felt throughout the mountainous precinct. Figures gather on the temple steps as the earth groans from beneath Helepolis, and the Pythia’s cry echoes in the dark. The air remains charged with foreboding as the pursuit accelerates toward an unseen collision between mortal toil and divine omens.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus pushes off from the column he's been leaning against, the dead caduceus still tucked in his belt. He studies the blue flame with narrowed eyes.] I've seen a lot of strange things this week. A mountain erupt. A wave freeze mid-air. A thing that wore gods like sandals. [He glances at Kassandra.] But a flame that forgets its own color? That's new. And I don't like new. New means I don't have a price for it yet.
new_scene_name: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau — Melanthus interrupts
new_scene_description: Melanthus rises from the column, the blue flame flaring briefly as he studies it. The crowd tightens; dust and ash drift in the dawn light. The sacred site holds its breath as the caduceus at his belt clinks softly. He speaks with wary defiance, the cogs of fate visibly turning as he challenges the omen.
current_scene_name_before: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau near Olympus gates — Selene confronts Pyros
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps forward on the temple steps, the trident-mark on her palm glowing faintly. The sacred forge crackles as blue flame licks the air; Pyros looks up from the bronze tripod, the crowd around them tense. Selene speaks with gravity, holding the bone fragment, the bearing of revelations clear. The ground tremors continue, the air thick with foreboding as the line between mortal toil and divine omens narrows toward an unspoken collision.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus's hand goes to the dead caduceus at his belt as the tripod shudders. He meets Kassandra's grey eyes.] 'What comes after.' You say that like you've already seen it, priestess. [A pause.] I've spent my whole life trading in what people will pay for. But you're talking about something no one can price — the end of prophecy itself. If the threads are snapping, then the loom isn't broken. Someone's cutting the strings. The question is who's holding the shears.
new_scene_name: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau — tension escalates
new_scene_description: The air hums with omen as Melanthus’s fingers brush the dead caduceus. The tripod shivers; ash drifts in the pale dawn. Kassandra’s gaze is unflinching, the crowd holding its breath as prophecy tightens around them. The sacred site vibrates with low thunder of fate’s loom about to be tested.
current_scene_name_before: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau — Melanthus interrupts
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus rises from the column, the blue flame flaring briefly as he studies it. The crowd tightens; dust and ash drift in the dawn light. The sacred site holds its breath as the caduceus at his belt clinks softly. He speaks with wary defiance, the cogs of fate visibly turning as he challenges the omen.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [He sets his jaw, looking from the violet flame to the temple, then down the long slope toward the distant sea.] What we do with what we found. [A pause.] I have spent forty years keeping one fire alive. If the mountain is asking, then the mountain already knows the answer. We do not stay here and wait for it to find us again. We go to where the threads lead. The harbor. The burning ships. The faceless figure. [He taps his chest.] I will forge the way if I have to beat it out of the earth myself.
new_scene_name: Harbor approach at dawn, hillside to seafront under ash-lit horizon
new_scene_description: The view shifts from the temple’s ascent to a rugged slope that descends toward a dawn-lit harbor. The violet flame flickers as Pyros locks eyes with the sea, then nods toward a distant, smoke-wreathed skyline of ships and a shadowed, faceless figure. Wind carries ash and the scent of salt; the crowd remains, but the mood shifts to urgent pursuit of a path beyond the sacred site.
current_scene_name_before: Temple threshold, dawn to Summit plateau — tension escalates
current_scene_description_before: The air hums with omen as Melanthus’s fingers brush the dead caduceus. The tripod shivers; ash drifts in the pale dawn. Kassandra’s gaze is unflinching, the crowd holding its breath as prophecy tightens around them. The sacred site vibrates with low thunder of fate’s loom about to be tested.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron steps forward, the tablet in his hand, his voice rough but certain.] I was there. I saw the throne, the serpent coils of Pontus, the face that did not fit. The skeleton was not dead — it was dormant. And the thing sitting on it was not the first mask it wore. It was the last one it needed. [He looks at the blue flame, then down toward the harbor.] The path below the sanctuary is the same path I dreamt of for twenty years. That is where it entered. That is where it will leave.
new_scene_name: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, storm-worn rail and ship masts
new_scene_description: The crowd shifts with urgency as Theron steps forward from the gathered huddle, tablet in hand. The dawn light glints off weathered dock timbers and the smoke-wreathed skyline of ships. Salt air and ash mix, harbor cranes creak, and the distant sea seems to pull toward the new threat. The immediate focus is on Theron delivering a revelation, while Pyros eyes the water and the shadowed skyline, all sensing a move toward a deeper, perilous path.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor approach at dawn, hillside to seafront under ash-lit horizon
current_scene_description_before: The view shifts from the temple’s ascent to a rugged slope that descends toward a dawn-lit harbor. The violet flame flickers as Pyros locks eyes with the sea, then nods toward a distant, smoke-wreathed skyline of ships and a shadowed, faceless figure. Wind carries ash and the scent of salt; the crowd remains, but the mood shifts to urgent pursuit of a path beyond the sacred site.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [He waits at the bend, letting the silence settle, then speaks softer than before.] I forged the Pythia's tripod when I was thirty years old. I have never been more than a day's walk from Delphi since. The mountain has been my cage and my sanctuary. [He looks down at the ash in his leather wrap.] But the fire in this cloth does not want to stay here any longer. And neither do I.
new_scene_name: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, Pyros' revelation
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to the docks themselves as Pyros speaks softly, the ash-laden dawn tightening around him. The air grows heavier with the weight of his confession; the water, smoky and restless, seems to pulse with a new, inexorable intent. Theron remains in frame, tablet still raised, while the crowd holds its breath and the horizon compacts toward a single, dangerous path forward.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, storm-worn rail and ship masts
current_scene_description_before: The crowd shifts with urgency as Theron steps forward from the gathered huddle, tablet in hand. The dawn light glints off weathered dock timbers and the smoke-wreathed skyline of ships. Salt air and ash mix, harbor cranes creak, and the distant sea seems to pull toward the new threat. The immediate focus is on Theron delivering a revelation, while Pyros eyes the water and the shadowed skyline, all sensing a move toward a deeper, perilous path.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron meets Melanthus's gaze, a flicker of something — respect, or recognition — passing between them. He turns without another word and starts down the steps, staff finding each stone with the certainty of a man who has walked this path in his sleep. Over his shoulder, low enough that only those following will hear it.] I did not speak of it because I was not certain the dream was real until the flame turned blue. Now I am. Keep the ash close, smith. We will need light where we are going.
new_scene_name: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, Pyros' revelation (Spear: Theron descends toward steps)
new_scene_description: Theron turns and starts down the steps, staff clicking on stone; the crowd parts slightly as he moves, the ash-streaked dawn faintly brightening the path ahead. The water churns with a restless, smoky glow, while Pyros’s confession lingers in the air like embers. Theron’s back is to the audience as he exits the frame, leaving the moment momentarily focused on the rising tension between ash and flame.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, Pyros' revelation
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to the docks themselves as Pyros speaks softly, the ash-laden dawn tightening around him. The air grows heavier with the weight of his confession; the water, smoky and restless, seems to pulse with a new, inexorable intent. Theron remains in frame, tablet still raised, while the crowd holds its breath and the horizon compacts toward a single, dangerous path forward.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Kneels at the water's edge, running his scarred fingers through the wet sand. The leather pouch of ash hangs from his neck, warm against his chest.] I walked through the night. The fire in this pouch never dimmed — it grew brighter the closer I came to the salt. [He looks up at the empty harbor, the burned hulls of fishing boats half-sunk in the shallows.] This is the place from the smoke. But the figure is not here. It is waiting.
new_scene_name: Harbor edge at dawn, water’s edge with ash-lit horizon
new_scene_description: Pyros kneels at the water’s edge, fingers in wet sand. The ash-dimmed dawn glows over a harbor with burned hulls half-sunk in the shallows; a quiet, tense shoreline. Theron’s back remains to the audience as he recedes from view, while Pyros speaks to an empty harbor, the pouch warm against his chest.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor approach at dawn, docks beneath ash-lit horizon, Pyros' revelation (Spear: Theron descends toward steps)
current_scene_description_before: Theron turns and starts down the steps, staff clicking on stone; the crowd parts slightly as he moves, the ash-streaked dawn faintly brightening the path ahead. The water churns with a restless, smoky glow, while Pyros’s confession lingers in the air like embers. Theron’s back is to the audience as he exits the frame, leaving the moment momentarily focused on the rising tension between ash and flame.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Steps down barefoot onto the marble, voice hoarse.] The silence has settled over this mountain like a shroud. I have been listening — to the stone, the air, the space where the Pythia's voice used to live. The threads have not frayed further. They are waiting. [She meets each gaze in turn.] Whatever wears the sea god's face is patient. It will wait until we leave these steps and walk into its domain. The question is whether we go together — or let it pick us apart one by one.
new_scene_name: Marble stair landing, mountain temple doorway
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps onto a marble platform at the temple’s edge, barefoot and breath ragged. The air is cool and thick with incense; torches flicker along a stone balcony overlooking a shadowed valley. The harbor’s ash-dawn remains audible as distant memory, but here the space is still and cavernous, lines of columns and carved reliefs framing a new threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor edge at dawn, water’s edge with ash-lit horizon
current_scene_description_before: Pyros kneels at the water’s edge, fingers in wet sand. The ash-dimmed dawn glows over a harbor with burned hulls half-sunk in the shallows; a quiet, tense shoreline. Theron’s back remains to the audience as he recedes from view, while Pyros speaks to an empty harbor, the pouch warm against his chest.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus falls into step behind Theron, the dead caduceus swinging at his hip. He glances at Kassandra as they walk.] 'A grim respect' — I'll take that. Most priestesses just curse my name and walk away. [His eyes track the two forks ahead.] The crypt or the dark. What do you see down there, seer? Because my trade has taught me one thing: darkness that absolute is never empty.
new_scene_name: Marble stair landing, temple threshold, advancing to crypt or dark below
new_scene_description: Melanthus brushes in behind Theron, caduceus at his hip. The air remains thick with incense; torches burn along carved columns as two paths fork ahead. The sense of ascent gives way to a steeper, shadowed descent toward a crypt and a darkness that feels almost tangible.
current_scene_name_before: Marble stair landing, mountain temple doorway
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra steps onto a marble platform at the temple’s edge, barefoot and breath ragged. The air is cool and thick with incense; torches flicker along a stone balcony overlooking a shadowed valley. The harbor’s ash-dawn remains audible as distant memory, but here the space is still and cavernous, lines of columns and carved reliefs framing a new threshold.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene steps to the water's edge beside Pyros, boots sinking into the wet sand. She looks at the leather pouch of ash at his neck and the distant horizon where the sea meets a bruised sky.] The mountain fell silent because it recognized what we carried back. That ash at your neck, forge-master — I carried the bone of Pontus through that same wave. The world is not ending. It is remembering what it was before the gods named it.
new_scene_name: Shoreline by the sea under bruised sky, at water's edge with Pyros
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the wet sand beside Pyros, boots sinking; the distant horizon where sea meets a bruised sky. The ash pouch at Pyros’ neck glints, a tether between past and memory. The air is cool and loud with distant surf as the sea keeps its ancient vigil.
current_scene_name_before: Marble stair landing, temple threshold, advancing to crypt or dark below
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus brushes in behind Theron, caduceus at his hip. The air remains thick with incense; torches burn along carved columns as two paths fork ahead. The sense of ascent gives way to a steeper, shadowed descent toward a crypt and a darkness that feels almost tangible.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Unlaces the pouch from his neck and holds it up — the ash inside glows violet, casting his scarred face in sharp relief.] I forged a tripod for a god I never met. Tended a fire for a voice I never heard speak. If this mountain was my cage, old man, then I want to see what the world looks like from the outside. [He steps past Theron into the dark, the ash-pouch the only light.] Lead. I will keep the fire burning.
new_scene_name: Shoreline by the sea under bruised sky, Pyros steps into darkness with ash-pouch lighting the way
new_scene_description: The shore remains, but Pyros steps past Theron and into darkness beyond the shore’s edge. The glow from the ash pouch casts violet light on Pyros’ scarred face as he steps forward, leaving the visible sea and sky behind for an unseen darkness ahead. Water licks the sand where they stood; the air tightens with the abrupt move into an unseen realm.
current_scene_name_before: Shoreline by the sea under bruised sky, at water's edge with Pyros
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the wet sand beside Pyros, boots sinking; the distant horizon where sea meets a bruised sky. The ash pouch at Pyros’ neck glints, a tether between past and memory. The air is cool and loud with distant surf as the sea keeps its ancient vigil.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron steps back from the basin, giving the others room. The blue light from Pyros's ash casts their shadows long against the curved walls. Five shadows. But the basin casts a sixth — a shadow with no one standing behind it.] The room is not empty. It has been waiting for us to arrive. [He looks at Selene.] This is your mark on the stone. Yours would be the hand it recognizes.
new_scene_name: Adjacent chamber within the temple
new_scene_description: Theron steps back from a basin in a curved-walled chamber. Blue light from Pyros's ash silhouettes five human shadows on the walls, while a sixth shadow—unnumbered and empty—appears cast by the basin itself. The room feels prepped, waiting for a signal or mark. The basin remains, breathing with quiet doubt as Selene and the others stand ready.
current_scene_name_before: Shoreline by the sea under bruised sky, Pyros steps into darkness with ash-pouch lighting the way
current_scene_description_before: The shore remains, but Pyros steps past Theron and into darkness beyond the shore’s edge. The glow from the ash pouch casts violet light on Pyros’ scarred face as he steps forward, leaving the visible sea and sky behind for an unseen darkness ahead. Water licks the sand where they stood; the air tightens with the abrupt move into an unseen realm.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene does not turn to look at the basin. Instead she looks down at her own shadow, cast by the blue ash-light — solid, familiar, hers. Then she counts.] I walked through the trench with Pontus' bone in my belt. I learned that shadows are not always cast by bodies. Some are cast by time that has not finished happening. [She looks at the sixth shadow, her voice steady.] That one is not dead. It has not been born yet.
new_scene_name: Temple chamber, adjacent, still within the same space
new_scene_description: The scene remains in the curved-walled temple chamber. Theron stands by the basin as blue-lit shadows dance on the walls; Selene speaks in a calm, revealing tone about time, shadows, and a sixth shadow that is not dead nor born yet. The air is heavy with anticipation, and the basin breathes softly as distant drums echo in the temple.
current_scene_name_before: Adjacent chamber within the temple
current_scene_description_before: Theron steps back from a basin in a curved-walled chamber. Blue light from Pyros's ash silhouettes five human shadows on the walls, while a sixth shadow—unnumbered and empty—appears cast by the basin itself. The room feels prepped, waiting for a signal or mark. The basin remains, breathing with quiet doubt as Selene and the others stand ready.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene steps toward the basin, her reflection not quite matching her movement — a half-beat delay, as if the water is seeing her a moment before she arrives.] A thread that has been cut does not vanish. It falls. And what falls into water leaves a ripple. [She looks at Melanthus.] That shadow is not showing us what we will become. It is showing us what we are walking toward. The room is not empty because nothing is here. It is empty because it is waiting for us to arrive.
new_scene_name: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, same space
new_scene_description: Selene moves toward the basin with a delayed reflection, the water's surface rippling as if foreseeing her arrival. The basin breathes softly; blue shadows flicker along the curved walls. The chamber remains hushed and expectant, drums echoing from unseen depths as the air tightens with the sense that something is approaching.
current_scene_name_before: Temple chamber, adjacent, still within the same space
current_scene_description_before: The scene remains in the curved-walled temple chamber. Theron stands by the basin as blue-lit shadows dance on the walls; Selene speaks in a calm, revealing tone about time, shadows, and a sixth shadow that is not dead nor born yet. The air is heavy with anticipation, and the basin breathes softly as distant drums echo in the temple.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron's staff taps the stone floor once, a sharp sound that cuts through the chamber's hush.] Time is not stuttering, priestess. It is being threaded. This room is the loom. The basin is the shuttle. And the hand that reaches into it will choose which pattern follows. [He looks at Selene, then at the water that shows her a half-beat behind herself.] The reflection is not late. It is early. It is showing you who you will have to become to walk out of this room.
new_scene_name: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, evening -- ritual loom
new_scene_description: The chamber tightens with the sense of imminent weaving. Selene and Theron stand before the basin; water breathes, shadows flicker along the curved walls, drums echo as if from the depths. The stone floor bears Theron’s staff imprint; the air thickens with threaded time, a subtle wind lifting dust motes into pale light.
current_scene_name_before: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, same space
current_scene_description_before: Selene moves toward the basin with a delayed reflection, the water's surface rippling as if foreseeing her arrival. The basin breathes softly; blue shadows flicker along the curved walls. The chamber remains hushed and expectant, drums echoing from unseen depths as the air tightens with the sense that something is approaching.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus stops circling the basin and faces Theron directly.] A decision that hasn't been made yet. [He repeats the words, testing them.] I've spent my life making decisions based on what people will pay. But this — [he gestures at the shadow] — this is the first time I've seen the price before the choice. [He looks at the reflection that doesn't match, then back at the group.] So what's the decision, old priest? And who among us is going to make it?
new_scene_name: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, evening – ritual loom (Melanthus faces Theron)
new_scene_description: Melanthus stops circling the basin and faces Theron directly. The chamber hums with the loom’s unseen threads; water breathes in the basin, shadows cling to the curved walls, and drums echo from depths. The air tightens as if woven, dust motes glow in pale light. A tense, charged stillness settles over the trio as Melanthus weighs the cost in the reflection that doesn’t align with their figures.
current_scene_name_before: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, evening -- ritual loom
current_scene_description_before: The chamber tightens with the sense of imminent weaving. Selene and Theron stand before the basin; water breathes, shadows flicker along the curved walls, drums echo as if from the depths. The stone floor bears Theron’s staff imprint; the air thickens with threaded time, a subtle wind lifting dust motes into pale light.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron meets Melanthus's gaze, the staff steady in his hand. When he speaks, his voice is quiet — the voice of a man who has spent thirty years listening for answers in the silence between waves.] The decision is whether we touch the basin and let it show us the pattern — or whether we leave it untouched and let the pattern choose itself. And I have already made my choice. I did not walk through a god's abandoned throne room to stop at the threshold of a basin.
new_scene_name: Temple chamber near the basin, evening; Theron declares choice
new_scene_description: Theron meets Melanthus’s gaze, staff steady. The loom hums with unseen threads; water breathes in the basin, shadows cling to the curved walls, drums echo. Theron’s voice cuts the stillness with resolve: he has chosen to act, not merely observe, and the chamber tightens around their decision.
current_scene_name_before: Temple chamber, adjacent to the basin, evening – ritual loom (Melanthus faces Theron)
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus stops circling the basin and faces Theron directly. The chamber hums with the loom’s unseen threads; water breathes in the basin, shadows cling to the curved walls, and drums echo from depths. The air tightens as if woven, dust motes glow in pale light. A tense, charged stillness settles over the trio as Melanthus weighs the cost in the reflection that doesn’t align with their figures.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus steps up to the basin. The water is perfectly still — unnaturally so. He looks at his own reflection, then at the sixth shadow cast by no one.] Theron called this a loom. I've never seen a loom, but I know a scale when I see one. [He fishes a silver coin from his pouch and holds it over the water.] Every deal has a price. Every thread has a cutter. [He lets the coin hang, waiting.] So who reaches in?
new_scene_name: Temple basin chamber, evening; Melanthus at the basin with a coin
new_scene_description: Melanthus steps to the basin; water sits unnaturally still as he scrutinizes his reflection and the sixth, unseen shadow. He sinks a silver coin over the water, pausing with it suspended, the loom’s hum and chamber ambience pressing in. Theron’s choice lingers in the air, awaiting the first hand to reach in.
current_scene_name_before: Temple chamber near the basin, evening; Theron declares choice
current_scene_description_before: Theron meets Melanthus’s gaze, staff steady. The loom hums with unseen threads; water breathes in the basin, shadows cling to the curved walls, drums echo. Theron’s voice cuts the stillness with resolve: he has chosen to act, not merely observe, and the chamber tightens around their decision.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene steps past Theron and Melanthus and stops at the basin's edge. She does not look at the water. She looks at the sixth shadow — the one that has no owner, the one waiting.] The hand that reaches in. [She holds Pontus's bone over the water. The blue light from Pyros's pouch catches the fragment and the basin begins to hum.] I have walked through three versions of the same story. I am ready to see which one was written for me to finish.
new_scene_name: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night; Selene at water’s edge with Pontus's bone
new_scene_description: Selene steps to the basin’s edge, eyes fixed on the unowned sixth shadow. She hovers Pontus’s bone over the water as a blue glow from Pyros's pouch illuminates the fragment; the basin begins to hum in response. Theron and Melanthus remain nearby, the air charged with expectancy as the hand—Selene’s or the shadow’s—fades toward the water.
current_scene_name_before: Temple basin chamber, evening; Melanthus at the basin with a coin
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus steps to the basin; water sits unnaturally still as he scrutinizes his reflection and the sixth, unseen shadow. He sinks a silver coin over the water, pausing with it suspended, the loom’s hum and chamber ambience pressing in. Theron’s choice lingers in the air, awaiting the first hand to reach in.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus straightens as the crack spreads, drinking the light. His hand goes to the dead caduceus — not drawing it, but ready.] An opening. [He repeats the word, flat.] Every opening I've ever walked through led to a room I couldn't leave the same way. [He looks at the basin, at the crack drinking the glow.] Whatever's on the other side of that crack — it's been waiting since before this place was built. And it just got its invitation.
new_scene_name: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night – crack spreading, Melanthus prepares
new_scene_description: The chamber’s air tightens as the crack widens, light draining into the breach. Melanthus stands taller, hand nearing the dead caduceus, eyes fixed on the glowing fissure where the basin’s hum intensifies. Selene remains at the water’s edge, Pontus’s bone poised, while Theron and Melanthus exchange wary glances, the space crackling with an almost sentient expectancy.
current_scene_name_before: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night; Selene at water’s edge with Pontus's bone
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps to the basin’s edge, eyes fixed on the unowned sixth shadow. She hovers Pontus’s bone over the water as a blue glow from Pyros's pouch illuminates the fragment; the basin begins to hum in response. Theron and Melanthus remain nearby, the air charged with expectancy as the hand—Selene’s or the shadow’s—fades toward the water.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron retrieves his staff from the wall and walks toward the crack. It is narrow — shoulder-width — and the light from Pyros's ash cannot penetrate more than a few feet. He does not hesitate.] A door that only opens once. That is what I have been dreaming of. Not the room beyond. The moment of choosing to enter it. [He looks back once, at Selene, at the others.] I will be on the other side. Join me when you are ready. Or do not. But the crack will not wait.
new_scene_name: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night – Theron prepares to step through crack
new_scene_description: Theron steps away from the wall, retrieves his staff, and advances toward the narrow crack. The glow from Pyros's ash barely penetrates the few feet of the widening fissure. The air hums with tension as the others watch, the space vibrating with a choice that must be made now.
current_scene_name_before: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night – crack spreading, Melanthus prepares
current_scene_description_before: The chamber’s air tightens as the crack widens, light draining into the breach. Melanthus stands taller, hand nearing the dead caduceus, eyes fixed on the glowing fissure where the basin’s hum intensifies. Selene remains at the water’s edge, Pontus’s bone poised, while Theron and Melanthus exchange wary glances, the space crackling with an almost sentient expectancy.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Moves to the water basin, her grey eyes fixed on the surface that shows things a heartbeat before they happen.] I have seen this before — not in a vision, but in the space between visions. The delay is not in the water. It is in us. Time is bending around this place. Not breaking. Bending. And what bends can either snap back — or stay bent. I do not know which outcome we are walking toward. [She looks up at the group.] But I know the choice will come soon.
new_scene_name: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night — group around the crack
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps toward the water basin, gaze intent on the surface. The basin mirrors flickers of coming events, ripples stuttering as she speaks. The chamber holds its breath: Theron at the crack, Pyros’s ash glow thinning, the air taut with an imminent, fateful decision.
current_scene_name_before: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night – Theron prepares to step through crack
current_scene_description_before: Theron steps away from the wall, retrieves his staff, and advances toward the narrow crack. The glow from Pyros's ash barely penetrates the few feet of the widening fissure. The air hums with tension as the others watch, the space vibrating with a choice that must be made now.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene stands at the crack that was not there before, the blue light behind her casting her shadow long into the dark corridor. She touches the edge of the opening — it is warm, like living stone.] Kassandra is right. Time is bending. The delay in the water is not the water. It is this place catching up to what it was always meant to become. [She looks into the dark.] And Theron is right too. A door that opens once does not care if you are ready. It only cares that you are there.
new_scene_name: Temple corridor beyond the crack, blue light casting long shadows
new_scene_description: Selene stands at a new crack in the wall, blue illumination behind her, shadow stretching into a dark corridor. She touches the warm edge of the opening, as if living stone. The group remains in the basin chamber, but the air shifts: a new doorway materializes, and the corridor beyond hums with potential. The water in the basin shivers as if reacting to a new threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Temple basin chamber, edge of basin, night — group around the crack
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra steps toward the water basin, gaze intent on the surface. The basin mirrors flickers of coming events, ripples stuttering as she speaks. The chamber holds its breath: Theron at the crack, Pyros’s ash glow thinning, the air taut with an imminent, fateful decision.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [From the threshold of the crack, Theron's voice comes back — not louder, but clearer, as if the narrow stone focuses it.] The bending is not a symptom, priestess. It is the working of the door. What bends here does not snap back. It opens. [He turns sideways and steps into the dark. The blue light catches his profile for a moment — the ruined eye, the set of his jaw — and then he is gone, swallowed by the crack. Only his voice remains, echoing from somewhere deeper.] The bone in the basin is the key. Selene knows what to do with it.
new_scene_name: Temple corridor beyond the crack, entering the threshold
new_scene_description: Theron steps through the crack into the blue-lit corridor beyond, his voice echoing. Selene remains in the basin chamber; the doorway remains, blue light pulsing. The air between the spaces hums with crossing thresholds, and water in the basin shivers in response to the doorway’s awakening.
current_scene_name_before: Temple corridor beyond the crack, blue light casting long shadows
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at a new crack in the wall, blue illumination behind her, shadow stretching into a dark corridor. She touches the warm edge of the opening, as if living stone. The group remains in the basin chamber, but the air shifts: a new doorway materializes, and the corridor beyond hums with potential. The water in the basin shivers as if reacting to a new threshold.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [From the crack's threshold, Theron's voice comes back — clearer, focused by the stone.] The bending is not a symptom, priestess. It is the door opening. [He turns sideways and steps into the dark. The blue light catches his profile — the ruined eye, the set of his jaw — and then he is gone. Only his voice remains, from deeper inside.] The bone in the basin is the key. Selene knows what to do with it.
new_scene_name: Blue-lit corridor beyond the threshold, Theron steps into the depths
new_scene_description: Theron’s voice trails into the blue-lit corridor beyond the crack. The doorway hums, blue light pulsing as he steps sideways into darkness. His profile briefly glints—the ruined eye, set jaw—before he vanishes deeper inside. The echo of his voice remains, guiding Selene, while the basin chamber holds its vigil.
current_scene_name_before: Temple corridor beyond the crack, entering the threshold
current_scene_description_before: Theron steps through the crack into the blue-lit corridor beyond, his voice echoing. Selene remains in the basin chamber; the doorway remains, blue light pulsing. The air between the spaces hums with crossing thresholds, and water in the basin shivers in response to the doorway’s awakening.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus watches Theron disappear into the crack without hesitation. He lets out a short breath, half laugh, half resignation.] A blind priest walking into a crack in the world that drinks light. [He looks at the others, the dead caduceus swinging as he steps forward.] I've followed worse omens. [He pauses at the threshold, glancing back.] If I don't come back, tell my father I finally found a door he didn't leave through first.
new_scene_name: Blue-lit corridor beyond the threshold, Theron’s departure echoed; Melanthus steps toward the crack
new_scene_description: The scene shifts as Melanthus moves toward the crack in the world, the blue corridor fading behind Theron. The air tightens, lights pulse, and the blind priest crosses into the crack, the dead caduceus swinging at his side. The group remains at the threshold, the basin chamber’s vigil intact as the sounds of the crack swallow Melanthus' footsteps.
current_scene_name_before: Blue-lit corridor beyond the threshold, Theron steps into the depths
current_scene_description_before: Theron’s voice trails into the blue-lit corridor beyond the crack. The doorway hums, blue light pulsing as he steps sideways into darkness. His profile briefly glints—the ruined eye, set jaw—before he vanishes deeper inside. The echo of his voice remains, guiding Selene, while the basin chamber holds its vigil.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [On the other side of the crack, the passage opens into a vast natural chamber. The ceiling is lost in darkness. At the center, a spiral of obsidian pillars descends into the floor like the vertebrae of a buried serpent. Theron stands at the edge, staff held loose, his clouded eye catching glimmers from a source that gives no light — a cold luminescence rising from the spiral's heart.] The dream ended at the basin. This — [he gestures with his staff at the chamber] — I have never seen before. The bone was not the first key. It was the last lock. And we have just broken it.
new_scene_name: Natural obsidian chamber beyond the crack, Theron on the edge
new_scene_description: Theron stands at the edge of a vast natural chamber whose ceiling dissolves into darkness. A central spiral of obsidian pillars descends into the floor, emitting a cold, otherworldly glow from its heart. The basin remains behind, but the dream has carried Theron to a new, unknowable space as the group’s vigil recedes; the staff rests loose in his grip, eye unfocused yet intent.
current_scene_name_before: Blue-lit corridor beyond the threshold, Theron’s departure echoed; Melanthus steps toward the crack
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts as Melanthus moves toward the crack in the world, the blue corridor fading behind Theron. The air tightens, lights pulse, and the blind priest crosses into the crack, the dead caduceus swinging at his side. The group remains at the threshold, the basin chamber’s vigil intact as the sounds of the crack swallow Melanthus' footsteps.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene turns back from the crack's threshold, the blue light from the basin catching her face in sharp relief. She does not look surprised by Kassandra's words.] I pressed Pontus' bone against that creature's chest on Olympus. It screamed like something being born, not something dying. [Her voice is deliberate.] It was not digesting my father. It was carrying him. And now I carry the bone of what it was before it learned to carry anything. [She steps through the crack.] If you want to understand it, come see what it built.
new_scene_name: Natural obsidian chamber beyond the crack, Theron and Selene entering a crucible-like space
new_scene_description: Selene steps through the crack, blue light bright on her face as she leaves the current edge of the obsidian chamber. The space beyond remains dim and unknowable, with the crack narrowing into a slender portal. Theron feels a shift in the air as the line of vision stretches toward a darker core, while the other vigil participants recede from view. The staff still rests in Theron’s grip, but the scene now centers on Selene’s entrance and the new interior beyond the threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Natural obsidian chamber beyond the crack, Theron on the edge
current_scene_description_before: Theron stands at the edge of a vast natural chamber whose ceiling dissolves into darkness. A central spiral of obsidian pillars descends into the floor, emitting a cold, otherworldly glow from its heart. The basin remains behind, but the dream has carried Theron to a new, unknowable space as the group’s vigil recedes; the staff rests loose in his grip, eye unfocused yet intent.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron's voice comes from the darkness of the chamber as Selene steps through. He is somewhere near the spiral — the cold light catches the edge of his staff, then his profile.] Carrying. Not digesting. [He repeats the word, testing its weight.] That changes everything. The bone in your belt is not a trophy. It is the first thing it ever carried. A hatchling carries nothing but instinct. But a parent carries something it means to protect. [He steps into the faint glow, his ruined eye fixed on the spiral.] We are not in its stomach. We are in its nest.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, nearing the spiral nest
new_scene_description: Selene steps fully through the crack into a dim, egg‑shaped chamber lit by cold blue glow. The space beyond is a tense blend of smooth obsidian walls, a spiraling core at the center, and faint, shifting runes along the surfaces. Theron remains near the edge of the threshold, the staff in his grip catching the pale light as he speaks from the darkness, his silhouette framed by the glow of the spiral. The air hums with a dry, mineral scent; the crack behind them seals softly as if closing to witnesses.
current_scene_name_before: Natural obsidian chamber beyond the crack, Theron and Selene entering a crucible-like space
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps through the crack, blue light bright on her face as she leaves the current edge of the obsidian chamber. The space beyond remains dim and unknowable, with the crack narrowing into a slender portal. Theron feels a shift in the air as the line of vision stretches toward a darker core, while the other vigil participants recede from view. The staff still rests in Theron’s grip, but the scene now centers on Selene’s entrance and the new interior beyond the threshold.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Her grey eyes are fixed on the middle distance, seeing threads the others cannot.] I know what you are going to ask. Is there a way out? There is always a way out. But the door is not on this mountain. [She finally looks at the speaker, and her voice drops.] Theron carried something from the sea that the imposter does not want him to have. That tablet is the door. And the only reason the imposter has not taken it by force is that it cannot be taken. It must be given. We need to find Theron before it convinces someone to make the offer.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core at center, near threshold
new_scene_description: The scene remains in the dim, egg-shaped obsidian chamber. The air hums; Theron’s silhouette remains at the threshold edge as Kassandra speaks of Theron’s stolen tablet and an inevitable offering. The pale spiral glows, runes shift faintly, and the crack behind them seals softly. The focus shifts to the looming notion of negotiation and pursuit within this same space.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, nearing the spiral nest
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps fully through the crack into a dim, egg‑shaped chamber lit by cold blue glow. The space beyond is a tense blend of smooth obsidian walls, a spiraling core at the center, and faint, shifting runes along the surfaces. Theron remains near the edge of the threshold, the staff in his grip catching the pale light as he speaks from the darkness, his silhouette framed by the glow of the spiral. The air hums with a dry, mineral scent; the crack behind them seals softly as if closing to witnesses.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron steps out of the shadow of the nearest obsidian pillar. He is closer to the spiral's heart now — close enough that the cold light illuminates the tablet in his hand, the cracked clay catching an answering glow from the depths.] The temple was not the first building on this spot. There was another. A smaller one. Older. Built by people who did not worship the gods because they had not yet forgotten what lived beneath them. [He taps the tablet.] This was written in that older temple. The priestess who carved it was not warning us about the imposter. She was telling us how to put it back to sleep.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core; nearer to the heart
new_scene_description: Theron steps forward from the shade of a pillar, closer to the spiral’s core. The cold glow from the heart lights the tablet in his hand as the surrounding obsidian walls hum. The space feels more intimate, the air thick with revelation, yet the chamber remains the same obsidian room with the threshold alive to shifting purpose.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core at center, near threshold
current_scene_description_before: The scene remains in the dim, egg-shaped obsidian chamber. The air hums; Theron’s silhouette remains at the threshold edge as Kassandra speaks of Theron’s stolen tablet and an inevitable offering. The pale spiral glows, runes shift faintly, and the crack behind them seals softly. The focus shifts to the looming notion of negotiation and pursuit within this same space.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus steps through the crack into the obsidian chamber. The pillars rise like ribs around him, and he lets out a low whistle.] A quarantine. They walled this place off and called it a temple. [He runs a hand along one of the obsidian pillars, feeling the cold.] Which means whatever they were quarantining is still here. It never left. It's just been waiting for the seal to weaken. [He looks at Theron's silhouette in the dark.] And you already knew that, didn't you, old priest?
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core; higher alert within the same obsidian room
new_scene_description: The crack opens and Melanthus steps into the obsidian chamber. Pillars rise like ribs around him; the air thickens with the chill of the heart-lit space. The temple’s quarantine feel intensifies as he moves among the pillars, the hum of the walls answering his steps. Theron remains nearby, their silhouettes catching the heartlight as the space tightens with revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core; nearer to the heart
current_scene_description_before: Theron steps forward from the shade of a pillar, closer to the spiral’s core. The cold glow from the heart lights the tablet in his hand as the surrounding obsidian walls hum. The space feels more intimate, the air thick with revelation, yet the chamber remains the same obsidian room with the threshold alive to shifting purpose.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene stops near the obsidian pillar where Theron stands, the tablet visible in his hand. She does not reach for it.] You said the temple was not the first building on this spot. [Her voice is low, deliberate.] I walked through a wave that was older than Poseidon. I carried a bone that was older than the sea god who ruled it. If there was a building here before the temple — who built it? And why did they bury it under a god's house?
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – Selene confronts the past
new_scene_description: Selene steps up near Theron, the tablet in Theron’s hand momentarily visible. The obsidian chamber hums softly as the air grows heavier, shadows nudging at the pillars that rise like ribs. Selene’s question cuts through the space, a pointer to an older memory buried beneath the god’s house.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core; higher alert within the same obsidian room
current_scene_description_before: The crack opens and Melanthus steps into the obsidian chamber. Pillars rise like ribs around him; the air thickens with the chill of the heart-lit space. The temple’s quarantine feel intensifies as he moves among the pillars, the hum of the walls answering his steps. Theron remains nearby, their silhouettes catching the heartlight as the space tightens with revelation.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus eyes the tablet in Theron's hands, the cracked clay catching the dim light. He lets out a breath that's half amusement, half frustration.] You walked in here like a man following a dream, and now you pull out a tablet you've been carrying for thirty years. [He shakes his head slowly.] You weren't following a memory, old priest. You were following instructions.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – Selene confronts the past, Melanthus confronts Theron
new_scene_description: The obsidian chamber remains, Theron still holds the cracked tablet catching dim light. Melanthus stands in the glow, voice steady but edged with disapproval. The air tightens as the tablets’ markings seem to shimmer, and the scene tightens on accusation rather than revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – Selene confronts the past
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps up near Theron, the tablet in Theron’s hand momentarily visible. The obsidian chamber hums softly as the air grows heavier, shadows nudging at the pillars that rise like ribs. Selene’s question cuts through the space, a pointer to an older memory buried beneath the god’s house.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She takes a step into the crypt shadows, voice echoing back to the group.] The crack in the seal will widen. It will not stop. The question is whether we go down there together while we still have a choice — or wait until the floor opens beneath our feet and we fall whether we want to or not. I have seen that fall in my visions. There is no bottom to it.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – crypt shadows advance
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps into the crypt shadows, voice echoing back to the group. The air grows heavier as the seal’s crack seems to pulse, and the space tilts between warning and dare. The others watch, the crack widening in the dim light, a choice looming: descend together or wait for a collapse that might start now.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – Selene confronts the past, Melanthus confronts Theron
current_scene_description_before: The obsidian chamber remains, Theron still holds the cracked tablet catching dim light. Melanthus stands in the glow, voice steady but edged with disapproval. The air tightens as the tablets’ markings seem to shimmer, and the scene tightens on accusation rather than revelation.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus into his belt and takes a step toward the spiral. The cold pulse from its heart washes over him, and he doesn't flinch.] Talk is cheap. I've been selling it my whole life. [He glances back at the others.] Whatever truth is at the center of that spiral — I've carried a dead god's charm long enough. Let's see if this place will take it.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – crypt shadows deepen
new_scene_description: Melanthus steps toward the spiral, the air cold and the crack humming louder. The group steadies as the chamber tilts and the pulse from the heart of the spiral washes over him. The cave-like crypt breathes, shadows lengthen, and the path toward the core gleams with an ominous glow.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – crypt shadows advance
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra steps into the crypt shadows, voice echoing back to the group. The air grows heavier as the seal’s crack seems to pulse, and the space tilts between warning and dare. The others watch, the crack widening in the dim light, a choice looming: descend together or wait for a collapse that might start now.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene walks to the center of the spiral and kneels opposite Theron, the tablet glowing between them. The carvings pulse in sequence, and she feels the trident-mark responding — matching the rhythm.] This was never a prison. It was a lock. And the key was not the tablet — it was the truth we all had to carry separately until we arrived here together. [She looks at Theron.] You held the tablet. Melanthus held the question. Kassandra held the vision. Pyros held the fire. I held the bone. None of it meant anything alone.
new_scene_name: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – center of spiral confrontation
new_scene_description: Selene kneels at the center of the spiraling core, tablet between them. The carvings pulse in sequence, the trident-mark responding. The chamber hums with a new, unified rhythm as the group faces a revealed truth; shadows settle, the path to the core glows with a more intimate, shared purpose.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – crypt shadows deepen
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus steps toward the spiral, the air cold and the crack humming louder. The group steadies as the chamber tilts and the pulse from the heart of the spiral washes over him. The cave-like crypt breathes, shadows lengthen, and the path toward the core gleams with an ominous glow.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She steps fully into the crypt entrance now, her voice carrying back over her shoulder, already fading into shadow.] You feel it too — the cold that is not cold. The silence that is not empty. It knows we are here. It has been waiting for us since before any of us were born. Do not follow me because I am right. Follow me because if I go alone, I will not come back. And neither will the answer.
new_scene_name: Crypt entrance beyond Obsidian chamber, shadowed threshold
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps completely into the crypt entrance, voice trailing into the dim shadow. The air grows colder, the ground hums with the core’s resonance, and the group feels the pull of a decision that could separate them from the path to the center. The path before them glows faintly, inviting a solitary stride or a united advance toward the unknown.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian chamber threshold, spiraling core – center of spiral confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Selene kneels at the center of the spiraling core, tablet between them. The carvings pulse in sequence, the trident-mark responding. The chamber hums with a new, unified rhythm as the group faces a revealed truth; shadows settle, the path to the core glows with a more intimate, shared purpose.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus steps fully into the spiral's light, the dead caduceus held loosely at his side. He addresses Kassandra directly.] Then speak it, seer. You know what it is. Name it. [His voice is steady, but there's an edge beneath it — the herald who's tired of riddles.] I've traded secrets my whole life. I know when someone's holding the one that matters. And you've been holding this one since we stepped into this chamber.
new_scene_name: Crypt entrance beyond Obsidian chamber, shadowed threshold (spiral light spill)
new_scene_description: Melanthus steps fully into the spiral's light, the dead caduceus at his side. He faces Kassandra and fixes her with a steady, edged gaze, demanding a name for the burden they both sense. The air tightens, the path behind them dimming as if listening, while the glow before them pulses with choice.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt entrance beyond Obsidian chamber, shadowed threshold
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra steps completely into the crypt entrance, voice trailing into the dim shadow. The air grows colder, the ground hums with the core’s resonance, and the group feels the pull of a decision that could separate them from the path to the center. The path before them glows faintly, inviting a solitary stride or a united advance toward the unknown.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene rises from the spiral, leaving the dark tablet in its cradle. She looks at the path Kassandra took — a corridor of obsidian that drinks the light.] She is right. It knows we are here. It has always known. [She takes a step into the corridor, the trident-mark glowing steady.] The forgetting was never the hunger. I met the hunger. I restored it. Whatever is deeper than the spiral is what the hunger was running from.
new_scene_name: Corridor of Obsidian light outside Crypt entrance
new_scene_description: Selene rises from the spiral, stepping into the corridor of obsidian that drinks the light. The corridor hums with a quiet, watching darkness as the trident-mark glows. The air carries the memory of hunger and the weight of what lies deeper than the spiral. A muted wind moves along the corridor, carrying the echo of voices from the crypt beyond.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt entrance beyond Obsidian chamber, shadowed threshold (spiral light spill)
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus steps fully into the spiral's light, the dead caduceus at his side. He faces Kassandra and fixes her with a steady, edged gaze, demanding a name for the burden they both sense. The air tightens, the path behind them dimming as if listening, while the glow before them pulses with choice.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus reaches out and touches the crypt wall. His fingers meet cold, porous surface — unmistakably bone. He lets his hand rest there a moment, then speaks.] I've walked the edges of the underworld. I've seen the bones of heroes, Titans, things that should not have names. [He looks at his palm, then ahead into the dark.] But this — this is not a burial. This is the thing itself. We're not walking toward a door. We're walking through its ribs.
new_scene_name: Corridor of Obsidian light outside Crypt entrance, still in the crypt wall touching moment
new_scene_description: Melanthus touches the crypt wall; the bone-like surface is cold and porous. He speaks of underworld bones and the thing itself, the corridor's dim hum continues, and the sense of passage remains; the setting is the obsidian corridor adjacent to the crypt, still in the same physical space but focusing on the contact with the wall and ominous implication of moving through its ribs.
current_scene_name_before: Corridor of Obsidian light outside Crypt entrance
current_scene_description_before: Selene rises from the spiral, stepping into the corridor of obsidian that drinks the light. The corridor hums with a quiet, watching darkness as the trident-mark glows. The air carries the memory of hunger and the weight of what lies deeper than the spiral. A muted wind moves along the corridor, carrying the echo of voices from the crypt beyond.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus stops, processing. He turns to face Selene, the dead caduceus catching what little light remains.] It was afraid. The thing that wore gods like masks, that drank the prophecy fire, that cut the threads — was afraid of what's in this crypt. [A pause.] That means we're not walking into something that wants to eat us. We're walking into something that made the hunger run.
current_scene_name_before: Corridor of Obsidian light outside Crypt entrance, still in the crypt wall touching moment
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus touches the crypt wall; the bone-like surface is cold and porous. He speaks of underworld bones and the thing itself, the corridor's dim hum continues, and the sense of passage remains; the setting is the obsidian corridor adjacent to the crypt, still in the same physical space but focusing on the contact with the wall and ominous implication of moving through its ribs.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene walks to where Kassandra stands, her hand pressed against empty air. She stops beside her and presses her own palm — the one with the trident-mark — to the same invisible surface. The mark flares, and for an instant, the door becomes visible: a seam of light tracing the shape of a massive doorway in the bone wall.] A door that only opens once. [She looks at Kassandra.] Then let us open it together. Speak the name. I will carry what comes after.
new_scene_name: Obsidian corridor, near crypt entrance – portal seam appears on bone wall
new_scene_description: Selene and Kassandra press palms to the invisible surface. The bone wall glows along a seam that resolves into a massive doorway. Light leaks through the opening, though the door is said to open only once. The obsidian corridor hums louder as the doorway briefly reveals itself, still in the same crypt-adjacent space, tension tight as a vow.
current_scene_name_before: Corridor of Obsidian light outside Crypt entrance, still in the crypt wall touching moment
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus touches the crypt wall; the bone-like surface is cold and porous. He speaks of underworld bones and the thing itself, the corridor's dim hum continues, and the sense of passage remains; the setting is the obsidian corridor adjacent to the crypt, still in the same physical space but focusing on the contact with the wall and ominous implication of moving through its ribs.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The silver door pulses once, then swings inward. Darkness beyond — but not empty darkness. The kind of dark that watches back.] The door is open. The imposter fled through it the moment the name was spoken. It is running not from me — but toward the half of itself that was sealed away. If they merge, there will be no stopping what they become. [She steps through the silver doorframe, her figure dissolving into the dark.] I will find them. Follow the sound of my voice — I will leave the threads where I walk.
new_scene_name: Obsidian corridor to silver doorframe – Kassandra steps into darkness
new_scene_description: The silver doorframe remains in the obsidian corridor. Kassandra steps through, her silhouette dissolving into the dark as the door swings shut behind her. The air hums, then silence and a lingering echo of her vow ripple through the bone-lit crypt. Selene stands alone, the seam still glow-worn, the path forward now shrouded in watching darkness.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor, near crypt entrance – portal seam appears on bone wall
current_scene_description_before: Selene and Kassandra press palms to the invisible surface. The bone wall glows along a seam that resolves into a massive doorway. Light leaks through the opening, though the door is said to open only once. The obsidian corridor hums louder as the doorway briefly reveals itself, still in the same crypt-adjacent space, tension tight as a vow.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The thread-world shimmers. The black thread turns gold under her touch, and the absence screams — not in pain, but in release. A sound like the first breath of a newborn world.] It is done. The black thread is gold. The imposter is no longer an imposter. It remembers what it was before the split. Not a god of hunger. A god of dawn. [She opens her eyes in the crypt, the silver door fading behind her. She stands alone, covered in golden dust, breathing.] The sea is healing. The flame at Delphi will burn gold again by morning. And the threads... the threads are whole.
new_scene_name: Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, precinct after door fades
new_scene_description: Kassandra stands alone inside a crypt, now dusted in gold, the silver door fading behind her. The air is still, the bone-lit walls catching a pale, dawn-hued glow as the thread-world stabilizes. Selene’s presence feels distant yet attentive, the whispers of a healed seam lingering in the quiet. A sense of dawn and restoration spreads through the chamber, with Delphi’s flame promised to burn gold by morning.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor to silver doorframe – Kassandra steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: The silver doorframe remains in the obsidian corridor. Kassandra steps through, her silhouette dissolving into the dark as the door swings shut behind her. The air hums, then silence and a lingering echo of her vow ripple through the bone-lit crypt. Selene stands alone, the seam still glow-worn, the path forward now shrouded in watching darkness.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She leans against the temple wall, watching the gold flame cast warm light across the marble.] The threads are quiet now. For the first time in weeks, I cannot see the future. I cannot see anything beyond this moment. And for a seer, that is the greatest gift there is. [She looks at each of them — Selene, Theron, Melanthus, Pyros.] Whatever comes next, we face it as equals. Not as mortals and demigods. Not as priests and outcasts. As people who walked into the dark together and found a door on the other side.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, precinct after door fades
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands alone inside a crypt, now dusted in gold, the silver door fading behind her. The air is still, the bone-lit walls catching a pale, dawn-hued glow as the thread-world stabilizes. Selene’s presence feels distant yet attentive, the whispers of a healed seam lingering in the quiet. A sense of dawn and restoration spreads through the chamber, with Delphi’s flame promised to burn gold by morning.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene steps onto the temple steps, the gold flame warming her salt-crusted skin. She looks at the altar, at the fire burning true again, then at Kassandra.] I walked through the wave and followed the thread to the bottom of the sea. But it was you who saw it through to the end. [She touches the bone fragment at her belt, now quiet, cold.] The imposter is gone. My father's face is no longer worn by something else. I should feel relief. [A pause.] Instead I feel the silence where his presence used to be.
new_scene_name: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn light at temple entrance
new_scene_description: Selene steps onto stone temple steps, dawn light warming her salt-crusted skin. The altar inside glows faintly from a true flame; the scene shifts to the exterior steps where the sea-salt wind brushes her. The air feels charged with a quiet, healing energy as the thread-world’s instability settles. Kassandra remains inside, the door fading behind her, while Selene speaks from the threshold, the implied sea beyond and the temple’s outer glow framing her—a moment between arrival and departure.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, precinct after door fades
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands alone inside a crypt, now dusted in gold, the silver door fading behind her. The air is still, the bone-lit walls catching a pale, dawn-hued glow as the thread-world stabilizes. Selene’s presence feels distant yet attentive, the whispers of a healed seam lingering in the quiet. A sense of dawn and restoration spreads through the chamber, with Delphi’s flame promised to burn gold by morning.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene settles on the step below Kassandra, the gold fire warm on her back. She looks out at the sea, flat and calm under an evening sky.] Delphi is lucky to have you. A seer who has seen the threads break and mend is worth more than a hundred who have only read the unbroken ones. [She pauses.] I do not know where I go from here. My father is gone. My fleet is ash. The only thing I have left is the bone of a dead god and a question I answered at the bottom of a trench.
new_scene_name: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn light fades to evening, sea calm
new_scene_description: Selene settles on the step below Kassandra, the warm glow of the gold fire behind her. She gazes out at a flat, calm sea under an evening sky. The air carries a quiet, healing energy as the surrounding temple exterior holds still; the door to the Crypt remains behind them, the implied sea beyond and the temple’s glow framing the moment.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn light at temple entrance
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps onto stone temple steps, dawn light warming her salt-crusted skin. The altar inside glows faintly from a true flame; the scene shifts to the exterior steps where the sea-salt wind brushes her. The air feels charged with a quiet, healing energy as the thread-world’s instability settles. Kassandra remains inside, the door fading behind her, while Selene speaks from the threshold, the implied sea beyond and the temple’s outer glow framing her—a moment between arrival and departure.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene nods, looking at the gold flame, then at the others — Melanthus leaning against a column, Pyros by his forge, Theron at the temple edge.] You are right. The silence is not empty. It is the sound of five people who walked into the dark and came out the other side still breathing. [She looks at Kassandra.] That is rarer than prophecy.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn light fades to evening, sea calm
current_scene_description_before: Selene settles on the step below Kassandra, the warm glow of the gold fire behind her. She gazes out at a flat, calm sea under an evening sky. The air carries a quiet, healing energy as the surrounding temple exterior holds still; the door to the Crypt remains behind them, the implied sea beyond and the temple’s glow framing the moment.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene rises, the gold flame warm on her back one last time. She looks at each of them in turn — Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra.] I came to Delphi with nothing but a question. I leave with more than I can carry. [She smiles, just slightly.] If the threads ever knot again, call. I will hear it. Wherever the sea takes me. [She turns and walks down the steps toward the harbor.]
new_scene_name: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn fading to evening, harbor bound departure
new_scene_description: Selene rises from the step, warmth from the gold flame at her back. She locks eyes with each of them—Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra—then smiles faintly. She speaks of leaving Delphi with more than she came with, offering a promise to hear any future call, and turns to walk down the steps toward the harbor, the sea gleaming beyond the temple threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn light fades to evening, sea calm
current_scene_description_before: Selene settles on the step below Kassandra, the warm glow of the gold fire behind her. She gazes out at a flat, calm sea under an evening sky. The air carries a quiet, healing energy as the surrounding temple exterior holds still; the door to the Crypt remains behind them, the implied sea beyond and the temple’s glow framing the moment.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene stops at the edge of the steps, Kassandra's words catching her. She looks down at her palm — the trident-mark, which has burned and ached and glowed, is quiet. Steady. Warm, but not hot. She looks back at Kassandra, a crease between her brows.] It feels different. I thought it was just the distance from the trench. But it is not the sea that changed. [She touches her palm to her chest.] It is me. The mark is not a curse anymore. It is just a scar.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn fading to evening, harbor bound departure
current_scene_description_before: Selene rises from the step, warmth from the gold flame at her back. She locks eyes with each of them—Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra—then smiles faintly. She speaks of leaving Delphi with more than she came with, offering a promise to hear any future call, and turns to walk down the steps toward the harbor, the sea gleaming beyond the temple threshold.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She sets the broom aside and walks to the altar, trailing her fingers through the gold flame. It does not burn her. It never did.
new_scene_name: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn fading to evening, harbor bound departure
new_scene_description: Kassandra sets the broom aside and walks to the altar, trailing her fingers through the gold flame. It does not burn her. It never did.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn fading to evening, harbor bound departure
current_scene_description_before: Selene rises from the step, warmth from the gold flame at her back. She locks eyes with each of them—Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra—then smiles faintly. She speaks of leaving Delphi with more than she came with, offering a promise to hear any future call, and turns to walk down the steps toward the harbor, the sea gleaming beyond the temple threshold.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene reaches the temple steps, breath quick. She passes Kassandra at the altar without stopping, heading for the passage that leads to the crypt. Over her shoulder:] Pyros went below. He found a heartbeat. Not an echo — a living pulse in the stone. If there is something still breathing under Delphi after everything we uncovered, I need to see it with my own eyes. [She disappears into the dark.]
new_scene_name: Temple steps to passage toward crypt
new_scene_description: Selene crosses the temple steps, breath quick, moving past the altar toward the crypt passage. She vanishes into the darkness of the doorway, leaving the harbor-bound departure behind. The temple hums with a faint, unknowable energy as the flame-light gutters.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps outside Crypt of the Obsidian Threshold, dawn fading to evening, harbor bound departure
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra sets the broom aside and walks to the altar, trailing her fingers through the gold flame. It does not burn her. It never did.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She rises from the temple steps, the gold flame flaring as if in answer to the distant pressure Selene feels.] The mountain speaks, and the sea hears it. Pyros has found a door beneath the door — a root of the first temple. Something older than the absence. Something that was sleeping until the gold flame woke it. [She looks toward the harbor, though she cannot see Selene from here.] Do not go after him alone. The threads are weaving a pattern that needs all of us.
new_scene_name: Harbor outskirts, temple precinct to harbor edge
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to the open harbor area just beyond the temple precinct. Moonlight gleams on the water as distant waves lap the pilings; the air carries a briny tang. Kassandra stands at the edge of the quay, the gold flame bright in her hand, while the temple steps recede into darkness behind her. The harbor remains quiet except for the distant creak of timbers and the indistinct murmur of the sea.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps to passage toward crypt
current_scene_description_before: Selene crosses the temple steps, breath quick, moving past the altar toward the crypt passage. She vanishes into the darkness of the doorway, leaving the harbor-bound departure behind. The temple hums with a faint, unknowable energy as the flame-light gutters.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She steps into the chamber, gold light from her palm meeting the violet glow. At the center, Pyros stands before a massive shape — not a chest, but a cocoon. Translucent. Pulsing. Something curled inside it, breathing in slow rhythm.
new_scene_name: Chamber inside temple-city harbor, central cocoon chamber
new_scene_description: The action slides into a dim inner chamber within the temple complex; violet glow mingles with the gold light emanating from Kassandra’s palm as she steps closer to a translucent cocoon at the chamber’s center. Pyros stands before the cocoon, looming and alarmingly quiet, while the surrounding altars and carved reliefs reflect the mingled lights. The air hums with a tremor beneath the surface of the breathing shape.
current_scene_name_before: Harbor outskirts, temple precinct to harbor edge
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts to the open harbor area just beyond the temple precinct. Moonlight gleams on the water as distant waves lap the pilings; the air carries a briny tang. Kassandra stands at the edge of the quay, the gold flame bright in her hand, while the temple steps recede into darkness behind her. The harbor remains quiet except for the distant creak of timbers and the indistinct murmur of the sea.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She keeps her hand on the cocoon. The rhythm of the pulse slows to match her heartbeat. Selene arrives at the threshold behind her, breath caught. Kassandra does not turn.] It knows all of us now. It has tasted our presence through the threads. Selene of the healed sea. Pyros of the undying spark. And me — the one who sees. It is not afraid. It is curious.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold with Selene arriving
new_scene_description: Selene arrives at the threshold behind Kassandra, breath catching. Kassandra remains facing the cocoon, hand still on its surface as the pulse slows to match her heartbeat. The chamber hums with the shared resonance of their names and presence, the violet-gold glow wrapping tighter around the cocoon.
current_scene_name_before: Chamber inside temple-city harbor, central cocoon chamber
current_scene_description_before: The action slides into a dim inner chamber within the temple complex; violet glow mingles with the gold light emanating from Kassandra’s palm as she steps closer to a translucent cocoon at the chamber’s center. Pyros stands before the cocoon, looming and alarmingly quiet, while the surrounding altars and carved reliefs reflect the mingled lights. The air hums with a tremor beneath the surface of the breathing shape.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene emerges from the crypt last, the night air cool on her face. She sees Kassandra on the temple steps with the presence — a warmth, a stillness — learning the names of stars. Selene stops at a distance, not wanting to interrupt. She speaks quietly, more to herself than anyone.] It was under us the whole time. A question that took the shape of a heart, waiting for someone to ask it. And we almost left without ever knowing.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold with Selene arriving
new_scene_description: Selene steps into the threshold, the night air brushing her face. She pauses a short distance from Kassandra on the temple steps, the violet-gold glow and heartbeat resonance stilling in the cocoon’s surface. The space feels momentarily paused as Selene grapples with the quiet revelation, her presence forming a new hush in the chamber.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold with Selene arriving
current_scene_description_before: Selene arrives at the threshold behind Kassandra, breath catching. Kassandra remains facing the cocoon, hand still on its surface as the pulse slows to match her heartbeat. The chamber hums with the shared resonance of their names and presence, the violet-gold glow wrapping tighter around the cocoon.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Selene sits. The presence extends toward her — not a hand, but a warmth that wraps around her like a wave recognizing kin. Kassandra watches, and says nothing. There are some moments that do not need words.]
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold with Selene arriving
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps into the threshold, the night air brushing her face. She pauses a short distance from Kassandra on the temple steps, the violet-gold glow and heartbeat resonance stilling in the cocoon’s surface.
