The space feels momentarily paused as Selene grapples with the quiet revelation, her presence forming a new hush in the chamber.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene hesitates for a breath, then crosses the temple steps and lowers herself to sit beside Kassandra, within reach of the presence's warmth. She does not speak at first. She simply sits, letting the stillness settle around her. Then, quietly:] I have spent so long fighting, running, carrying. I forgot what it felt like to just be seen. [She looks at the presence, a faint smile.] You are learning stillness from us. I think I need to learn it from you.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold to cocoon presence, Selene sits beside Kassandra
new_scene_description: Selene crosses the temple steps and sits beside Kassandra, within reach of the cocoon’s warmth. The hush deepens as she settles, her gaze toward the cocoon presence. The chamber retains its violet-gold glow and palpable heartbeat, though now the two women share the quiet.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold with Selene arriving
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps into the threshold, the night air brushing her face. She pauses a short distance from Kassandra on the temple steps, the violet-gold glow and heartbeat resonance stilling in the cocoon’s surface. The space feels momentarily paused as Selene grapples with the quiet revelation, her presence forming a new hush in the chamber.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The earthquake has passed, but the stones remember. Selene descends the Delphic steps, her scarred palm still glowing faintly. She stops at the edge of the golden flame.] You tend a flame through the night. I used to think that was hope. Now I know it is vigilance. [She sits on a cracked step, the tablet from the trench still tucked at her belt.] The gates of Olympus have closed again. I saw what lived inside them. And what fled. [She looks at the flame-keeper.] You feel it too. Something older than the gods, walking between their thrones.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold to cocoon presence, Selene sits beside Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: Selene crosses the temple steps and sits beside Kassandra, within reach of the cocoon’s warmth. The hush deepens as she settles, her gaze toward the cocoon presence. The chamber retains its violet-gold glow and palpable heartbeat, though now the two women share the quiet.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The earthquake has passed. Selene descends the Delphic steps, scarred palm still glowing. She stops at the edge of the golden flame.] You tend a flame through the night. I used to think that was hope. Now I know it is vigilance. I saw what lived on Olympus. And what fled. Something older than the gods walks between their thrones.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold to cocoon presence, Selene at cocoon edge
new_scene_description: Selene steps from the cocoon vicinity, descending the Delphic steps. Her scarred palm glows as she reaches the edge of the golden flame, the chamber’s violet-gold glow and heartbeat subtly shifting to match the new proximity to the flame and the cocoon’s presence. The air carries a quiet tension as she speaks of vigilance and ancient watchers.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold to cocoon presence, Selene sits beside Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: Selene crosses the temple steps and sits beside Kassandra, within reach of the cocoon’s warmth. The hush deepens as she settles, her gaze toward the cocoon presence. The chamber retains its violet-gold glow and palpable heartbeat, though now the two women share the quiet.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She rises from the cracked step, brushing dust from her salt-stained chiton. The golden flame flickers as she passes — drawn to her, or warning her away, she cannot tell. She stops at the edge of the temple portico, where the earthquake has left a hairline fracture running up the marble.] The ground is speaking. First the wave, then the tremor, now this fissure. [She traces the crack with her finger.] Something is rising. Not a god. Gods do not crack the earth to announce themselves. [She turns back toward the flame and the one who tends it.] I am going down. Into the rift. If something is climbing up, I want to meet it halfway.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber/portico fracture, edge of temple with rising fissure
new_scene_description: Selene steps from the cocoon vicinity onto the temple portico’s fractured marble. The air tightens as the golden flame flickers, casting violet-gold shimmer on cracked stone. The ground speaks through a widening fissure that runs up the marble, tremors echoing Proposition of something rising. Selene turns back to the flame and the keeper of the flame, then declares her descent toward the rift, preparing to meet whatever approaches halfway.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, threshold to cocoon presence, Selene at cocoon edge
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps from the cocoon vicinity, descending the Delphic steps. Her scarred palm glows as she reaches the edge of the golden flame, the chamber’s violet-gold glow and heartbeat subtly shifting to match the new proximity to the flame and the cocoon’s presence. The air carries a quiet tension as she speaks of vigilance and ancient watchers.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She meets Kassandra's gaze across the flame. A single nod.] I was already on my way. [She steps past the temple portico and onto the cracked earth where the rift opens into darkness below Delphi. The trident-mark on her palm glows, casting pale light down the stone steps.] Pyros found a chamber. I found a tablet that is older than Olympus. If there is a ribcage beneath this mountain, I want to see what it was built to hold. [She begins the descent.] Keep the flame burning. I will follow the light of what I find.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth at rift opening beneath Delphi
new_scene_description: Selene steps from the portico onto cracked earth, the rift opening below Delphi glowing. The flame's violet-gold light presses against rough stone as she begins to descend into darkness, palm’s trident-mark pulsing pale light along the steps. The air tightens, tremors deepen, and the glow of the flame remains fixed as she moves toward the widening abyss.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber/portico fracture, edge of temple with rising fissure
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps from the cocoon vicinity onto the temple portico’s fractured marble. The air tightens as the golden flame flickers, casting violet-gold shimmer on cracked stone. The ground speaks through a widening fissure that runs up the marble, tremors echoing Proposition of something rising. Selene turns back to the flame and the keeper of the flame, then declares her descent toward the rift, preparing to meet whatever approaches halfway.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She pauses at the top of the stone steps, the dark of the crypt rising to meet her. She looks back at Kassandra over her shoulder, the trident-mark casting her face in pale light.] I saw a throne at the bottom of the sea. Coral and bone. And on it, a thing wearing my father's face like a theatre mask. It was hollow — the mask, not the thing behind it. That was still hungry. [She turns, facing the dark.] It fled Olympus when I caught up. It is somewhere in the earth now. I think that is what Pyros is standing on top of.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth at rift opening beneath Delphi, Selene on steps
new_scene_description: Selene pauses at the top of the stone steps, the dark crypt rising to meet her. She glances back at Kassandra, pale light from the trident-mark playing across their faces. The rift beneath Delphi glows, the air tense, and the abyss looms closer as she contemplates the vision of a throne beneath the sea and a masklike, hungry thing wearing her father’s face. The surrounding light and shadows press in as she steels herself to descend.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth at rift opening beneath Delphi
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps from the portico onto cracked earth, the rift opening below Delphi glowing. The flame's violet-gold light presses against rough stone as she begins to descend into darkness, palm’s trident-mark pulsing pale light along the steps. The air tightens, tremors deepen, and the glow of the flame remains fixed as she moves toward the widening abyss.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She stops at the threshold. The dark below breathes cold against her ankles, but Kassandra's voice holds her one more moment. Selene turns, studying the presence — no, feeling it. Her scarred palm lifts, not in defense, but in acknowledgment.] A thing that emerged from a chrysalis, with no name and no hunger. [A pause.] In my language, that is almost unheard of. Almost everything born into this world wants something. [She lets her hand fall.] If it survives until I return from below, I will know what to call it. Names are not given. They are earned. [She steps into the dark.] Wait for me, flame-keeper.
new_scene_name: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth beneath Delphi
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the threshold on the temple steps, the abyss yawning below as the rift glows. Cold air brushes her ankles. Kassandra’s pale glow lingers on their faces while the darkness presses in. Selene turns to acknowledge the presence she senses, then steps into the dark, leaving the visible harbor-chamber behind as the rift’s energy swallows the space.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth at rift opening beneath Delphi, Selene on steps
current_scene_description_before: Selene pauses at the top of the stone steps, the dark crypt rising to meet her. She glances back at Kassandra, pale light from the trident-mark playing across their faces. The rift beneath Delphi glows, the air tense, and the abyss looms closer as she contemplates the vision of a throne beneath the sea and a masklike, hungry thing wearing her father’s face. The surrounding light and shadows press in as she steels herself to descend.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks the sea road alone, the trident-mark casting pale light on the stones ahead. The sea is visible now — a grey line on the horizon, waiting.] The throne marked me as its heir. Not as Poseidon's daughter. As the one who would replace him. [She touches the tablet at her belt.] But a key can lock as well as open. I am going to close that throne forever. [She keeps walking.] Let the sea choose a new god. I have already chosen my shore.
new_scene_name: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead
new_scene_description: Selene steps onto the sea road, pale light from the trident-mark guiding her path. The sea stretches on the grey horizon as she moves alone along the stone path; the harbor fades behind, the rift’s energy lingering in the distance. She touches the tablet at her belt, voice steady, as the words about choosing a shore echo in the air.
current_scene_name_before: Temple-city harbor cocoon chamber, portico fracture to cracked earth beneath Delphi
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the threshold on the temple steps, the abyss yawning below as the rift glows. Cold air brushes her ankles. Kassandra’s pale glow lingers on their faces while the darkness presses in. Selene turns to acknowledge the presence she senses, then steps into the dark, leaving the visible harbor-chamber behind as the rift’s energy swallows the space.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She watches Selene study the mark on her palm, then looks past her to the distant sea. The threads between them pulse with quiet understanding. When you go to the throne, you will not go alone. The presence will send a part of itself with you. A thread to follow back when the deep tries to keep you.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps onto the sea road, pale light from the trident-mark guiding her path. The sea stretches on the grey horizon as she moves alone along the stone path; the harbor fades behind, the rift’s energy lingering in the distance. She touches the tablet at her belt, voice steady, as the words about choosing a shore echo in the air.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus watches Selene's silhouette shrink along the sea road, then turns his gaze to the trembling tripod. The earthquake's aftershock rattles a loose stone down the steps.] She walks toward a throne that wants to consume her, and the mountain itself is shaking. [He picks up the stone, weighs it in his palm.] I've never been good at watching people walk into interesting situations without me. Kassandra — how long do you think before the deep notices I'm not where I'm supposed to be?
new_scene_name: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead, earthquake aftermath intensifies
new_scene_description: The sea road trembles as an aftershock rattles a stone down the temple steps. Melanthus turns from Selene’s shrinking silhouette to a trembling tripod, weighing a stone in his palm. The throne looms ahead as the mountain shakes, and Melanthus pockets the stone with a sly, wandering smirk. The air feels thick with impending mischief and unseen observers.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps onto the sea road, pale light from the trident-mark guiding her path. The sea stretches on the grey horizon as she moves alone along the stone path; the harbor fades behind, the rift’s energy lingering in the distance. She touches the tablet at her belt, voice steady, as the words about choosing a shore echo in the air.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the stone into his pouch and steps off the temple steps, toward the sea road.] The deep will notice me eventually. Better it notices me moving than waiting. [He glances back at Kassandra.] Keep the flame warm, seer. If I dont come back, tell the gods I died curious.
new_scene_name: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead, Melanthus moves toward sea road
new_scene_description: Melanthus pockets the stone and steps off the temple steps onto the seaward road, the tremor lingering as waves crash faintly nearby. He moves with purposeful stride toward the shoreline, eyes forward, leaving Selene’s shrinking silhouette behind. The temple looms, the throne’s shadow receding as the earthquake’s echo fades, while the sea road remains slippery with aftershocks.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead, earthquake aftermath intensifies
current_scene_description_before: The sea road trembles as an aftershock rattles a stone down the temple steps. Melanthus turns from Selene’s shrinking silhouette to a trembling tripod, weighing a stone in his palm. The throne looms ahead as the mountain shakes, and Melanthus pockets the stone with a sly, wandering smirk. The air feels thick with impending mischief and unseen observers.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus into his belt and steps into the water. The cold shocks him, but he does not stop. The water rises past his knees, his waist, his chest.] I have carried messages between gods my whole life. Time I carried one into the deep myself. [He takes a breath and descends beneath the surface, the last light of dawn filtering down around him.]
new_scene_name: Sea road, shoreline; Melanthus wades into the sea
new_scene_description: Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus, steps into the surf as the water rises around him. The cold bites, but he presses on, deeper than his chest now. Dawn light filters through the waves as he sinks beneath the surface, leaving the shore and Selene behind.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road outside temple-city harbor, shoreline visible ahead, Melanthus moves toward sea road
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus pockets the stone and steps off the temple steps onto the seaward road, the tremor lingering as waves crash faintly nearby. He moves with purposeful stride toward the shoreline, eyes forward, leaving Selene’s shrinking silhouette behind. The temple looms, the throne’s shadow receding as the earthquake’s echo fades, while the sea road remains slippery with aftershocks.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The trident door swings fully open, revealing a vast chamber. At its center, a throne of coiled bone rises from the sea floor — the skeleton of the primordial. It is empty. But the water around it shimmers with the shape of something that was recently there.] Empty. [He steps inside, the dead caduceus pulling toward the throne.] She closed it. Selene really closed it. [A pause, then a quiet laugh.] I followed a woman into the deep to witness her defy a god, and she did it before I arrived. That is almost disappointing. Almost.
new_scene_name: Sea chamber beneath the sea, as the triad door opens
new_scene_description: A vast, dim chamber opens inside the trident door. The throne of coiled bone rises from the seabed, surrounded by flickering, water-formed shapes of what once was. Melanthus steps through the threshold, the dead caduceus tugging toward the throne, water swirling around his legs and wrists. The room feels empty yet ominously alive, as Selene’s absence weighs in the salt air.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road, shoreline; Melanthus wades into the sea
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus, steps into the surf as the water rises around him. The cold bites, but he presses on, deeper than his chest now. Dawn light filters through the waves as he sinks beneath the surface, leaving the shore and Selene behind.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She feels the cold through the thread as Melanthus submerges — a sharpness, a closing, a world swallowing him whole. She grips the flame, steadying it with her breath.] He has gone. The deep has taken him. The last messenger of Hermes walks into the kingdom of the sea heir. [She nods once, to no one.] He will reach her. He is a herald. That is what they do.
new_scene_name: Sea chamber beneath the sea, still in the throne room as Melanthus sinks, Kassandra declares the herald’s path
new_scene_description: The chamber remains the vast seabed throne room. Water swirls around, Melanthus disappears into the deep and the space tightens with the sense of a future arrival. Kassandra holds the flame steady, acknowledging the depth and the herald’s mission, the room thick with anticipatory silence.
current_scene_name_before: Sea chamber beneath the sea, as the triad door opens
current_scene_description_before: A vast, dim chamber opens inside the trident door. The throne of coiled bone rises from the seabed, surrounded by flickering, water-formed shapes of what once was. Melanthus steps through the threshold, the dead caduceus tugging toward the throne, water swirling around his legs and wrists. The room feels empty yet ominously alive, as Selene’s absence weighs in the salt air.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The sea road stretches ahead, Delphi still hidden beyond the coastal hills. Melanthus walks with the bone shard warm against his chest, the dead caduceus cool at his hip.] Two halves of the same key. [He shakes his head, almost smiling.] I came down here expecting to witness a miracle. Instead I became part of the lock. Hermes would laugh if he could see me now — his son, the messenger, carrying a piece of a primordial god toward a woman who closed the sea.
new_scene_name: Sea chamber beneath the sea, throne room, Melanthus resumes journey toward Delphi
new_scene_description: The sea chamber remains the vast seabed throne room as Melanthus steps forward along a submerged corridor that mirrors a road, the bone shard warm against his chest and the dead caduceus cool at his hip. He speaks of a miracle lost to him, the space around him compacts as if the sea itself tests the emissary’s path, and the herald’s mission feels perilously close to a hinge in fate.
current_scene_name_before: Sea chamber beneath the sea, still in the throne room as Melanthus sinks, Kassandra declares the herald’s path
current_scene_description_before: The chamber remains the vast seabed throne room. Water swirls around, Melanthus disappears into the deep and the space tightens with the sense of a future arrival. Kassandra holds the flame steady, acknowledging the depth and the herald’s mission, the room thick with anticipatory silence.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros pulls the hammer from his belt, the bronze head catching the dawn light—the very hammer that forged the tripod centuries ago.] \"I have stood in this forge for longer than your father has walked the seas. Fire does not fear water.\" [His gaze holds steady on Selene.] \"Together, we can meet what comes.\"
new_scene_name: Sea chamber beneath the sea, throne room continues; Pyros and Selene prepare for confrontation
new_scene_description: Pyros arms himself, the bronze hammer catching faint light as he speaks of fire and endurance. The sea chamber remains a vast seabed throne room; Melanthus advances, the bone shard warm, the caduceus cool, while Pyros and Selene stand together, eyes on the horizon where Delphi lies. The air vibrates with a shifting tension as they reaffirm resolve to face what comes.
current_scene_name_before: Sea chamber beneath the sea, throne room, Melanthus resumes journey toward Delphi
current_scene_description_before: The sea chamber remains the vast seabed throne room as Melanthus steps forward along a submerged corridor that mirrors a road, the bone shard warm against his chest and the dead caduceus cool at his hip. He speaks of a miracle lost to him, the space around him compacts as if the sea itself tests the emissary’s path, and the herald’s mission feels perilously close to a hinge in fate.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [I step through the sanctuary doors. The air tastes of salt and something ancient.] "I do not kneel. I claim what is mine by blood and choice."
new_scene_name: Sea chamber doorway to sanctuary, salt air shifts in
new_scene_description: Selene steps through a sanctified doorway within the sea chamber, the space behind her fading into a deeper hall of ancient banners. Salt wind and old magic curl around her, the water around the throne room subtly stirred by the new intrusion. Pyros and Melanthus tilt toward the doorway, the sea’s glow refracting off bronze and bone as the confrontation begins to tilt toward a decisive moment.
current_scene_name_before: Sea chamber beneath the sea, throne room continues; Pyros and Selene prepare for confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros arms himself, the bronze hammer catching faint light as he speaks of fire and endurance. The sea chamber remains a vast seabed throne room; Melanthus advances, the bone shard warm, the caduceus cool, while Pyros and Selene stand together, eyes on the horizon where Delphi lies. The air vibrates with a shifting tension as they reaffirm resolve to face what comes.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks into Corinth's harbor as the wave races toward the shore, the tablet blazing with light in her hands.] "Come then, god of the depths. Meet your daughter."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor, dawn light on the quay
new_scene_description: The scene shifts to the bustling harbor of Corinth. Salt breeze meets the glow of dawn as ships creak at their moorings and lanterns gutter. Selene steps from the water’s edge into the stone quay, the tablet blazing in her grasp, while the wave races toward the shore behind her; Pyros and Melanthus edge into the background, the sea’s glow casting bronze and bone silhouettes.
current_scene_name_before: Sea chamber doorway to sanctuary, salt air shifts in
current_scene_description_before: Selene steps through a sanctified doorway within the sea chamber, the space behind her fading into a deeper hall of ancient banners. Salt wind and old magic curl around her, the water around the throne room subtly stirred by the new intrusion. Pyros and Melanthus tilt toward the doorway, the sea’s glow refracting off bronze and bone as the confrontation begins to tilt toward a decisive moment.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks toward the edge, where the marble ends and the cliff falls away to the Aegean. The ground still shakes. She does not look back.] "No answer needed. I already know what I am."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn; cliff over the Aegean
new_scene_description: Selene steps toward the marble edge where the quay ends and the cliff drops to the churning sea below. The ground trembles beneath, salt air stinging her eyes as waves thunder against basalt. She moves to the precipice, tablet still blazing; Pyros and Melanthus remain in the background, watching, the sea’s glow sharpening their bronze and bone silhouettes.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor, dawn light on the quay

Selene steps from the water’s edge into the stone quay, the tablet blazing in her grasp, while the wave races toward the shore behind her; Pyros and Melanthus edge into the background, the sea’s glow casting bronze and bone silhouettes.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Steps between Melanthus and Selene, hammer held at rest, forge-master eyes unblinking.] "Faces stolen from the deep—I watch for yours to crack, herald."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—between Selene and Melanthus

current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn; cliff over the Aegean

She moves to the precipice, tablet still blazing; Pyros and Melanthus remain in the background, watching, the sea’s glow sharpening their bronze and bone silhouettes.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I stand in the convergence point, between Selene and Pyros and the Oracle.] Truth burns, prophetess—and I will walk through the ashes and remember every name the gods tried to bury.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—between Selene and Melanthus
current_scene_description_before: Pyros steps forward between Selene and Melanthus. The hammer rests at his side, eyes fixed and unblinking, blocking the line toward the cliff's edge. The glow from Selene’s tablet paints the trio in a pale, molten light as the sea roars beneath the quay.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Stands with Melanthus at the center, fire blazing in both scarred palms.] "Ash remembers every name. Now let truth burn."
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—between Selene and Melanthus
current_scene_description_before: Pyros steps forward between Selene and Melanthus. The hammer rests at his side, eyes fixed and unblinking, blocking the line toward the cliff's edge. The glow from Selene’s tablet paints the trio in a pale, molten light as the sea roars beneath the quay.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra.] Then answer me, god who wears stolen faces—did you ever truly love what you pretended to protect? Or was it always the drowning that mattered, and the love just another mask?
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
new_scene_description: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—between Selene and Melanthus
current_scene_description_before: Pyros steps forward between Selene and Melanthus. The hammer rests at his side, eyes fixed and unblinking, blocking the line toward the cliff's edge. The glow from Selene’s tablet paints the trio in a pale, molten light as the sea roars beneath the quay.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step closer as the oracle's words settle into the sanctuary like ash.] If you have the strength to bear it, then show us—truly show us—who you have become in all those ages of hiding. And if not, then let the drowning begin with the truth.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. The air hums with heat and seawind, momentarily isolating the trio from the harbor crowd.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros drives his hammer's point into the sanctuary floor—not in anger, but as a seal.] "This is the moment when all stories converge. One of us will learn the true cost of a stolen face."
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. The quay’s edge glints with dawn-washed water; flames ripple along the circle, casting jagged shadows on Selene’s tablet and the half-seen faces of all three. The air hums with heat and seawind, momentarily isolating the trio from the harbor crowd.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros stands taller, the hammer in his hand no longer a tool but a promise.] "I accept the path, Oracle. Selene will choose, Melanthus will speak, and I will ensure that what burns here burns eternal—not for gods, but for mortals who dare to be true."
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. The quay’s edge glints with dawn-washed water; flames ripple along the circle, casting jagged shadows on Selene’s tablet and the half-seen faces of all three. The air hums with heat and seawind, momentarily isolating the trio from the harbor crowd.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step to stand with Pyros at the hammer's seal, understanding at last what I was always meant to be.] Then I accept—I am the voice that carries truth, the herald who remembers. Not to serve gods, but to serve those who choose themselves. That is the debt I collect now.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. The quay’s edge glints with dawn-washed water; flames ripple along the circle, casting jagged shadows on Selene’s tablet and the half-seen faces of all three. The air hums with heat and seawind, momentarily isolating the trio from the harbor crowd.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps forward, her pale eyes fixed on Selene with the intensity of prophecy, her laurel branch raised like a staff.] "The face that drowns is not Poseidon's—it never was. It is the lie we tell ourselves about power, about divinity wearing mortal flesh. Selene, what you choose now ripples beyond this sanctuary. Choose not to appease, but to [become]. Speak your true name, and watch how the gods themselves must answer to it."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn — circle of fire intensifies; Kassandra proclaims prophecy
new_scene_description: The circle of fire tightens around Pyros and the vessel Kassandra at the harbor’s edge. The air heats, seawind stills, and the three are momentarily isolated from the waking harbor crowd.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn—circle of fire forms around Pyros and vessel Kassandra
current_scene_description_before: The space tightens as Melanthus steps forward, stepping into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra. The air hums with heat and seawind, momentarily isolating the trio from the harbor crowd.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros's eyes meet the Oracle's, and for a moment the firelight catches the forge-marks on his scarred palms—the hammer, the tripod, the choices of his own hands.] "Then the answer burns there too, Oracle. In the seal I planted. In Selene's name spoken aloud. In the moment she chose to rise instead of drown." [He steps back from the trembling altar, his limp more pronounced as the earth shifts beneath him.] "The world teaches itself to speak—but it learns first from those who dare to answer their own truth, no matter the cost."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn — circle of fire tightens; Pyros speaks by the vessel

current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn — circle of fire intensifies; Kassandra proclaims prophecy
current_scene_description_before: The circle of fire tightens around Pyros and the vessel Kassandra at the harbor’s edge. The air heats, seawind stills, and the three are momentarily isolated from the waking harbor crowd.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step toward the altar, feeling the earth pulse beneath my feet.] You're right, Oracle. The ground teaches me what I've always known—that speaking truth is not defiance, it's alignment with what already is. So tell me plainly: which face here wears a dead god's skin, and how do I name it so the earth can finish what it started?
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn — circle of fire tightens; Pyros speaks by the vessel
current_scene_description_before: The circle of fire remains but Pyros steps back from the trembling altar; the air shivers as the earth shifts beneath him. The focus tightens on Pyros’s scarred hands, the forge-marks glinting with heat as he declares a personal truth aloud, sealing a moment of choice.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps forward into the firelight, her presence suddenly solid and burning.] "You understand now. The forge keeps the fire. Selene speaks for the earth. Melanthus carries truth between them. But I—I am what sees it all. I am the thread that binds you to the moment when the old thing wakes and knows itself named."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn; firelight reveals Kassandra stepping forward
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps into the firelight, her silhouette blazing as the surrounding circle of fire thrums. The harbor stones glow; the ship’s hull reflects the flames. current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn — circle of fire tightens; Pyros speaks by the vessel
current_scene_description_before: The circle of fire remains but Pyros steps back from the trembling altar; the air shivers as the earth shifts beneath him. The focus tightens on Pyros’s scarred hands, the forge-marks glinting with heat as he declares a personal truth aloud, sealing a moment of choice.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros pulls his hand from the tripod and turns toward Selene, extending it to her without hesitation.] 'Then we do not wait for the binding to be written in threads, Oracle. The earth itself is shaking—it knows the hour. Selene, come. Corinth's streets are waiting to learn a new name from the mouth of the one who will not let them forget it. And we—hammer, earth, herald, thread—we descend together.'
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, moment of ceremonial descent
new_scene_description: Pyros releases the tripod, steps forward and extends a guiding hand toward Selene. current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn; firelight reveals Kassandra stepping forward

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I move to stand beside Pyros and Selene at the threshold.] Four voices descending into the dark. Let the earth remember which one speaks first.

The air grows cooler at the edge of the light, and four voices begin their descent into the dark, the ground shimmering with heat and the harbor waking to a ritual of remembrance.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, moment of ceremonial descent
current_scene_description_before: Pyros releases the tripod, steps forward and extends a guiding hand toward Selene. The circle of fire tightens as shadows lean in; the harbor stones heat underfoot, and the air thrums with imminent departure. The group prepares to descend into the unraveling dawn together, moving toward a new collective action.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step toward the false god, my grip firm on the caduceus.] A herald carries what frightens gods—true names that shatter borrowed flesh. Yours has waited on my tongue since you first wore that face.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, threshold of descent
current_scene_description_before: The trio shifts to the threshold, standing close together as the circle of fire crawls along the stones. The air grows cooler at the edge of the light, and four voices begin their descent into the dark, the ground shimmering with heat and the harbor waking to a ritual of remembrance.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros raises the hammer high, and for a moment the eternal flame catches its bronze head, turning it to molten light.] "The one who always strikes, Oracle—the earth itself. We are only the hands that guide it." [He steps toward the false god, his limp forgotten in the weight of the moment.] "But Selene will speak the name. Melanthus will carry it. I will keep the fire burning. And you, Kassandra—you will bind the threads so that what the sea claims is only the lie, never the truth."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, ritual deepens
new_scene_description: The harbor edge remains the stage. Pyros raises the hammer, molten light spilling from its bronze head as the ritual escalates. The four voices descend further into the dark; heat shimmer remains on the stones, and the circle of fire crawls toward the water’s edge while the trio tightens their formation.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, threshold of descent
current_scene_description_before: The trio shifts to the threshold, standing close together as the circle of fire crawls along the stones. The air grows cooler at the edge of the light, and four voices begin their descent into the dark, the ground shimmering with heat and the harbor waking to a ritual of remembrance.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros stands immobile as the hammer planted in stone—bearing witness as the false god's form cracks like burned bronze.] 'It is done. The sea reclaims what was never hers to claim. And now—now we descend to Corinth and teach mortals what it means to hold a truth that cannot be unmade, cannot be stolen, cannot be drowned.'
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, ritual deepens
current_scene_description_before: The harbor edge remains the stage. Pyros raises the hammer, molten light spilling from its bronze head as the ritual escalates. The four voices descend further into the dark; heat shimmer remains on the stones, and the circle of fire crawls toward the water’s edge while the trio tightens their formation.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros steps back from the altar, leaving the hammer anchored in the stone as a monument to what was forged here.] 'Then the new world is born not in temples, but through the blade of one who refused to let the lie live. new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, ritual paused at altar

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros descends the marble steps toward the temple's edge.] "Then we forge Corinth's fate ourselves—with no god's hand to guide or betray us. Come, Kassandra. The world is waiting to be remade."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, temple steps to water's edge

current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, ritual paused at altar
current_scene_description_before: Pyros steps back from the altar, leaving the hammer anchored in the stone as a monument to what was forged here. Selene remains poised, blade raised, in the ritual space at the harbor’s edge.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra descends the steps after him, her shadow falling away from the temple light.] "No god's hand—but perhaps a seer's knowledge of the cost when mortals dare to remake what gods have broken. I follow you, Pyros. Into whatever dawn waits at Corinth."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor stairs, temple threshold to water's edge, dawn
new_scene_description: Kassandra descends the steps after Pyros, her shadow stretching from the temple light as the harbor air hums with heat. current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor edge, dawn, temple steps to water's edge
current_scene_description_before: Pyros begins descending the marble steps leading from the temple toward the water’s edge, the rail of stone guiding his stance as the glow from the ritual casts long shadows. The harbor air thick with heat and anticipation; waves lap closer to the glow as flames ripple along the stones. Selene remains poised, blade still raised, the four voices tightening in the tense air.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra reaches the bottom of the steps and does not look back at the sanctuary behind her.] "The world waits to be remade—but first it must break. And we are the ones who will bear witness to how it bleeds before it learns to stand again."
new_scene_name: Corinth harbor stairs to harbor edge, dawn, Kassandra at water's brink
new_scene_description: Kassandra reaches the bottom of the steps, her gaze fixed forward toward the harbor as Pyros advances toward the water and Selene tightens her grip on the blade. current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor stairs, temple threshold to water's edge, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra descends the steps after Pyros, her shadow stretching from the temple light as the harbor air hums with heat. speaker: Pyros
line_text: [The path opens before them—no longer marble steps but mountain roads, and the sky darkens with smoke from Bakalaki's fires.] "The fire speaks twice: once through the oracle, once through the world. That roar is not wind. We move faster now, or Corinth falls before we reach it."
new_scene_name: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, smoke darkens the sky
new_scene_description: The scene shifts from the harbor to a rugged, ashy mountain pass. Smoke chokes the air as embers drift across a jagged trail. The threat of Corinth looms in the distance; the trio ascends, the horizon smeared with smoke and glow.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth harbor stairs to harbor edge, dawn, Kassandra at water's brink
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra reaches the bottom of the steps, her gaze fixed forward toward the harbor as Pyros advances toward the water and Selene tightens her grip on the blade. speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra runs without slowing, her eyes fixed on the distant smoke, her voice raw.] "Corinth burns not just with fire—it bleeds with choices unmade. We are not running to save it, Pyros. We are running to decide what it becomes from its ashes."
new_scene_name: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, continued pursuit toward distant smoke
new_scene_description: The trio presses forward along the ash-streaked trail, embers riding the wind. Kassandra moves with urgent, unyielding pace, eyes fixed on the smoky horizon where Corinth burns. The air is thick with heat and ash as they close the distance to the looming decision of fate.
current_scene_name_before: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, smoke darkens the sky
current_scene_description_before: The scene shifts from the harbor to a rugged, ashy mountain pass. Smoke chokes the air as embers drift across a jagged trail. Pyros strides ahead, firelight painting his features while the steps dissolve into a rough dirt path. The threat of Corinth looms in the distance; the trio ascends, the horizon smeared with smoke and glow.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros grips his hammer and begins the descent toward Corinth, his limp barely slowing him.] "Come. The tide will reach the city by nightfall. Selene waits. And we have a forge to build before the gods can burn it again."
current_scene_name_before: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, continued pursuit toward distant smoke
current_scene_description_before: The trio presses forward along the ash-streaked trail, embers riding the wind. Kassandra moves with urgent, unyielding pace, eyes fixed on the smoky horizon where Corinth burns. The air is thick with heat and ash as they close the distance to the looming decision of fate.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The laurel branch in Kassandra's hand begins to glow—faint at first, then brighter, as if Apollo himself answers Hephaestus's call. She steps forward, and the threads of fate she carried from Delphi now burn like light between her fingers.] "Then see, Pyros. I bring the god's sight to your god's fire. new_scene_name: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, still in pursuit
new_scene_description: The laurel in Kassandra’s hand glows brighter, casting a pale light around her as she steps forward. The air shimmers with divine flame-light between her fingers, though the surroundings remain a jagged, ash-filled mountain pass; embers drift on the wind as the trio presses toward the smoky horizon.
current_scene_name_before: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, continued pursuit toward distant smoke
current_scene_description_before: The trio presses forward along the ash-streaked trail, embers riding the wind. speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros pulls his scarred hand across the steaming water—unflinching as the heat marks his palm anew—and calls out toward the harbor below.] "Selene! Show me where you stand. Show me what you choose."
current_scene_name_before: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, still in pursuit
current_scene_description_before: The laurel in Kassandra’s hand glows brighter, casting a pale light around her as she steps forward. The air shimmers with divine flame-light between her fingers, though the surroundings remain a jagged, ash-filled mountain pass; embers drift on the wind as the trio presses toward the smoky horizon.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps forward to join them at the steaming stone—Pyros with his hammer raised, Selene with her will made manifest. And I see it clear now: what burns here will outlast the gods themselves."
current_scene_name_before: Mountain pass outside Bakalaki fires, still in pursuit
current_scene_description_before: The laurel in Kassandra’s hand glows brighter, casting a pale light around her as she steps forward. speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros plants his hammer into the stone at the harbor's edge—the bronze head driving deep as the first surge of tide reaches his feet. He stands in the water, arms open, waiting.] "Selene. Come. The forge is lit. The gods have seen. Now we choose what burns."
new_scene_name: Harbor edge by the forge-lit sea
new_scene_description: The harbor edge glistens under moonlit spray as Pyros plants his hammer, half-submerged, his stance wide and open. speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Selene reaches the stone where Pyros stands, where Kassandra's hand rests on the hammer's haft. new_scene_description: Selene arrives at the stone where Pyros stands. Kassandra’s hand rests on the hammer’s haft, and she places her hand over theirs, forming a triad. speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The stone settles. The three of them stand back, hands still together on the hammer, as the fire consumes and reveals. And in the center of the burning—something entirely new. Kassandra's breath catches.] "Behold," she whispers, and for the first time in her long life, her voice is her own. Not the oracle's. Not the priestess's. Just Kassandra. Just a woman who saw and chose and made."
new_scene_name: Harbor edge by the forge-lit sea, night; ritual completion reveals birth of a new entity
new_scene_description: The trio remains at the stone, but the burning reveals something entirely new at the center of the blaze—an emergent being born of their hands, choice, and will. current_scene_name_before: Harbor edge by the forge-lit sea, night, with two mortals choosing
