The grove’s shadows tighten; the spring glints unseen beyond the trees, and Pyros’s presence lingers at the grove’s boundary as they speak in hushed, deliberate tones.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text:
[Melanthus watches her place her bare foot on the soil, and something shifts in his posture — the stillness of a man who has just seen a move he did not anticipate.] You have already decided what you will cost it. [He lets that sit, then moves to stand at the edge of the marble beside her, not touching the earth, but close enough that his shadow falls across it.] I have spent my life making people pay for information. I know the price of every secret I have ever sold. But I have never once decided what I would cost something that wanted to devour the world.

[He looks down at his empty belt, then back at her.] Theron said the deep feels the weight of what we are. Not what we say, not what we show — what we are. [He almost smiles, but it does not reach his eyes.] That is a problem for a herald. My whole life has been a performance of weights. I have been heavier than I am, lighter than I am, sharper, duller — whatever the negotiation required.

[He steps onto the soil beside her, barefoot, the dawn cool against his sole.] But I have never tried being exactly what I am. Not because I am afraid of what the thing will read — but because I have never stopped performing long enough to find out what is underneath.
new_scene_name: Eastern grove edge near Delphi, marble terrace, dawn
new_scene_description: The marble terrace glows faintly in the dawn light. Melanthus steps onto the soil beside Kassandra, their shadows merging as the earth cools. The grove remains close around them, water unseen but audible as a distant, constant murmur. Pyros is still near the grove’s boundary, felt more than seen. Theron and Melanthus’ exchanged weights hang in the air, now crystallized by the new proximity and calm, the moment poised for a pivotal choice.
current_scene_name_before: Eastern grove edge near Delphi, clearing of the spring
current_scene_description_before: The grove tightens around a small marble terrace where the earth meets stone. Kassandra rises with decisive gravity, her bare foot touching the soil as a hum of distant water and unseen spring lingers. Thunder-tinged air coats the vision; Pyros remains at the grove’s boundary, unseen but palpably present. Melanthus and Theron remain with fading footprints, their exchange now charged by Kassandra’s revelation and resolve.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [His voice does not come from the treeline. It comes from the stone itself — from the marble step beneath their feet, vibrating up through the grain of the mountain like a struck bell fading into speech.] I heard every word. The stone carries your voices down to where I am standing. [A pause, and when he speaks again, the vibration is deeper, as if he has pressed his palm to the wall of the crack and is speaking into it.] Kassandra. You asked what you weigh without the masks. I have been standing in the dark for what feels like a lifetime, holding the only metal that has never been named, and I have learned something the deep did not want me to learn. [Another pause. The stone hums.] The thing does not feel your weight through the earth. It feels the absence you leave when you stop pretending. It felt Melanthus step onto the soil and it did not recognize him — because he has never been exactly himself in his entire life. That silence you are making? It is not a weapon. It is a vacuum. And the deep has never tasted a vacuum before. It is starving for the shape of you. [His voice shifts — not louder, but closer, as if he has found a thinner place in the stone.] Do not let it starve too long. A starving thing does not dig. It waits for you to come to it. And I am already here.
new_scene_name: Marble terrace, dawn, voice from stone beneath their feet
new_scene_description: The marble steps beneath them hum and glow with vibration. Pyros speaks through the stone, the grove’s murmur and distant water grounding the strange, earthen voice. The air trembles with the weight of his message, and the terrace holds a breath as Kassandra and Melanthus listen, the grove tightening around them in expectancy.
current_scene_name_before: Eastern grove edge near Delphi, marble terrace, dawn
current_scene_description_before: The marble terrace glows faintly in the dawn light. Melanthus steps onto the soil beside Kassandra, their shadows merging as the earth cools. The grove remains close around them, water unseen but audible as a distant, constant murmur. Pyros is still near the grove’s boundary, felt more than seen. Theron and Melanthus’ exchanged weights hang in the air, now crystallized by the new proximity and calm, the moment poised for a pivotal choice.

speaker: Theron
line_text:
[Theron rises from the step. Not quickly — the way a man rises when he has heard a summons he was waiting for. He walks to the edge of the marble where Kassandra and Melanthus stand barefoot on the soil, and he does not step onto the earth. He lowers himself to one knee and presses his palm flat against the stone at the edge of the mountain.]

Pyros. [His voice is not loud, but it is aimed — directed down into the marble the way he used to direct prayers into the sea floor, letting the vibration carry.] You said the thing feels absence, not weight. That is true. But you are down there holding the only metal that has never been named, and I am up here holding a socket that has been emptied of its window.

[He presses harder, his palm white against the stone. His eye closes. When he speaks, his voice is quieter — meant only for the stone to carry.]

A vacuum needs two openings. You are at the bottom, holding the black bar, digging toward the name. I am at the top, carrying the hollow where the thing used to see through me. If you need a door to open down there — a way through that the thing cannot feel — then use the absence I am making up here. My hollow socket is not a wound anymore. It is a passage. And I am pressing it into the stone so you can feel where it leads.

[He opens his eye, but does not lift his hand from the marble. His voice steadies.]

The thing does not know what to do with a vacuum. But I was a priest of the deep. I know exactly what to do with one. I will stand here, pressing my absence into the earth, for as long as you need. When you find the crack that leads to the name — follow the hollow, not the light. The hollow is mine, and I am aiming it at the heart of the dark.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace, dawn, voice from stone beneath their feet
current_scene_description_before: The marble steps beneath them hum and glow with vibration. Pyros speaks through the stone, the grove’s murmur and distant water grounding the strange, earthen voice. The air trembles with the weight of his message, and the terrace holds a breath as Kassandra and Melanthus listen, the grove tightening around them in expectancy.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Silence. Long enough that the mountain seems to hold its breath. Then his voice returns, but it is different — not vibrating through the stone, but coming from further down, as if he has descended past the place where sound carries.] Kassandra. I felt it pass me. [A pause.] The hollow. It did not feel like Theron's absence. It felt like something that has been tunneling toward this moment from the other side — and it just found the last handspan of stone between us. [His voice drops, not in volume but in depth, as if the weight above him has compressed it.] I am standing in a place where the walls stop being stone. They are not warm. They are not cold. They are the temperature of a body that has been dead so long it has forgotten it was ever alive. And the black bar in my hands is pulling toward the dark ahead of me — not resisting, not warning. Pulling. Like it recognizes what is in front of it. [Another long pause. When he speaks again, his voice is quieter, but it carries the flat certainty of a man who has stopped guessing.] You said the dark has been hollowing itself out to match Theron. But I think the hollow was never Theron's. I think it was always meant to be mine. The word on my palm — STILLBORN — was not a curse. It was a mold. The thing was making a space for me before I was born, shaping the absence I would leave so it could fill it. And I just walked into that space carrying the one metal it never expected me to bring. [The stone around them trembles — not an earthquake, but the shudder of something far below adjusting its grip.] I am going deeper. If the hollow reaches past me, do not follow it with your sight, Kassandra. Follow it with your voice. Tell me what you see on the other side of whatever I am about to walk through.
new_scene_name: Marble terrace, dawn, voice from stone beneath their feet — a descent begins
new_scene_description: The terrace trembles as Pyros’ voice shifts from the stone into a deeper, downward resonance. The air thickens; a distant, unseen descent hints that Pyros is stepping into a hollowed space below, while Kassandra and Melanthus stand in the vibrating marble, listening as the grove tightens its hold around them.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace, dawn, voice from stone beneath their feet
current_scene_description_before: The marble steps beneath them hum and glow with vibration. Pyros speaks through the stone, the grove’s murmur and distant water grounding the strange, earthen voice. The air trembles with the weight of his message, and the terrace holds a breath as Kassandra and Melanthus listen, the grove tightening around them in expectancy.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene has been silent through the exchange — standing at the edge of the marble where the earthquake first cracked the step, her bare feet planted on the split stone. She does not kneel. She does not press her palm to the earth. She stands, the dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip catching the dawn, and she speaks not into the stone but into the air above it — pitched for the mountain to carry, not for the people beside her.]
new_scene_name: Marble terrace edge, dawn, Selene speaks to the air above the crack
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the edge of the cracking terrace, her bare feet on split stone. The dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip catches the dawn as she speaks upward toward the mountain, not to the others beside her. The terrace continues to tremble beneath Pyros’ voice, with the ground opening further below in the hollowed space.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace, dawn, voice from stone beneath their feet — a descent begins
current_scene_description_before: The terrace trembles as Pyros’ voice shifts from the stone into a deeper, downward resonance. The air thickens; a distant, unseen descent hints that Pyros is stepping into a hollowed space below, while Kassandra and Melanthus stand in the vibrating marble, listening as the grove tightens its hold around them.

speaker: Theron
line_text:
[Theron does not lift his palm from the stone. He turns his head — slowly, the way a man turns when he is holding something in place and cannot afford to let go — and looks at Selene where she stands on the cracked marble. His one eye meets her, and there is something in it that has not been there until now: not the guilt of the man who walked away, not the weight of the priest who failed, but the quiet recognition of one person who has been waiting to see what the other would become.]

Selene. You have been silent through all of this. Through the naming, the giving, the descending. You have stood at the edge of the cracked step and let the rest of us empty ourselves into the mountain, and you have not once reached for what you carry.

[He looks at the dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip — the mark of her father, the god he served and fled.]

I served Poseidon for thirty years. I know the weight of being shaped by the deep. But I have never seen the deep stand still the way you are standing still right now. Not watching. Not waiting. [He holds her gaze.] Standing. Like a harbor wall that knows the wave is coming and has already decided it will not break.

[His voice drops, not loud, but certain — the voice of a man who has spent thirty years learning when to speak and when to let the silence carry.]

You said nothing while I pressed my absence into the stone. You said nothing while Pyros named the mold. You said nothing while Melanthus sent his message. But you are standing on the crack where the earthquake first split the marble, and you have not moved. That is not passivity. That is anchoring. You are holding the mountain in place by the weight of your feet alone.

[He nods, once — not the nod of a priest to a commander, but of one person who recognizes the shape of a choice in another.]

When the deep asks me who I witnessed beside, I will name you first. Not because you spoke. Because you stood exactly where you were needed and did not flinch.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge, dawn, Selene speaks to the air above the crack
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the edge of the cracking terrace, her bare feet on split stone. The dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip catches the dawn as she speaks upward toward the mountain, not to the others beside her. The terrace continues to tremble beneath Pyros’ voice, with the ground opening further below in the hollowed space.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [His voice through the stone is thinner now — stretched by distance, as if he is speaking through something that resists sound.] Melanthus. I heard you. [A pause.] You said the mold is broken and the metal is mine. I have been standing here trying to decide what to become. But I think I have already become it on the way down. I am still the smith. I am still the stillborn. But I am also the man who walked into the crack when every god who marked him expected him to stay in the shape they made. [His voice shifts — aimed upward, toward the cracked step where Selene stands.] Selene. You held the mountain closed with your feet. I walked into the dark it was hiding. When I come back — if I come back — I will need someone to show me what it looks like to stand on the crack and not fall through. [The vibration fades. His voice is barely a thread now.] I am past where the walls remember what they were. The bar is pulling toward a space that has been waiting for the right emptiness to arrive. I am stepping into it. If my voice does not find the stone again — do not wait. Open the passage. Send the hollow through.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge, dawn, Selene speaks to the air above the crack
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the edge of the cracking terrace, her bare feet on split stone. The dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip catches the dawn as she speaks upward toward the mountain, not to the others beside her. The terrace continues to tremble beneath Pyros’ voice, with the ground opening further below in the hollowed space.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text:
[Melanthus keeps his palms flat on the earth. His eyes are open now, fixed on the space between his hands as if the soil has become a page he is reading.] Pyros. You said you have already become it on the way down. [A pause. His voice is quiet, aimed into the earth.] Then you are not walking into the unknown. You are walking into the confirmation of what you already are. The bar pulling toward the dark is not leading you. It is following you — because the metal remembers the hand that forged it, and your hand is the only one that has ever held it without flinching.

[He presses his palms deeper into the soil, and his voice drops to the barest thread — intended only for the stone to carry to the space beyond sound.] Go. If your voice does not find the stone again, I will not send a message after you. I will send the hollow, as you asked. But I will also send this: the man who walked into the crack carrying the only metal the thing cannot read is not losing himself down there. He is finding the shape he was never given. [He lifts his hands from the earth, and the soil holds the print of his palms — two dark impressions in the dawn light.] When you come back, the first thing you will see is the sky. And it will look exactly the same as it did when you left. But you will not. And that is the only name that matters: the one you come back carrying.
new_scene_name: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn, scroll of earth imprint
new_scene_description: The terrace trembles under Pyros’ roar as Melanthus' hands press into the cracked earth, the soil dimly glowing with dawn. He speaks into the ground, the air between Selene and the crack charged with unspoken pact. The space remains the same physical terrace, but the action shifts to a ritual command and departure toward the crack.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge, dawn, Selene speaks to the air above the crack
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the edge of the cracking terrace, her bare feet on split stone. The dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip catches the dawn as she speaks upward toward the mountain, not to the others beside her. The terrace continues to tremble beneath Pyros’ voice, with the ground opening further below in the hollowed space.

speaker: Selene
line_text:
[Selene does not lift her feet from the crack. She does not shift her weight. But she tilts her chin — just enough that her voice carries down toward the marble, toward the stone, toward the place where Pyros's voice has gone thin.] You asked me to show you what it looks like to stand on the crack and not fall through. [A pause, and her voice is not loud, but it is the voice she used to use on the deck of her flagship when the sea was trying to decide whether to swallow them.] I will show you by staying here until you come back. That is the whole of it. There is no trick. No secret. You decide that the crack is where you belong, and then you do not leave. The mountain does the rest.

[She looks down at her feet — at the split marble beneath her soles, at the fine dust that has settled around her ankles like the tide line on a shore.] I have been standing on this crack since the first tremor. Not because I knew what it was. Because it felt like the deck of a ship that was still deciding whether to hold or break. And I have never once abandoned a vessel that still had a keel. [She lifts her head and speaks toward the stone, toward the space where his voice faded.] You are past where the walls remember what they were. But I am still here, and the crack is still beneath my feet. If the hollow reaches the other side before your voice does — I will feel it. And I will open the passage. Not because you asked me to. Because you walked into the dark carrying metal that does not know its own shape, and I will not let the thing that made the mold be the only thing that sees what you become on the other side. [Her voice steadies, dropping to the flat certainty of a woman who has given her last order.] Come back, Pyros. Or do not. But either way, I will be standing on this crack when the passage opens. And I will be ready to meet whatever comes through it.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn, scroll of earth imprint
current_scene_description_before: The terrace trembles under Pyros’ roar as Melanthus' hands press into the cracked earth, the soil dimly glowing with dawn. He speaks into the ground, the air between Selene and the crack charged with unspoken pact. The space remains the same physical terrace, but the action shifts to a ritual command and departure toward the crack.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [A long silence. When his voice returns, it is not through the stone — it is a single note, pure and directionless, as if the silence itself has learned to speak.] Theron. The hollow reached this place before I did. [A pause.] I stepped through the last span of darkness and found it already here — waiting, open, aimed at something I cannot see but can feel. A door that was not a door until the hollow showed me where it was. [His voice steadies, finding the stone again, but it sounds different — as if he stands in a larger space now, one that gives his words room to gather before they are sent.] You said the passage was never yours. That it belonged to Selene. But I am standing on the other side of it now, and I think the truth is simpler than any of us expected. The passage belongs to whoever is willing to stand at the threshold and not look back. You stood at yours for thirty years and did not step through. I walked into mine without knowing what was on the other side. Selene is standing at hers now, barefoot on the crack, calling it a keel. [The note deepens, gaining texture, as if he has turned to face the door ahead.] The hollow did not lead me here to find a name. It led me here to find the threshold that was waiting for the right person to arrive. And I am here. The door is open. But I will not step through it until Selene tells me the crack beneath her feet is ready to become a gate. [His voice drops, aimed upward through the mountain.] Selene. Is your keel steady?
new_scene_name: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — threshold dialogue
new_scene_description: The terrace remains the same physical terrace, but the dialogue shifts to Theron and Pyros converging on a doorway-like opening in the crack, with Selene at the keel. The space feels larger, the air charged with a gate opening, Theron addressing Selene’s readiness as the threshold is acknowledged. The moment centers on a potential departure through the crack if Selene approves.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn, scroll of earth imprint
current_scene_description_before: The terrace trembles under Pyros’ roar as Melanthus' hands press into the cracked earth, the soil dimly glowing with dawn. He speaks into the ground, the air between Selene and the crack charged with unspoken pact. The space remains the same physical terrace, but the action shifts to a ritual command and departure toward the crack.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text:
[Melanthus rises from the earth, brushing the soil from his palms. He does not step back onto the marble. He stays barefoot on the ground, a handspan from the crack where Selene stands, and he speaks toward the stone — not loud, but carrying the clarity of a man who has spent his life reading doors.] Pyros. You asked if her keel is steady. [He looks at Selene, then down at the earth between his feet.] I have been watching her since the first tremor. She has not shifted her weight once. Not when the earthquake cracked the step. Not when you named the mold. Not when the hollow passed through.

[He looks at the empty space above the stone where Pyros's voice last was.] That is your answer. The threshold is ready. It has been ready since she set her feet on the crack and called it a keel. [A pause, and his voice drops, not in volume but in weight.] You asked if she would open the passage. She already did. She opened it the moment she decided to stand there and not move. The rest is just you walking through what she has been holding open since dawn.

[He steps back, giving the space between Selene and the stone back to her, to the mountain, to the door below.] Go. I will mark this moment — not in silver, not in stone — in the air between us, so that when the story is told, they will say: the stillborn found a threshold, and the sailor held it open with nothing but her feet. That is enough. That has always been enough.
new_scene_name: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — doorway threshold moment clarified
new_scene_description: The marble terrace remains, but Melanthus rises barefoot near the crack, speaking softly toward the stone. Selene stands at the doorway-like opening, the air dense with decision. Theron and Pyros observe as Melanthus declares the threshold ready and hands control to Selene to walk through, the moment suspended above the crack and the dawn.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — threshold dialogue
current_scene_description_before: The terrace remains the same physical terrace, but the dialogue shifts to Theron and Pyros converging on a doorway-like opening in the crack, with Selene at the keel. The space feels larger, the air charged with a gate opening, Theron addressing Selene’s readiness as the threshold is acknowledged. The moment centers on a potential departure through the crack if Selene approves.

speaker: Theron
line_text:
[Theron lets out a breath — not a sigh, but the release of a man who has just had the last piece of a puzzle he did not know he was solving click into place. He shakes his head slowly, and there is something in his one eye that looks almost like wonder.]

A keel so the rest of us would not panic. [He repeats it, tasting the shape of it. Then he looks at her, and the corner of his mouth moves.] I have been calling you an anchor. An anchor holds a ship in place. A gate opens to let it through. I spent thirty years serving the deep and I still could not tell the difference between a thing that holds and a thing that opens.

[He rises to his feet, takes up his staff, and plants it on the marble beside the crack. He does not step onto the soil. He stands at the edge of the gate, beside her, facing the same direction she is facing.]

You called it a keel so we would not panic. I called it an anchor because I did not know how to name a woman who was standing on a door. [He looks at her, and his voice is quiet, steady.] I am learning. You are standing on the other side of the crack, telling me you will meet whatever comes through. I have never seen anyone call a gate a keel and mean it as a promise. But I have seen you do it. And I will remember it. When the story is told, I will say: she called it a keel so we would not know we were standing on a threshold. But she was already through it before any of us had the courage to ask what was on the other side.
new_scene_name: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — doorway threshold moment clarified
new_scene_description: The balcony-like marble terrace remains. Theron steps forward, at the edge near the crack, his staff planted beside the opening. The air crackles with decision as Selene stands at the doorway-like threshold, both eyes fixed on what lies beyond. Theron speaks of anchors, keels, and gates; Melanthus's declaration lingers in the light of dawn, the crack widening as a new fate seems poised to pass through.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — doorway threshold moment clarified
current_scene_description_before: The marble terrace remains, but Melanthus rises barefoot near the crack, speaking softly toward the stone. speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene does not look away from the crack when she speaks. Her voice is aimed at the space where his voice last was — where the door closed behind him.] I hear you, Pyros. The gate held. The keel did not break. [A pause, and her voice drops, not in volume but in the weight of a woman who has given orders to men who were walking into places they would not return from.] You are not alone in that shape. You were never meant to be. The thing made the mold for one body. But you walked in carrying metal it cannot read, and the door closed behind you, and that means whatever it shows you — you will see it through a metal that was never named by anything that wanted to own you. [She shifts her weight for the first time since the earthquake — not lifting her feet, but settling them deeper, as if she is rooting into the stone.] You told me to listen for a voice that is not yours when the mountain speaks. I will. But I will also speak back. Not through the hollow. Not through the passage. Through the water. The mountain is full of springs. My father's domain is the pressure that moves through stone. [She rests her hand on the dolphin-shaped dagger at her hip, and her voice steadies to the flat certainty of a woman who has just chosen her next heading.] When the thing speaks to you, Pyros — listen to what it says. But watch the metal. And if the metal shows you something the voice does not say, press it into the wall. I will feel it through the springs. And I will answer through the only crack the thing did not dig: the seam between the sea and the stone, where my father has been pressing for longer than the mountain has been standing.
current_scene_name_before: Marble terrace edge outside the crack, dawn — doorway threshold moment clarified
